subject: english
title: Family Song
concept: Celebrating family through song.
explanation: Let's sing a fun song about our family! 🎶
text:
Mother, mother
Will you play with me?
Yes, I shall, yes, I shall
We are a family!

Father, father
Will you eat with me?
Yes, I shall, yes, I shall
We are a family!

Brother, sister
Will you dance with me?
Yes, I shall, yes, I shall
We are a family!

Grandmother, grandmother,
Will you sing with me?
Yes, I shall, yes, I shall
We are a family!

Grandfather, grandfather,
Will you read with me?
Yes, I shall, yes, I shall
We are a family!

subject: english
title: The Sparrow Family Story
concept: Understanding family dynamics through a story.
explanation: Let's read a lovely story about a sparrow family and their adventures! 🐦
text:
Mama and Papa sparrow were making a nest. They found a safe place. The little nest was soon ready. It was soft and warm.

Grandmother and Grandfather sparrow, Aunt and Uncle sparrow, cousins and friends all came to meet the baby sparrows. Mama sparrow laid three small eggs. One day the eggs broke open. Three little heads popped out. Papa sparrow brought food for them.

The baby sparrows grew bigger and bigger. One day, the baby sparrows hopped out of the nest. They flew up into the big blue sky.

subject: english
title: Understanding Family Members

subject: english
title: Five Little Monkeys Jumping on a Tree
concept: A fun rhyme about monkeys and counting.
explanation: Let's sing a fun rhyme about monkeys jumping on a tree! 🎶
text:
Five little monkeys jumping on a tree,
One fell down and bumped his knee.
Ah! Ah! Ah!
Four little monkeys jumping on a tree,
One fell down and bumped his knee.
Ah! Ah! Ah!
Three little monkeys jumping on a tree,
One fell down and bumped his knee.
Ah! Ah! Ah!
Two little monkeys jumping on a tree,
One fell down and bumped his knee.
Ah! Ah! Ah!
One little monkey jumping on a tree,
One fell down and bumped his knee.
Ah! Ah! Ah!
Now, no more monkeys jumping on a tree,
Each of them is holding his knee!

subject: english
title: The Cap-seller and the Monkeys
concept: A story about a clever cap-seller and mischievous monkeys.
explanation: Let's read a fun story about a cap-seller and some cheeky monkeys! 🐒
text:
Once there was a man who sold caps. He carried many caps in a basket on his head. He was a cap-seller.
One day, he slept under a tree. There were monkeys on the tree. They came down from the tree. They took away all the caps.
The cap-seller woke up. He saw the basket was empty! He looked around and above. The monkeys were wearing his caps.
The cap-seller shook his fists at the monkeys. They also did the same. The cap-seller scratched his head. The monkeys did the same.
The cap-seller had an idea! He took off his cap and threw it into the empty basket. The monkeys also did the same. The cap-seller got all his caps back and went away happily.

subject: english
title: My Dear Grandpa Had a Farm

full_text:
My dear grandpa had a farm
E-I-E-I-O
And on that farm,
He had a cow
E-I-E-I-O
With a moo-moo here
And a moo-moo there
Here a moo, there a moo
Everywhere a moo-moo
My dear grandpa had a farm
E-I-E-I-O

subject: english
title: Understanding Holistic Development
concept: Holistic development nurtures all aspects of a child's growth.
explanation: Holistic development means helping children grow in every way! This includes their feelings, thinking, and even how they play. 🌱 For example, when you play with friends, you learn to share and be kind! 🤗

practice_questions.q: What does holistic development mean?
practice_questions.a: It means helping children grow in all areas like feelings, thinking, and play.

subject: english
title: What is the Foundational Stage?
concept: The Foundational Stage is crucial for early learning.
explanation: The Foundational Stage is like the first steps in a fun adventure of learning! 🎒 It helps kids from ages 3 to 8 learn important things that they will use forever! 🌈

practice_questions.q: Why is the Foundational Stage important?
practice_questions.a: It helps kids learn important skills they will use for life!

subject: english
title: What is the National Curriculum Framework?
concept: The NCF guides how children should learn.
explanation: The National Curriculum Framework is like a treasure map for teachers! 🗺️ It shows them how to help kids learn in the best way possible. 📚

practice_questions.q: What does the NCF help teachers do?
practice_questions.a: It helps them teach kids in the best way possible!

subject: english
title: Understanding Panchkoshiya Vikas
concept: Panchkoshiya Vikas is about developing the whole person.
explanation: Panchkoshiya Vikas means growing in five important ways! 🌟 It helps us be strong, smart, and happy! Each part helps us be the best we can be! 💖

practice_questions.q: What are the five ways we can grow?
practice_questions.a: We can grow physically, emotionally, cognitively, socially, and culturally!

subject: english
title: Play-Based Learning
concept: Learning can be fun and engaging through play!
explanation: Learning isn't just about books 📚; it's also about playing games 🎲, talking with friends 🗣️, and doing fun activities! This helps you learn better and enjoy the process.
examples: Playing a game to learn numbers 🔢

practice_questions.q: What are some fun ways to learn?
practice_questions.a: Playing games, using toys, and talking with friends!

subject: english
title: Language and Communication
concept: Language helps us communicate and learn!
explanation: Language is not just for talking; it helps us understand the world 🌍 and learn new things! It's like a key that opens doors to knowledge 🔑.

practice_questions.q: Why is language important?
practice_questions.a: It helps us communicate and learn about everything!

subject: english
title: Understanding Our Culture
concept: Our culture shapes who we are!
explanation: The traditions and stories from our culture help us understand ourselves and our community better. It's like a beautiful tapestry that tells our story! 🧵

practice_questions.q: What is culture?
practice_questions.a: It's the traditions and stories that make us who we are!

subject: english
title: Fun Learning Activities
concept: Learning through fun activities!
explanation: Let's do some fun activities to learn! 🎨✂️

practice_questions.q: Can you name a fun activity we can do to learn?
practice_questions.a: We can draw or play a game!

subject: english
title: Developing Strong Oral Language Skills
concept: Children develop strong oral language skills through interaction.
explanation: At this stage, children learn to express themselves better by speaking and using words they know. For example, they can talk about their favorite toys or describe what they see around them. 🧸🌳

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite toy?
practice_questions.a: My favorite toy is a teddy bear.

practice_questions.q: Can you describe your home?
practice_questions.a: My home is cozy and has a garden.

subject: english
title: Introduction to Writing
concept: Writing is introduced gradually from drawing to sentences.
explanation: Children start by drawing pictures and labeling them, then move on to writing simple words and sentences. For example, they can draw a cat and write 'cat' under it. 🐱✍️
examples: Labeling a picture of a dog with 'dog'

practice_questions.q: What did you draw today?
Can you write the word for your drawing?

subject: english
title: Action Song
concept: Learning through songs and rhymes.
explanation: Singing songs helps children remember words and have fun! 🎶😊

subject: english
title: Learning with Activities
concept: Children learn best through play and activities.
explanation: Activities like drawing, singing, and playing games make learning exciting! For example, playing a game where you name animals helps you learn new words. 🐶🎨

practice_questions.q: What game do you like to play?
Can you name three animals?

subject: english
title: Thinking and Observing
concept: Learning through observation and critical thinking.
explanation: Children learn by looking around and asking questions. For example, if they see a tree, they might wonder why leaves change color. 🌳🤔

practice_questions.q: What do you see outside?
Why do you think the sky is blue?

subject: english
title: Parts of the Body and Hygiene
concept: Learn about body parts and the importance of hygiene.
explanation: Let's learn the names of our body parts and why keeping them clean is important! 🧼👐

practice_questions.q: What do we use to see?
Why should we wash our hands?

subject: english
title: Politeness and Greetings
concept: Learn different ways to greet people.
explanation: Saying 'Hello' or 'Namaste' makes everyone feel happy! 😊👋

practice_questions.q: How do we greet someone in India?
What is another way to say hello?

subject: english
title: The Sparrow Family
concept: Understanding family members through a story.
explanation: Let's read a cute story about a sparrow family! 🐦❤️
narrative: Once upon a time, there was a little sparrow family. They lived in a cozy nest and loved each other very much. The mother sparrow taught her little ones how to fly and find food. They chirped happily together every day! 🐦✨

subject: english
title: Animals and Birds
concept: Learn about animals and birds in our surroundings.
explanation: Look around! There are so many animals and birds. Let's discover them! 🦁🐦

practice_questions.q: What animal is known as the king of the jungle?
Which bird can talk?

subject: english
title: Up and Down
concept: Learn prepositions using a folktale.
explanation: Let's learn about 'up' and 'down' with a fun story! 📖⬆️⬇️
examples: The monkey went up the tree! 🌳

practice_questions.q: If the ball is on the table, where is it?
If the cat is under the chair, where is it?

subject: english
title: Catch Me If You Can
concept: Learn about rhyming words through a comic strip.
explanation: Rhyming words are fun! Let's see how they sound together! 🎤🎶
rhyme:
Catch me if you can, I’m the fastest in the land! 🏃‍♂️💨
I can run and jump, and play, all through the sunny day! ☀️
With my friends, we laugh and cheer, rhyming words are always near! 🎉

subject: english
title: Variety of Fruits and Vegetables
concept: Explore different fruits and vegetables in our country.
explanation: Yummy fruits and veggies are everywhere! Let's learn about them! 🍎🥦

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite fruit?
Which vegetable is green and healthy?

subject: english
title: The Food We Eat
concept: Learn about respecting food and diversity.
explanation: Food is important! Let's learn to respect it and appreciate its variety! 🍽️🙏

practice_questions.q: Why should we respect food?
practice_questions.a: Because it helps us grow strong! 💪

practice_questions.q: What do we say before eating?

subject: english
title: Understanding Seasons
concept: Learn about different seasons and their characteristics.
explanation: Seasons change the weather! Let's explore them! 🌦️❄️

practice_questions.q: What season do we wear warm clothes?
Which season has flowers blooming?

subject: english
title: The Colors of the Rainbow
concept: Learn about the colors of the rainbow through a story.
explanation: Rainbows are magical! Let's discover their colors! 🌈✨
narrative: Once after a rain, a beautiful rainbow appeared in the sky. It had seven colors: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet! Each color shone brightly, making everyone smile! 🌈😊

subject: english
title: Colouring the National Flag
concept: Understanding the national flag and its significance.
explanation: The national flag is a symbol of our country. 🌍 Let's learn about its colors and what they mean!
examples: The flag has three colors: saffron, white, and green.

practice_questions.q: What colors are in the national flag?

subject: english
title: Learning Languages
concept: The importance of learning multiple languages.
explanation: Learning different languages helps us communicate with more people! 🗣️
examples: Speaking Hindi at home and English at school.

practice_questions.q: Why is it good to know more than one language?
practice_questions.a: It helps us talk to more friends!

subject: english
title: Growing in Different Ways
concept: Understanding holistic development in children.
explanation: Children grow in many ways: physically, emotionally, and socially! 🌱
examples: Playing with friends helps us learn to share.

practice_questions.q: What are some ways we grow?
practice_questions.a: By playing, learning, and making friends!

subject: english
title: Using Different Learning Tools
concept: The importance of using various resources for learning.
explanation: We can learn from books, stories, and even videos! 📚🎥

practice_questions.q: What can we use to learn?

subject: english
title: Learning is Fun
concept: A fun rhyme about learning.
explanation: Let's enjoy learning together! 🎉
text:
Learning is fun, oh yes indeed! 🎶
With friends and books, we plant the seed. 🌱
Stories and games, we laugh and play,
Learning together, every day! 🎉

subject: english
title: Two Little Hands
concept: Understanding the importance of family and helping hands.
explanation: We all have two little hands that help us do many things! 🤲 Let's learn how we can use our hands to help our family.

practice_questions.q: What can you do with your hands to help your family?
practice_questions.a: I can help set the table or clean my room!

subject: english
title: Learning to Greet
concept: The importance of saying hello and goodbye.
explanation: Greeting people is a nice way to show you care! 🌟 Let's learn some fun ways to say hello and goodbye.

practice_questions.q: How do you greet your friends?

subject: english
title: Picture Time
concept: Exploring the world through pictures.
explanation: Pictures tell stories without words! 📸 Let's look at some pictures and see what stories they tell.
examples: A picture of a park with children playing 🌳
A picture of a market with fruits 🍎🍌

practice_questions.q: What do you see in this picture?
practice_questions.a: I see children playing on swings!

subject: english
title: The Cap-seller and the Monkeys
concept: A fun story about a cap-seller and some mischievous monkeys.
explanation: Once there was a cap-seller who sold colorful caps. One day, some monkeys stole his caps! 🐒 Let's see how he got them back.
narrative: The cap-seller was walking through the forest when he stopped to rest. He placed his caps on a tree and fell asleep. When he woke up, he found that monkeys were wearing his caps! He tried to get them back by throwing his own cap on the ground. The monkeys copied him and threw the caps down! He collected all his caps and went home happily.

subject: english
title: The Food We Eat
concept: Understanding different types of food.
explanation: Food gives us energy! 🍽️ Let's learn about the different foods we eat and why they are important.

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite food?

subject: english
title: The Four Seasons
concept: Learning about the four seasons of the year.
explanation: There are four seasons: spring, summer, autumn, and winter! 🌸☀️🍂❄️ Let's discover what happens in each season.

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite season and why?
practice_questions.a: I love summer because I can go swimming! 🏊

subject: english
title: Anandi’s Rainbow
concept: A fun rhyme about rainbows.
explanation: Rainbows are magical! 🌈 Let's enjoy this rhyme about Anandi's rainbow.
content:
Red and yellow, green and blue,  
Look at the rainbow, shining bright too!  
Anandi smiles, oh what a sight,  
Colors of the rainbow, pure delight! 🌈

subject: hindi
title: मीना का परिवार
concept: परिवार के सदस्यों की पहचान
explanation: हमारे परिवार में कई लोग होते हैं। मीना के परिवार में उसके दादा, दादी, माता, पिता, चाचा, और उसका छोटा भाई पदवाकि हैं। 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦

practice_questions.q: इस कहानी में कौन-कौन है?
practice_questions.a: मीना, दादा, दादी, माता, पिता, चाचा, और पदवाकि।

practice_questions.q: मीना के भाई का नाम क्या है?
आप अपने घर में कहाँ-कहाँ खेल सकते हैं?
practice_questions.a: आप अपने घर के आँगन, कमरे, या बगीचे में खेल सकते हैं।

subject: hindi
title: मीना का परिवार
narrative: मीना के परिवार में सात लोग हैं— उसके दादा, दादी, माता, पिता, चाचा, मीना और उसका छोटा भाई पदवाकि। पदवाकि तीन साल का है। वह बहुत नटखट और चुलबुला है। मीना को अपने भाई के साथ खेलने में बहुत आनंद आता है। पदवाकि भागकर कमियों के पीछे छिप जाता है। मीना उसे ढूंढ लेती है तो वह जोश-जोश से हँसता है। मीना, चाचाजी और पदवाकि बगीचे में जाते हैं जहाँ दादी और माँ फल काट रही हैं। मीना के पिता और दादाजी गमलों में पानी दे रहे हैं। थोड़ी देर में माँ सबको फल देती हैं। सब लोग मिल-जुलकर खुशी से फल खाते हैं और आपस में बातें करते हैं।

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों का खेल
concept: शब्दों की पहचान
explanation: बच्चों को 'दी', 'दे', 'दो' से शुरू होने वाले शब्दों की पहचान करनी है। जैसे: 'दादा', 'दादी', 'दीदी'। 📝

practice_questions.q: ‘द’ से शुरू होने वाले कुछ शब्द बताइए।
नीचे दिए गए शब्दों को पढ़ें और पहचानें।

subject: hindi
title: चंदा मामा
concept: A fun rhyme about Chanda Mama.
explanation: This rhyme is about Chanda Mama and how he brings joy to children. 🌙✨

subject: hindi
title: दादा-दादी
concept: Understanding family relationships.
explanation: This lesson teaches us about our grandparents and how they wear beautiful clothes. 👵👴

practice_questions.q: दादा-दादी क्या करते हैं?

subject: hindi
title: रीना की सुबह
concept: A story about Reena's daily routine.
explanation: This story follows Reena as she wakes up early, brushes her teeth, and goes to school. 🌅📚

practice_questions.q: रीना सुबह क्या करती है?
practice_questions.a: रीना सुबह जल्दी उठती है और अपने दाँत साफ करती है।

practice_questions.q: रीना स्कूल में क्या करती है?
practice_questions.a: वह अपनी सहेलियों के साथ खेलती है।

subject: hindi
title: दैनिक गतिविधियाँ
concept: Discussing daily routines.
explanation: Let's talk about what we do every day! 🏠🌞

practice_questions.q: आप सुबह कैसे उठते हैं?
आप नहाते कैसे हैं?

subject: hindi
title: घर-घर खेल की सामग्री
concept: बच्चों को मिट्टी से घर-घर खेलने की चीजें बनाना सिखाना।
explanation: बच्चे मिट्टी से चीजें बनाकर खेल सकते हैं। जैसे कि बर्तन, कटोरी, और घर। 🏠👩‍🍳

subject: hindi
title: घर के विभिन्न कमरे
concept: घर के विभिन्न कमरों के नाम और उनके कार्य।
explanation: हमारे घर में कई कमरे होते हैं, जैसे रसोई, कमरा, बाथरूम आदि। 🏡

practice_questions.q: आपके घर में कौन-कौन से कमरे हैं?

subject: hindi
title: इमा और जानी की कहानी
concept: दो बहनों की कहानी जो स्कूल जाना चाहती हैं।
explanation: इमा और जानी दो बहनें हैं। इमा स्कूल जाती है, लेकिन जानी अभी छोटी है। माँ उसे समझाती है। 📚👧👧

practice_questions.q: जानी स्कूल क्यों नहीं जा पाई?
practice_questions.a: क्योंकि वह अभी बहुत छोटी है।

practice_questions.q: इमा ने क्या पहना?

subject: hindi
title: काम की कविता
concept: काम करने की प्रेरणा।
explanation: यह कविता हमें काम करने के लिए प्रेरित करती है। 💪✨

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों की पहचान
concept: शब्दों की पहचान और उनके अर्थ।
explanation: बच्चों को शब्दों की पहचान करने और उनके अर्थ समझाने में मदद करें। 📖🔍

practice_questions.q: नाना और नानी में क्या अंतर है?
practice_questions.a: नाना दादा होते हैं और नानी दादी होती हैं।

subject: hindi
title: कुब्द शिखने का महत्व
concept: बच्चों को कुब्द शिखने के लिए प्रेरित करना
explanation: बच्चों को कुब्द शिखने के लिए प्रोत्साहित करें ताकि वे अपने विचारों को व्यक्त कर सकें। 🌟

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों को जोड़ें
concept: अक्षरों को जोड़कर शब्द बनाना
explanation: नीचे दिए गए अक्षरों को जोड़कर अपने शब्द बनाएं। यह एक मजेदार खेल है! 🎉

practice_questions.q: शब्द बनाएं (i)
शब्द बनाएं (ii)
शब्द बनाएं (iii)
शब्द बनाएं (iv)
शब्द बनाएं (v)
शब्द बनाएं (vi)
शब्द बनाएं (vii)
शब्द बनाएं (viii)
शब्द बनाएं (ix)
शब्द बनाएं (x)

subject: hindi
title: बकिी आई, पबलली आई
concept: बच्चों को खेल के माध्यम से सिखाना
explanation: यह एक मजेदार खेल गीत है जो बच्चों को आनंदित करेगा! 🎶
text:
बकिी आई, पबलली आई,
मेंऽ-मेंऽ आई, म्ाऊँ -म्ाऊँ  आई।
धक की गाड़ी धौं-धौं-धौं,
चल दी गाड़ी पौं-पौं-पौं।

subject: hindi
title: गधे की कहानी
concept: गधे की मीठी चीज़ों की इच्छा
explanation: यह कहानी एक गधे के बारे में है जो मीठी चीज़ें खाना चाहता था। 🐴🍬
text:
एक पदन गधे का मन पमठाई खाने का हुआ।
गधे ने अपने पमरिों से क्ुछ मीठा खाने को माँगा।
भालू ने कहा, “शहद खा लो।”
गधे ने मना कि पद्ा।
खिगोश ने कहा, “गाजि खा लो।”
गधे ने मना कि पद्ा।
चींटे ने कहा, “गुड़ खा लो।”
गधे ने मना कि पद्ा।
हाथी ने कहा, “गनना खा लो।”
गधे ने मना कि पद्ा।
पगलही ने कहा, “आम खा लो।”
गधे ने मना कि पद्ा।
पबलली बोली, “हलवाई की दुकान पर चलो।” गधे को यह बात पसंद आ गई।
तब सब साथ-साथ पमठाई खाने के लिए चल पड़े।

subject: hindi
title: कहानी से प्रश्न
concept: कहानी के आधार पर प्रश्न पूछना
explanation: कहानी पढ़ने के बाद, इसके बारे में कुछ प्रश्न पूछें। 🤔

practice_questions.q: शिक्षण-संकेत – 'शमठ्ई' कहानी में आए हुए शब्दों एवँ अन्य शब्दों की पहचान करें।
practice_questions.a: ग, ह, ठ, ध की ध्वनि और आकृशत की पहचान करें।

practice_questions.q: किसे खाने में क्या पसंद है? रेखा खींचकर शमि्इए।
इस कहानी में कौन-कौन से जानवर हैं? तीन जानवरों के नाम लिखिए।

subject: hindi
topic: गधे और उसके दोस्तों की कहानी
title: गधे की मदद
concept: सच्ची दोस्ती और मदद का महत्व
explanation: कहानी में गधा अपने दोस्तों की मदद करता है। यह हमें सिखाता है कि हमें हमेशा एक-दूसरे की मदद करनी चाहिए। 🤝
examples: गधे ने बकरी की मदद की।

practice_questions.q: गधे ने बकरी की मदद कैसे की?
practice_questions.a: गधे ने बकरी को तालाब से बाहर निकाला।

practice_questions.q: किसने पचपड़्ा को बचाया?

subject: hindi
title: सही या गलत बताइए
concept: विचार करने की क्षमता
explanation: यहाँ कुछ वाक्य दिए गए हैं। आपको बताना है कि ये सही हैं या गलत। 🤔

practice_questions.q: गधे ने पचपड़्ा की बात मान ली।
खिगोश ने आम खाने को कहा।
हाथी ने कुछ नहीं कहा।
सभी ने गधे की सहायता करने का प्रयास किया।

subject: hindi
title: इमली की डाल पर
concept: प्रकृति का आनंद
explanation: यह कविता हमें प्रकृति के बारे में बताती है और हमें इमली के पेड़ के बारे में जानकारी देती है। 🌳

subject: hindi
title: हाथी, बकरी और पचपड़्ा
concept: साथी होने का महत्व
explanation: यह कहानी हाथी, बकरी और पचपड़्ा की है जो मिलकर एक-दूसरे की मदद करते हैं। यह हमें सिखाती है कि दोस्ती में एकता महत्वपूर्ण है। 🐘🐐🐦
text: एक था हाथी। एक थी बकरी। दोनों साथ-साथ जंगल में जाते। हाथी डाली झुकाता और बकरी पत्ते खाती। एक दिन दोनों जंगल में गए। वहाँ एक तालाब दिखाई पड़ा। वहाँ एक बेर का पेड़ था। हाथी ने पेड़ को झुकाया। बकरी बेर खाने लगी। तभी पेड़ से पचपड़्ा का बच्चा पानी में गिरा। बकरी उसे बचाने गई तो वह भी डूबने लगी। हाथी ने दोनों को बाहों में पकड़ लिया। कुछ दिनों में पचपड़्ा का बच्चा बड़ा हो गया। पचपड़्ा पता लगाती कि कहाँ-कहाँ फल लगे हैं। तीनों वहाँ जाते और खूब फल खाते। अब तीन साथी साथ रहते।

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों की पहचान
concept: शब्दों की ध्वनियाँ
explanation: इस गतिविधि में आपको शब्दों की ध्वनियाँ पहचाननी हैं। यह आपकी भाषा कौशल को बढ़ाने में मदद करेगा। 📚

practice_questions.q: कहानी में आए तीन साथियों के नाम बताइए।

narrative: एक भालू था। उसे नानी के घर जाना था। रास्ते में खाने के लिए भालू ने बोरी में आलू रख लिए। रास्ते में पीपल के पेड़ पर एक बंदर बैठा था। उसने भालू से पूछा, 'कहाँ जा रहे हो, भालू?' भालू ने कुछ नहीं कहा, लेकिन आलू टपकते रहे। बंदर ने सोचा, 'मैं पता लगा सकता हूँ कि भालू कहाँ जा रहा है।'

subject: hindi
title: मेघा और बिखा का साहस
concept: साहस और सहयोग
explanation: मेघा और बिखा ने अपने बच्चों के साथ मिलकर नदी पार करने का साहस दिखाया। यह हमें सिखाता है कि जब हम एक साथ होते हैं, तो हम किसी भी चुनौती का सामना कर सकते हैं। 🌊
narrative: एक बार की बात है, दो सहेपलियाँ थीं— एक मुर्गी और एक बत्तख। मुर्गी का नाम था— मेघा। बत्तख का नाम था— बिखा। उनके तीन-तीन बच्चे थे। वे सब मेला देखने दूसरे गाँव जा रहे थे। रास्ते में नदी आ गई। मेघा ने कहा, “हम नदी कैसे पार करेंगे? हम तो डूब जाएंगे।” बिखा ने कहा, “हम सब तैरकर नदी पार कर लेंगे।” मेघा ने पूछा, “वो कैसे?”

practice_questions.q: मेघा और बिखा ने बच्चों के साथ नदी कैसे पार की?

narrative: हिंगिंग हिंगिंग, गीली होली आई, सबके मन को होली भा गई। खेलें-कूदें खुशी मनाएँ, ढोल बजाकर होली गाएँ। पंक्तियाँ पड़ी पत्तियों की टोली, सबने मिलकर खेली होली। आओ, लाल गुलाल लगाएँ, पिचकारी से रंग उड़ाएँ। आनंदमयी कचिता।

practice_questions.a: क्योंकि यह चाँद के बारे में है।

subject: hindi
title: Identify the Shapes

subject: hindi
title: कौए की कहानी

subject: hindi
title: पेड़-पौधों के नाम

subject: hindi
title: चित्र बनाना

subject: hindi
title: कौन परिंदा

text:

कौन परिंदा कुकड़ू-कूँ
कौन परिंदा बोले काँव-काँव
कौन परिंदा बोले कुहू-कुहू
कौन परिंदा बोले टें-टें

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों का खेल

subject: hindi
title: हमने तीन चीजें देखीं

subject: hindi
title: अनाम दापड़म

text:
अनाम दापड़म भी कहलाता।
आम चूसकि बच्चा खाता॥
इमली तो खट्टी होती है।
ईख सदा मीठी होती है॥
उललू रात में जगते रहते।
ऊदपबलाव जल-थल में रहते॥

subject: hindi
title: आकाश और खेत
concept: The beauty of nature and farming.
explanation: आसमान में चमकते तारे और खेतों में काम करते किसान हमें बताते हैं कि प्रकृति कितनी सुंदर है। 🌟🌾

subject: hindi
title: घी और मिठाई

practice_questions.q: घी किससे बनता है?

subject: hindi
title: षट्कोण

practice_questions.q: षट्कोण में कितने कोने होते हैं?
practice_questions.a: षट्कोण में छह कोने होते हैं।

subject: hindi
title: समीक्षा
concept: Revising what we learned.
explanation: अब हम सब कुछ जो हमने सीखा है, उसे दोहराएंगे। 📚🔄

practice_questions.q: आपको घी किससे मिलता है?
practice_questions.a: घी हमें दूध से मिलता है।

practice_questions.q: षट्कोण में कितने कोने होते हैं?
practice_questions.a: षट्कोण में छह कोने होते हैं।

subject: hindi
title: समग्र विकास का महत्व

explanation: बच्चों का समग्र विकास उनके शारीरिक, मानसिक और सामाजिक स्वास्थ्य के लिए जरूरी है। यह उन्हें जीवन में आगे बढ़ने में मदद करता है। 😊
examples: बच्चे खेलते हैं, पढ़ाई करते हैं और दोस्तों के साथ समय बिताते हैं।

practice_questions.q: समग्र विकास क्या है?
practice_questions.a: यह बच्चों के सभी पहलुओं का विकास है।

practice_questions.q: बच्चों को किस तरह का विकास करना चाहिए?

subject: hindi
title: शिक्षा का महत्व

explanation: शिक्षा से बच्चे ज्ञान प्राप्त करते हैं और अपने भविष्य को उज्जवल बनाते हैं। 📚✨

practice_questions.q: शिक्षा क्यों जरूरी है?
practice_questions.a: यह हमें ज्ञान और समझ देती है।

practice_questions.q: आप स्कूल क्यों जाते हैं?
practice_questions.a: पढ़ाई करने और सीखने के लिए।

subject: hindi
title: अपने अनुभव साझा करें

examples: आपने पहली बार स्कूल जाने पर क्या महसूस किया?

practice_questions.q: आपका सबसे अच्छा अनुभव क्या था?
practice_questions.a: जब मैंने पहली बार साइकिल चलाना सीखा।

practice_questions.q: आपको कौन सा खेल पसंद है?

subject: hindi
title: स्रीखने के उपकरण और माध्यम

practice_questions.q: स्रीखने के लिए कौन-कौन से उपकरण चाहिए?

subject: hindi
title: भाषा और सामाजिकता
concept: भाषा का विकास और सामाजिकता का संबंध
explanation: भाषा हमारे विचारों को व्यक्त करने का एक तरीका है। जब हम भाषा सीखते हैं, तो हम दूसरों के साथ अच्छे से बात कर पाते हैं। 🗣️

practice_questions.q: भाषा क्यों महत्वपूर्ण है?
practice_questions.a: क्योंकि यह हमें संवाद करने में मदद करती है।

subject: hindi
title: स्रीखने का अभ्यास

explanation: अब आप अपनी किताब से कुछ शब्द लिखें और उन्हें अपने दोस्तों को दिखाएँ। ✍️

subject: hindi
title: भाषा का महत्व

explanation: भाषा हमारे विचारों और भावनाओं को व्यक्त करने का एक महत्वपूर्ण तरीका है। यह हमें समझने और संवाद करने में मदद करती है। 😊
examples: भाषा से हम अपने दोस्तों से बात कर सकते हैं।
कहानियाँ सुनकर हम नई चीजें सीखते हैं।

practice_questions.q: भाषा का क्या महत्व है?
practice_questions.a: भाषा से हम अपने विचारों को व्यक्त कर सकते हैं।

practice_questions.q: आप भाषा का उपयोग कैसे करते हैं?
practice_questions.a: मैं भाषा का उपयोग अपने दोस्तों से बात करने के लिए करता हूँ।

subject: hindi
title: भाषा विकास गतिविधियाँ

explanation: भाषा का विकास करने के लिए हमें कहानियाँ सुननी और सुनानी चाहिए। हम खेल-खेल में नए शब्द भी सीख सकते हैं। 🎉

practice_questions.q: आप कौन सी कहानी सुनना पसंद करते हैं?

practice_questions.q: आप शब्दों का खेल कैसे खेलते हैं?
practice_questions.a: मैं शब्दों को मिलाकर नए शब्द बनाता हूँ।

subject: hindi
title: चतुर चूहा
concept: चतुराई से समस्याओं का समाधान करना।
explanation: एक बार एक चूहा था जो बहुत चतुर था। उसने अपनी चतुराई से बिल्ली को धोखा दिया और बच गया। 🐭

practice_questions.q: चूहा कैसे बचा?
practice_questions.a: चूहा अपनी चतुराई से बिल्ली को धोखा देकर बचा।

practice_questions.q: आप चतुराई से क्या सीख सकते हैं?
practice_questions.a: समस्याओं का समाधान करने के लिए सोच-समझकर काम करना।

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों का खेल
concept: शब्दों के साथ खेलना।
explanation: शब्दों के साथ खेलना बहुत मजेदार होता है। चलो, हम मिलकर कुछ शब्द बनाते हैं! 🎈

subject: hindi
title: Reading Stories and Understanding
concept: Understanding stories through pictures and words.
explanation: When we read stories, we can look at the pictures to guess what happens next! 📖✨
examples: Look at the picture of a cat, what do you think it will do next?
If the story is about a tree, what do you think will happen in the story?

practice_questions.q: What did the cat do in the story?
Why did the tree look sad?

subject: hindi
title: Finding Words in Rhymes
concept: Finding similar words in rhymes.
explanation: Let's find words that sound the same in our rhymes! 🎶
examples: Find words that rhyme with 'गाना' (gana) like 'साना' (sana).

practice_questions.q: What rhymes with 'बिल्ली'?
Can you find a word that rhymes with 'गाड़ी'?

subject: hindi
title: Mrina's Family
concept: Understanding family relationships.
explanation: This is a story about Mrina and her family. Let's learn about who is in her family! 👨‍👩‍👧
narrative: Mrina lives with her mother, father, and little brother. They all love to play together. One day, they went to the park and had a picnic. Mrina's mother made delicious sandwiches, and her father brought a ball to play with. They had so much fun! 🥪⚽

subject: hindi
title: Rhymes about Food
concept: Learning about food through rhymes.
explanation: Let's sing a fun rhyme about our favorite foods! 🍎🍌
text:
हमारा खाना, मीठा और नमकीन, 
दाल, चावल, सब्जी, और रोटी, 
खाने में सबको है मज़ा, 
खाओ, खिलाओ, सबको है भा! 🎉

subject: hindi
title: कहानियों का आनंद
concept: कहानियों को पढ़ते समय बच्चों से सवाल पूछना।
explanation: जब हम कहानियाँ पढ़ते हैं, तो हमें बच्चों से सवाल पूछने चाहिए जैसे, 'आपको कहानी में क्या अच्छा लगा?' यह उन्हें सोचने और अपनी बात कहने का मौका देता है। 😊

subject: hindi
title: कहानी के बाद चर्चा
concept: कहानी के बाद बच्चों से चर्चा करना।
explanation: कहानी के बाद बच्चों से 'कहानी में कौन-कौन से जानवर थे?' जैसे सवाल पूछें। यह उन्हें अपनी समझ को साझा करने में मदद करता है। 🐶🐱

title: शब्दों का पहचानना

explanation: कहानी में जो शब्द बार-बार आते हैं, उन्हें पहचानने का अभ्यास करें। जैसे 'नाना', 'नानी', 'भाई'। यह बच्चों को शब्दों की पहचान में मदद करता है। 🔤

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों का खेल
concept: शब्दों के समूह बनाना।
explanation: बच्चों को 'दादा', 'दादी', 'नाना', 'नानी' जैसे शब्दों के समूह बनाने के लिए कहें। यह उन्हें शब्दों को जोड़ने में मदद करेगा। 👫

subject: hindi
title: कहानी में दोहराव
concept: कहानी में दोहराव का आनंद।
explanation: कहानी में कुछ शब्दों का दोहराव होता है, जैसे 'भुट्टे' में 'नाना', 'नानी', 'भुट्टे'। बच्चों को यह सुनने में मज़ा आता है। 🎶

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों को जोड़कर नए शब्द बनाना
concept: शब्दों को जोड़कर नए शब्द बनाना सिखाना।
explanation: बच्चे शब्दों को जोड़कर नए शब्द बना सकते हैं। जैसे, 'चिड़िया' और 'अशिरों' को जोड़कर नए शब्द बनाना।

practice_questions.q: मेरे नाम का उलटा क्या है?

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों का उलटा पहचानना
concept: शब्दों का उलटा पहचानने का अभ्यास।
explanation: बच्चे दिए गए शब्दों का उलटा पहचानेंगे। जैसे, 'नदी' का उलटा 'दिन' है।

practice_questions.q: नदी का उलटा क्या है?

subject: hindi
title: कबड़ी झबड़ी बकरड़ी
concept: शब्दों का चयन और दोहराव।
explanation: इस कविता में 'कबड़ी', 'झबड़ी', और 'बकरड़ी' शब्दों का मजेदार उपयोग किया गया है।

subject: hindi
title: नई कविता बनाना
concept: बच्चों को नई कविता बनाने के लिए प्रेरित करना।
explanation: बच्चे 'झबरा', 'कबरा', 'बकरा' शब्दों से नई कविता बना सकते हैं।
examples: झबरा झबरा, कबरा कबरा, बकरा बकरा

subject: hindi
title: अपने विचारों को चित्रों में व्यक्त करना
concept: बच्चे चित्रों के माध्यम से अपने विचार व्यक्त करते हैं।
explanation: बच्चे चित्र बनाकर अपने विचार और भावनाएं व्यक्त कर सकते हैं। जैसे, 'आप क्या देख रहे हैं?'

practice_questions.q: आप अपने चित्र में क्या-क्या देख पा रहे हैं?
practice_questions.a: अपने विचारों को चित्र में दिखाएं!

subject: hindi
title: अपने आस-पास की खोज
concept: बच्चों को अपने आस-पास की चीजों को जानने के लिए प्रेरित करना।
explanation: बच्चे अपने परिवार और आस-पड़ोस के बारे में जान सकते हैं। जैसे, पेड़-पौधों और जानवरों के बारे में।

practice_questions.q: आपके आस-पास कौन से जानवर हैं?

subject: hindi
title: Sharing is Caring
concept: Sharing experiences helps children learn and grow.
explanation: When children share their experiences, they learn from each other and become happier! 😊 For example, telling stories about fun times with family helps everyone feel connected.
examples: Telling your friend about a fun trip!
Drawing pictures and showing them to your family.

practice_questions.q: Why is sharing important?
practice_questions.a: It helps us learn and feel happy together!

subject: hindi
title: Fun with Sounds
concept: Rhymes make learning fun!
explanation: Rhymes are a fun way to learn new words and sounds! 🎶 Let's enjoy some rhymes together!

subject: hindi
title: घर-घर, घड़्री-घड़्री
concept: Enjoying playful sounds.
explanation: This rhyme is all about fun sounds we can make! Let's say it together! 🏠🔔

subject: hindi
title: भाई को आयु भाया
concept: Celebrating siblings.
explanation: This rhyme celebrates our brothers! Let's sing it together! 🎉

subject: hindi
title: Make Your Own Learning Tools
concept: Creating materials helps in learning.
explanation: Let's create some fun learning tools together! You can make drawings or use things around you! 🎨
examples: Make a drawing of your favorite animal!
Use old magazines to cut out pictures.

subject: hindi
title: Understanding Hindi Letters
concept: Learning the basic Hindi letters.
explanation: Hindi has many letters that help us form words! Let's learn some of them together! 🌟
examples: अ, आ, इ, ई, उ, ऊ

practice_questions.q: What is the first letter of Hindi?
How many vowels are there in Hindi?

subject: hindi
title: Hindi Rhymes
concept: Fun rhymes to learn Hindi.
explanation: Rhymes make learning fun and easy! 🎶

subject: hindi
title: The Clever Rabbit
concept: A story about a clever rabbit and his adventures.
explanation: Once upon a time, there was a clever rabbit who outsmarted a hungry lion! 🐰🦁

subject: hindi
title: Letter Tracing
concept: Practice tracing Hindi letters.
explanation: Let's practice writing the letters we learned! ✍️

subject: hindi
title: मीना का परिवार
concept: परिवार के सदस्यों के बारे में जानना
explanation: मीना के परिवार में उसके माता-पिता, दादा-दादी और भाई-बहन हैं। परिवार में सभी एक-दूसरे से प्यार करते हैं। ❤️👨‍👩‍👧‍👦
examples: मीना की माँ का नाम सुमिता है।

practice_questions.q: मीना के परिवार में कौन-कौन है?
practice_questions.a: मीना के परिवार में माँ, पिता, दादा-दादी और भाई हैं।

subject: hindi
title: दादा-दादी
concept: दादा-दादी का महत्व
explanation: दादा-दादी हमारे जीवन में बहुत महत्वपूर्ण होते हैं। वे हमें कहानियाँ सुनाते हैं और हमें प्यार करते हैं। 📖❤️
examples: दादी ने मुझे एक कहानी सुनाई।
दादा मुझे पार्क में ले गए।

practice_questions.q: दादा-दादी हमें क्या सिखाते हैं?
practice_questions.a: दादा-दादी हमें जीवन के अनुभव सिखाते हैं।

subject: hindi
title: मीना का दिन
concept: एक सामान्य दिन की कहानी
explanation: यह कहानी मीना के एक सामान्य दिन के बारे में है। वह सुबह उठती है, स्कूल जाती है और दोस्तों के साथ खेलती है। 🌞🏫
narrative: मीना सुबह जल्दी उठती है। वह नाश्ता करती है और स्कूल जाती है। स्कूल में वह अपने दोस्तों के साथ पढ़ाई करती है और खेलती है। दिन खत्म होने पर वह घर लौटती है।

subject: hindi
title: चाँद मामा
concept: चाँद की सुंदरता
explanation: यह कविता चाँद की सुंदरता के बारे में है। चाँद रात में आसमान में चमकता है। 🌙✨
text:
चाँद मामा, चाँद मामा, रात में आते हो।
चमकते हो आसमान में, सबको भाते हो।
तारों के संग खेलते हो, सबको जगमगाते हो।

subject: hindi
title: परिवार के सदस्यों का चित्र बनाओ
concept: परिवार के सदस्यों को पहचानना
explanation: अपने परिवार के सदस्यों के चित्र बनाओ और उनके नाम लिखो। 🎨🖍️
examples: चित्र में माँ, पिता, भाई, बहन बनाओ।

practice_questions.q: तुम्हारे परिवार में कौन-कौन है?
practice_questions.a: मेरे परिवार में माँ, पिता, दादा-दादी और भाई हैं।

subject: maths
title: Positional Words
concept: Understanding positional words like inside, outside, above, and below.
explanation: Positional words help us describe where things are! For example, a cat can be inside a box or outside in the garden. 🐱📦🌳
examples: The cat is under the table. 🐱⬇️
The ball is above the shelf. ⚽️⬆️

practice_questions.q: Where do you put your shoes?
Where do you throw the garbage?

subject: maths
title: Looking for My Furry Cat
concept: A fun rhyme about searching for a cat.
explanation: This rhyme helps us practice looking for things and using positional words! 🎶🐾
text:
Looking, looking, looking
Looking for my furry cat!
Are you sitting on the window shed?
Are you sleeping under my bed?
Where are you my furry cat?
Looking, looking, looking
Looking for my furry cat!
Are you inside the backpack?
Are you outside the red rack?
Where are you my furry cat?
Saw you hiding below the mat
Here comes my furry cat!
Come, come, come
Come to me my furry cat!
Saw you hopping above the hat.
Come, come, come
Come to me, my furry cat!
Saw you scratching the bottom of my jar.
Saw you playing at the top of my car.
Come to me, my furry cat!

subject: maths
title: Finding Objects Using Positional Words
concept: Using positional words to find hidden objects.
explanation: Let's play a game! One team hides objects and the other team finds them using words like 'inside' or 'outside'. 🕵️‍♂️🔍
examples: Look for a white thing which is hidden near the board and under the table.
Look for a red thing which is outside the classroom and under the tree.

subject: maths
title: Tick the Correct Items
concept: Identifying items that are inside or outside.
explanation: Look at the pictures and tick the things that are inside or outside! ✅🌳

subject: maths
title: Draw and Identify Positions
concept: Drawing and identifying the positions of objects.
explanation: Draw a smile below the nose and eyebrows above the eyes! Let's practice our drawing skills! 🎨😊

practice_questions.q: A boy is sitting on/under the tree.
Birds are on/under the tree.

subject: maths
title: Understanding Our National Flag
concept: Learning about the colors of the National Flag.
explanation: How many colors are there in our National Flag? Let's learn and have fun! 🇮🇳🎉

practice_questions.q: Which colour is at the top of the tiranga?
Which colour is below the white colour on the tiranga?
Which colour is above the green colour on the tiranga?
Where is the ashoka chakra on the tiranga?

subject: maths
title: Chhuk Chhuk goes our Train!
concept: Understanding the concept of trains and counting bogies.
explanation: Let's have fun with trains! 🚂 Can you count how many bogies are there? Let's sing together!

practice_questions.q: How many bogies are there after the engine?
practice_questions.a: Count the bogies behind the engine.

practice_questions.q: How many bogies are there before the red bogie?
practice_questions.a: Count the bogies in front of the red bogie.

practice_questions.q: Fill the orange color in the bogies after the red bogie.
practice_questions.a: Color the bogies after the red one orange.

practice_questions.q: Fill the blue color in the bogies before the red bogie.
practice_questions.a: Color the bogies before the red one blue.

subject: maths
title: Sharing Train Journeys
concept: Discussing experiences related to trains.
explanation: Let's talk about our train journeys! 🚆 Have you ever traveled by train? Share your stories!

subject: maths
title: Sorting Leaves and Chalk Pieces
concept: Learning to sort objects into groups.
explanation: Let's sort some objects! 🍂 Can you group leaves, pebbles, and chalk pieces?

subject: maths
title: Arranging the Cupboard
concept: Using positional vocabulary to arrange objects.
explanation: Let's arrange the cupboard! 📚 Can you put two objects in the bottom rack and one in the top rack?

subject: maths
title: What is Long? What is Round?
concept: Identifying long and round objects.
explanation: Let's find long and round things around us! 🏀 Can you name one long object and one round object?

subject: maths
title: Look Around
concept: Recognizing shapes in our surroundings.
explanation: Let's sing about shapes! 🎶 Can you spot long and round shapes around you?

subject: maths
title: Matching Similar Shapes
concept: Matching objects based on their shapes.
explanation: Let's match the shapes! 🔵 Can you find shapes that look alike?

practice_questions.q: Tick the shape which is similar to a ball.
practice_questions.a: Find and tick the round shape.

practice_questions.q: Tick the shape which is similar to a cap.
practice_questions.a: Find and tick the shape that looks like a cap.

practice_questions.q: Tick the shape which is similar to a glass.
practice_questions.a: Find and tick the shape that looks like a glass.

practice_questions.q: Tick the shape which is similar to a matchbox.
practice_questions.a: Find and tick the shape that looks like a matchbox.

subject: maths
title: Make Something with Shapes
concept: Using different objects to create shapes.
explanation: Let's create something fun! 🎨 Use objects around you to make a house, robot, or anything you like!

subject: maths
topic: The Little Lamb and the Wolf
title: The Little Lamb's Adventure
concept: Understanding safety and cleverness.
explanation: Once there was a little lamb who wanted to visit her grandmother. But a wolf wanted to catch her! 🐑💨 The lamb cleverly told the wolf that she needed to grow big first, and her grandmother helped her escape by rolling her in a dholak. This story teaches us to be smart and safe.

subject: maths
title: Understanding Rolling and Sliding
concept: Identifying objects that roll and slide.
explanation: Some objects can roll like a ball ⚽️, while others slide like a book 📚. Let's explore which objects can do both! Collect items around you and see if they roll or slide.

practice_questions.q: Write ‘R’ for rolling objects and ‘S’ for sliding objects.
Do you see things which can do both, roll and slide?

subject: maths
title: Roll or Slide?
concept: Classifying objects based on their movement.
explanation: Let's find out if the following objects roll, slide, or do both! 🛴🛹

practice_questions.q: Does a cardboard box roll, slide, or do both?

subject: maths
title: Mango Treat
concept: Counting and addition through storytelling.
explanation: In our story, birds join a squirrel eating a mango. 🐦🍑 Let's count how many birds join one by one! 1 bird, then 2 birds, and so on. This helps us learn counting!
examples: 1 bird, 2 birds, 3 birds... up to 9 birds.

practice_questions.q: How many animals or birds were eating the mango in the beginning?
How many more animals or birds joined every time?

subject: maths
title: Match the Birds
concept: Matching numbers with fingers.
explanation: Let's match the number of birds with the number of fingers! 🖐️ This helps us understand numbers better.

practice_questions.q: Show 3 fingers to your friend. How many fingers does your friend show?

subject: maths
title: Different Ways to Show Numbers
concept: Showing numbers using fingers.
explanation: You can show numbers using your fingers! For example, if you show 4 fingers, your friend can show fewer or more fingers. Let's have fun with this! ✋✋✋✋
examples: Show 4 fingers and ask a friend to show 3 fingers.
Show 5 fingers and ask a friend to show 6 fingers.

practice_questions.q: What are the different ways of showing 4 fingers using one hand?
practice_questions.a: You can show 4 fingers by holding up your hand with all fingers extended.

subject: maths
title: Let Us Play Outside
concept: Counting and grouping children.
explanation: Let's play a fun game where we count and make groups! When one child claps and says a number, everyone can form groups of that number. For example, if the number is 4, everyone holds hands in groups of four! 🤝
examples: If there are 8 children, they can form 2 groups of 4.

practice_questions.q: How can you count and make groups?
practice_questions.a: By holding hands and forming groups of the number called out.

subject: maths
title: My Drawing Day
concept: Understanding more, less, and equal.
explanation: Let's learn about comparing numbers! We will tick if there are more, less, or equal items. If you have 5 apples and your friend has 3, you have more apples! 🍏🍏🍏🍏🍏
examples: Tick the box if you have more apples than your friend.

practice_questions.q: How do you know if you have more or less?
practice_questions.a: By comparing the number of items.

subject: maths
title: Count and Match
concept: Counting and matching objects.
explanation: Let's count objects and match them with numbers! For example, if you see 3 balls, you can write the number 3 next to them. 🎾🎾🎾
examples: Count the number of stars and write the number next to them.

practice_questions.q: How many balls do you see?
practice_questions.a: Count them and write the number.

subject: maths
title: Join the Numbers in Order
concept: Connecting numbers in sequence.
explanation: Let's draw a path connecting the numbers from 1 to 9! Make sure the lines don't cross each other. It’s like a fun maze! 🧩
examples: Draw a line from 1 to 2, then to 3, and so on.

practice_questions.q: Can you connect the numbers in order?
practice_questions.a: Yes, by drawing a path without crossing lines.

subject: maths
title: Ordering Numbers from Smallest to Largest
concept: Understanding how to arrange numbers in order.
explanation: Let's help the naughty monkey put the number cards in the right order! 🐒📊 For example, if we have the numbers 3, 5, 2, we can arrange them as 2, 3, 5.

practice_questions.q: Arrange these numbers: 5, 2, 8, 3?

subject: maths
title: Counting Yellow Boxes
concept: Counting objects in a picture.
explanation: How many yellow boxes can you see? Let's count together! 📦✨
examples: Count the yellow boxes in the picture.

practice_questions.q: How many yellow boxes are there?

subject: maths
title: Identifying Numbers from Pictures
concept: Recognizing numbers based on visual representation.
explanation: Look at the picture and write down how many sheep you see! 🐑✍️
examples: Count the sheep in the picture.

practice_questions.q: How many sheep are in the picture?

subject: maths
title: Fruit and Umbrella Counting
concept: Counting and grouping objects.
explanation: Draw any 4 fruits 🍎🍌🍇🍊 and see which two groups together have 8 fruits. Let's encircle them!

practice_questions.q: Which two groups together have 8 fruits?

subject: maths
title: Dot Designs and Counting
concept: Creating designs with dots and counting them.
explanation: Make dot designs with seeds or buttons! How many dots did you make? 🌟🔢
examples: Count the dots on your bug!

practice_questions.q: How many dots are on each bug?

subject: maths
title: Gola Monkey and His Buttons
concept: Understanding the concept of zero.
explanation: Gola monkey loves bananas 🍌 and keeps losing his buttons! How many buttons does he have left? Let's find out!
examples: Gola had 4 buttons, but after eating bananas, he lost some.

practice_questions.q: How many buttons does Gola have now?

subject: maths
title: Counting Backwards from 5 to 0
concept: Learning to count backwards.
explanation: Let's count backwards together! 5, 4, 3, 2, 1, 0! 🚀

practice_questions.q: Can you count backwards from 5?

subject: maths
title: Count the Diyas
concept: Counting objects in a celebration.
explanation: Aastha has lighted diyas on her birthday! 🎉 How many diyas can you count?
examples: Count the number of diyas in the picture.

practice_questions.q: How many diyas are there?

subject: maths
title: Making a String of 10 Beads
concept: Counting and grouping objects to make ten.
explanation: Let's count and draw beads to create a string of 10 beads! 🎨✨

subject: maths
title: Drawing Buttons in a Ten Frame
concept: Visualizing numbers using a ten frame.
explanation: Let's draw buttons to fill a ten frame! How many can we fit? 🖍️🟡
examples: Draw a ten frame and fill it with buttons: 🔵🔵🔵🔵🔵🔵🔵🔵🔵🔵

subject: maths
title: The Handy Five and Ten
concept: Using fingers to understand number pairs.
explanation: Show 3 fingers ✋, and your friend shows fingers to make it 5! Let's play! 🤗

subject: maths
title: Finding Number Pairs
concept: Identifying pairs of numbers that add up to 10.
explanation: Let's find pairs of numbers that make 10! 🧮

practice_questions.q: What pairs make 10?

subject: maths
title: Counting Up to 20
concept: Counting numbers from 11 to 20.
explanation: Let's count together from 11 to 20! 🎉📏

subject: maths
title: Counting Concrete Objects
concept: Using objects to count up to 20.
explanation: Count seeds or buttons up to 20! How many do you think are there? 🌱🔘

practice_questions.q: How many seeds do you have?

subject: maths
title: Colouring Tens Frames
concept: Visual representation of numbers using tens frames.
explanation: Colour the tens frames to show the numbers! 🎨🖍️

subject: maths
title: Building Towers with Blocks
concept: Comparing quantities using blocks.
explanation: Let's see which tower is the tallest and which has the most blocks! 🏗️📏

practice_questions.q: Which tower is the tallest?
Which tower used the most blocks?
practice_questions.a: Write the number of blocks used.

practice_questions.q: Which tower used the least blocks?
practice_questions.a: Write the number of blocks used.

subject: maths
title: Which Number is Bigger?
concept: Understanding how to compare numbers.
explanation: Let's find out which number is bigger! For example, 15 is bigger than 11 because it is 4 more than 11. 🥳

practice_questions.q: Circle the smallest number: 8, 12, 6
Circle the biggest number: 16, 19, 11
Write the numbers from biggest to smallest: 11, 3, 16, 20, 13, 9

subject: maths
title: Recognizing Groups of 10
concept: Counting objects in groups of ten.
explanation: Let's count objects! Look for things around you that come in groups of 10, like bindi cards. 🎉

practice_questions.q: Find things in your surroundings that are in groups of 10.

subject: maths
title: How Many Altogether?
concept: Learning to add numbers together.
explanation: Let's add! If you have 4 children and 2 children, how many children do you have altogether? 🤔

practice_questions.q: How many brothers and sisters do your parents have altogether?
How many family members do you and your friend have altogether?
How many fingers do you have in both your hands and feet?
How many numbers can you count on your fingers?

subject: maths
title: Let us Play— Addition with Dice!
concept: Using dice to practice addition.
explanation: Roll the dice and add the dots! 🎲 Let's see who scores more!

subject: maths
title: Kishore and Nitya's Game
concept: Counting how many times each player won.
explanation: Kishore and Nitya played a game with dice. Let's find out who won more times! 🎲
examples: Kishore won 3 times, Nitya won 2 times.

practice_questions.q: How many times did Kishore win?
practice_questions.a: Count the marks on the scoreboard.

practice_questions.q: How many times did Nitya win?
practice_questions.a: Count the marks on the scoreboard.

subject: maths
title: Beads and String
concept: Adding numbers using colored beads.
explanation: Color the beads and add them up! Let's see how many colored beads we have in total! 🎨

practice_questions.q: What is 3 + 4?
What is 2 + 5?

subject: maths
title: Hop and Find the Sum
concept: Finding sums by hopping.
explanation: Hop along the strip and write down the sum! Let's jump into math! 🐰

practice_questions.q: What is 4 + 2?
What is 5 + 3?

subject: maths
title: Add in Your Own Way
concept: Adding numbers in different ways.
explanation: Abdul and Rihana are adding numbers differently. Can you help them find the sums? 🤔

practice_questions.q: What is 2 + 3?
What is 9 + 1?

subject: maths
title: Shells and Marbles Count
concept: Counting total items.
explanation: Let's count how many shells and marbles we have altogether! 🐚⚽
examples: Raghav has 4 shells, Sarita has 5 shells. Total = 9 shells.

practice_questions.q: How many shells do Raghav and Sarita have?
How many marbles do Ranjeet and Meenakshi have?

subject: maths
title: Five Little Children
concept: A fun rhyme to count down.
explanation: Sing along and count the children as they step out! 🎶
text:
Five little children
Raise your hand
Here comes your stand
One steps out, with a roar
Now, we are four!
Four little children
Raise your hand
Here comes your stand
One steps out, near a tree
Now, we are three!
Three little children
Raise your hand
Here comes your stand
One steps out, with shining shoe
Now, we are two!
Two little children
Raise your hand
Here comes your stand
One steps out, looking at the sun
Now, remains one!

subject: maths
title: How Many Children Are Left?
concept: Understanding subtraction through real-life scenarios.
explanation: Let's figure out how many children are left on the bus after each stop! 🚍✨
examples: If 10 children get on the bus and 2 get off at the first stop, how many are left? 10 - 2 = 8 children left! 🎉

practice_questions.q: How many children get down from the bus on the first stand?
How many children are left in the bus after the first stop?
How many children are left at the end?

subject: maths
title: One Little Child
concept: Counting down with fun.
explanation: A fun rhyme to help us count down! 🎶
text:
One little child
Raise your hand
Here comes your stand
Last steps out,
having much fun
Now, there are none!

subject: maths
title: How Many Left?
concept: Using objects to visualize subtraction.
explanation: Let's see how many are left after some are taken away! 🍌🍌🍌
examples: If you have 6 frogs and 2 jumped away, how many are left? 6 - 2 = 4 frogs left! 🐸

practice_questions.q: 6 frogs 2 jumped away, how many left?
7 balloons flew away, how many left?
9 bananas, 6 taken away, how many left?

subject: maths
title: Fill in the Blanks
concept: Practicing subtraction with blanks.
explanation: Fill in the blanks to practice your subtraction! ✏️

practice_questions.q: 7 ladoos, 4 eaten, how many left?
7 balls, ___ taken away, ___ left?

subject: maths
title: Make Your Own Ten Dots Card
concept: Creating a card to visualize hidden and visible dots.
explanation: Make a card with 10 dots and hide some! How many can you see? 🎨

subject: maths
title: Rumi and Shami's Vegetable Farm
concept: Adding quantities of vegetables.
explanation: Let's add the vegetables Rumi and Shami collected! 🥕🍅
examples: Rumi has 7 tomatoes and Shami has 5 tomatoes. Together they have 7 + 5 = 12 tomatoes! 🍅🍅🍅

practice_questions.q: How many carrots do Rumi and Shami have together if Rumi has 9 and Shami has 4?
How many radishes do they have if both have 8?

subject: maths
title: Hop and Find the Sum
concept: Practicing addition through hopping.
explanation: Hop along the number line to find the sum! 🐾

practice_questions.q: What is 13 + 4?
What is 14 + 5?

subject: maths
title: Adding Pencils
concept: Understanding addition through counting objects.
explanation: When we want to find out how many pencils we have in total, we can add the numbers together! For example, if Sapna counted 12 pencils and then found 6 more, we add them like this: 12 + 6 = 18. 🎉

practice_questions.q: Aman has 8 laddoos and Charanjeet brought 6 laddoos. How many ladoos are there in all?
Sheena has 12 bangles, she got 6 more from her sister. How many bangles does she have now?

subject: maths
title: Subtracting Numbers
concept: Learning subtraction with simple numbers.
explanation: Subtraction is when we take away some items from a group. For example, if we have 16 and we take away 4, we can find out how many are left: 16 - 4 = 12. 🧮

practice_questions.q: What is 15 - 9?
What is 13 - 3?

subject: maths
title: Solving Word Problems
concept: Applying addition and subtraction in real-life scenarios.
explanation: Let's solve some fun problems! These stories help us use addition and subtraction in our daily life. 📚

practice_questions.q: A potter had 9 diyas. He sold 5 diyas. Then he made 7 more diyas. How many diyas does he have now?
6 children sit in the bus at the first stop. At the second stop, 8 more children board the bus. 7 children get down at the third bus stop. How many children are there in the bus at the third stop?

subject: maths
title: Lina's Family Get Together
concept: Understanding family relationships and heights.
explanation: Lina lives with her family in a village. They all gathered for a get-together! Can you find out who is the tallest and shortest in her family? 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦

practice_questions.q: How many members are there in Lina’s family?
Who is the tallest and shortest member of your family?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Length
concept: Comparing lengths using handspan and fingers.
explanation: We can measure things using our hands! A handspan is the distance from the tip of your thumb to the tip of your pinky when your hand is stretched out. Let's see how we can use it to measure different objects! ✋📏

practice_questions.q: How long is your bag in handspans?
Can you find something shorter than a handspan?

subject: maths
title: Measuring Objects
concept: Using handspan and fingers to measure various objects.
explanation: Let's measure different objects around us! You can use your handspan or fingers to see which is better for measuring. 🤔✋

practice_questions.q: Circle what you will use to measure a bottle: handspan or fingers?
What did you find out about the length of your friend's nose?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Heaviness
concept: Identifying heavier and lighter objects.
explanation: Some things are heavier than others! We can compare two objects to see which one is heavier or lighter. ⚖️
examples: Which is heavier: a rock or a feather? The rock is heavier!

practice_questions.q: Who is heavier between you and your friend?
Color the heavier object in the picture.

subject: maths
title: Measuring Water
concept: Estimating how much water fills a container.
explanation: Let's find out how many cups or jugs of water it takes to fill a bucket! 💧🧊

practice_questions.q: How many jugs of water will fill the bucket?
How many cups of water will fill your water bottle?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Water Capacity
concept: Comparing the capacity of different containers.
explanation: Let's find out how much water different bottles can hold! 💧 You can fill a bottle with cups of water and see which one holds more.
examples: How many cups of water will fill your friend’s bottle? 🥤

practice_questions.q: Which bottle holds less water?
Tick the amount of water you need for brushing teeth?

subject: maths
title: Discussing Water Importance
concept: Understanding the importance of water conservation.
explanation: Let's talk about why we should save water! 🌍 If you have leftover water in your glass, should you throw it away or give it to the plants? Let's find ways to save water together!

subject: maths
title: Packing Oranges in Boxes
concept: Understanding how to group items.
explanation: Simran is packing oranges! 🍊 Each box can hold 10 oranges. How many boxes does she need for all her oranges?
examples: If there are 22 oranges, how many boxes do we need? 22 ÷ 10 = 2 boxes and 2 oranges left over.

practice_questions.q: How many boxes would she need for 25 oranges?

subject: maths
title: Writing Numbers 21 to 30
concept: Learning to write numbers.
explanation: Let's practice writing numbers from 21 to 30! ✍️ Can you write them down?

subject: maths
title: Counting Flowers and Neem Daatun
concept: Counting objects in a fun way.
explanation: Let's count the flowers and the neem daatun! 🌼 How many do you see?

subject: maths
title: Farha's Matchbox Surprise
concept: Exploring curiosity and counting.
explanation: Farha packed more than fifty amazing things in her matchbox! 🎁 What do you think they could be? Let's find out!

subject: maths
title: Counting Numbers
concept: Learning to count from 51 to 60.
explanation: Let's count together! 📚 We will learn the numbers from 51 to 60. Each number has a special name!

subject: maths
title: Counting Numbers
concept: Learning to count from 61 to 80.
explanation: Let's keep counting! 🚀 Now we will learn the numbers from 61 to 80. Each number is fun to say!

subject: maths
title: Counting Numbers
concept: Learning to count from 81 to 100.
explanation: We're almost there! 🎉 Let's learn the numbers from 81 to 100. Each number is a step closer to 100!

subject: maths
title: Make a Slide and Ladder Game
concept: Creating a fun game to practice numbers.
explanation: Let's play a game! 🎲 You can make a slide and ladder game using numbers. Fill in the missing numbers and have fun!

subject: maths
title: Make Your Own Warli Drawing
concept: Creating art inspired by Warli painting.
explanation: Let's get creative! 🎨 You can make your own Warli drawing. Use shapes and patterns to tell a story!

subject: maths
title: Discuss Local Festivals
concept: Talking about local festivals and their significance.
explanation: Let's talk about festivals! 🎉 What festivals do you celebrate with your family? Each festival has its own fun and stories!

subject: maths
title: Creating Patterns
concept: Understanding and creating patterns using colors and shapes.
explanation: Patterns are sequences that repeat. You can create your own patterns using colors or shapes! 🎨✨

practice_questions.q: What colour should come after pink and why?

subject: maths
title: Complete the Pattern
concept: Extending given patterns by coloring.
explanation: Look at the patterns and see what comes next! Can you color them in? 🖍️

subject: maths
title: Study the Pattern
concept: Filling in missing numbers in a pattern.
explanation: Can you find the missing numbers in the pattern? Let's fill them in! 🔢

subject: maths
title: Explore Patterns
concept: Identifying patterns in nature and everyday objects.
explanation: Look around you! Patterns are everywhere in nature. Can you find them? 🌿🐾

subject: maths
title: Create Your Own Patterns
concept: Creating patterns using various objects.
explanation: Gather items like pebbles, flowers, or sticks and arrange them in a pattern! 🌼🪨

subject: maths
title: Pihu's Daily Routine
concept: Understanding daily activities and their sequence.
explanation: Pihu shares her daily routine from morning to night! 🌞🌜

subject: maths
title: Tick the Activities
concept: Identifying daily activities.
explanation: Tick the activities you do in the morning and at night! ✅🌅

practice_questions.q: What is your daily routine?
When do you brush your teeth?

subject: maths
title: Guess the Time of Day
concept: Identifying different times of the day.
explanation: Look at the pictures and guess what time of day it is! 🕒🌅

subject: maths
title: Match the Objects with the Season
concept: Understanding seasons through matching objects.
explanation: Can you match the objects with the correct season? 🌸❄️☀️🌧️

subject: maths
title: Understanding Seasons
concept: Identifying and distinguishing between different seasons.
explanation: There are four main seasons: summer, winter, spring, and monsoon. Each season has its own unique weather and activities. 🌞❄️🌸🌧️

practice_questions.q: What do you like to eat in the summer season?
What do you like to wear in the winter season?

subject: maths
title: Seasonal Food Art
concept: Drawing favorite foods for each season.
explanation: Draw or stick pictures of your favorite food items during different seasons! 🍉🍂🍰

subject: maths
title: A Visit to an Amusement Park
concept: Counting children and rides at an amusement park.
explanation: A group of children went to an amusement park and had fun on different rides! 🎢 Let's count how many can sit together.
examples: 2 children can sit on a horse. 🐴
3 children can sit in a toy train. 🚂

practice_questions.q: How many children are there in total?
How many children can sit in each compartment?

subject: maths
title: Counting Jalebis
concept: Calculating total pieces of jalebi.
explanation: Each plate of jalebi has 3 pieces. If we order 6 plates, how many pieces do we have? 🍽️

subject: maths
title: How Much Can We Spend?
concept: Understanding coins and notes.
explanation: Riya and Sahil are counting their coins and notes. Let's learn about money! 💰

practice_questions.q: If a ball costs ₹20, how many ₹5 coins does Riya need?
What toys can you buy for ₹10?

subject: maths
title: Shopping Game
concept: Playing a shopping game using pretend money.
explanation: Let's play a shopping game! Some children will be shopkeepers and others will be customers. Use play money to buy things! 🛒

subject: maths
title: Saving Money
concept: Understanding the importance of saving money.
explanation: Saving money is a good habit! 💰 Let's learn how to save and manage our money wisely.
examples: Try making your own gullak (piggy bank) with a spare box! 🐷

practice_questions.q: Why is saving money important?
practice_questions.a: It helps us buy things we want later!

subject: maths
title: So Many Toys
concept: Comparing the number of different toys.
explanation: Let's compare how many toys we have! 🎲 We can use words like more than, less than, or equal to.
examples: The number of dolls is more than the number of cars.

practice_questions.q: The number of dolls is ______ the number of cars.
The number of teddy bears is ______ the number of elephants.

subject: maths
title: Colourful Flowers
concept: Identifying and comparing flower colors.
explanation: Flowers come in many colors! 🌼 Let's find out which colors are most and least common in a garden.
examples: Blue flowers, Orange flowers, Red flowers, Purple flowers.

practice_questions.q: Name the color of flowers which are least in number.
Name the color of the flowers which are most in number.

subject: maths
title: Counting Logs
concept: Counting objects and arranging them.
explanation: Let's count how many logs we see! 🌳 Is it 3 logs or 4 logs?

practice_questions.q: How many logs do you see?

subject: maths
title: Who am I?
concept: Solving number riddles.
explanation: Can you guess the number based on the clues? 🤔 Let's solve some fun riddles!

practice_questions.q: I am between 5 and 10. I become three more when read upside down. Who am I?
I am 3 more than 8 and 3 less than 14. Who am I?

subject: maths
title: Creating Numbers with Matchsticks
concept: Using matchsticks to form numbers.
explanation: You can make different numbers by shifting matchsticks! For example, if you have a zero (0) made of matchsticks, can you change it to another number like 6 or 8? 🤔
examples: Can you make a 1 from a 0? 🔢
What about making a 9 from a 6?

practice_questions.q: Can you make any other number by shifting a single matchstick?
practice_questions.a: Yes, you can create different numbers!

subject: maths
title: Subtracting from 25
concept: Understanding subtraction.
explanation: When we subtract, we take away a number from another. If you have 25 apples 🍏 and you eat 5, how many do you have left? Let's find out!

practice_questions.q: How many times can you subtract 5 from 25?

subject: maths
title: Ranu's Seeds
concept: Finding combinations that add up to a specific number.
explanation: Ranu has 3 seeds 🌱 and she wants to place them on numbers that add up to 17. Can you help her find those numbers? Let's explore together!
examples: If you place seeds on 5, 6, and 6, they add up to 17!

practice_questions.q: Can you help Ranu find numbers that sum to 17?
practice_questions.a: Yes! You can use combinations like 8, 9.

subject: maths
title: Identical Shadows
concept: Identifying identical images.
explanation: Can you find the shadow that looks exactly like the original? 🌑 Let's encircle the identical shadow image!

practice_questions.q: Encircle the identical shadow image.
practice_questions.a: Look for the one that matches!

subject: maths
title: Arranging Numbers
concept: Arranging numbers to achieve equal sums.
explanation: Can you write the numbers from 5 to 9 in a way that the rows and columns add up to the same total? Let's see how clever you are! 🧠

practice_questions.q: Write numbers from 5 to 9 in the card given below.
practice_questions.a: Make sure the rows and columns add up!

subject: maths
title: Minimum Coins for Spending
concept: Finding the minimum number of coins to reach a value.
explanation: Sarita has four coins of different values. Can you help her find the minimum number of coins to spend exactly 49? 💰 Let's solve this puzzle!
examples: If she has coins of 20, 10, 5, and 1, how many does she need?

practice_questions.q: What is the minimum number of coins used to spend exactly 49?
practice_questions.a: You can use a combination of coins!

subject: maths
title: Choosing Balls for Scores
concept: Selecting items to achieve specific scores.
explanation: Let's play a ball game! 🎾 Choose 3 balls so that their sum is 15. Can you find the right combination? Let's have fun!
examples: If you choose balls of 5, 5, and 5, they add up to 15!

practice_questions.q: Choose 3 balls in such a way that their sum will be 15.

subject: maths
title: Understanding Numbers
concept: Recognizing and counting numbers.
explanation: Let's learn about numbers! Numbers help us count things around us. For example, if you have 🍎🍎🍎, you have 3 apples!

practice_questions.q: How many apples are there? 🍎🍎🍎
Count the bananas: 🍌🍌

subject: maths
title: Simple Addition
concept: Adding two numbers together.
explanation: Addition means putting things together! If you have 🍎🍎 and I give you 🍎, how many do you have now? That's right! 🍎🍎🍎 - 3 apples!

practice_questions.q: What is 1 + 1?
What is 2 + 2?

subject: maths
title: Addition Exercises
concept: Practice adding numbers.
explanation: Let's practice our addition! Can you solve these? 🤔

practice_questions.q: What is 3 + 1?
What is 2 + 3?
What is 5 + 0?

subject: maths
title: Counting Fun
concept: A fun rhyme to help with counting.
explanation: Let's sing a counting rhyme! 🎶
text:
One, two, buckle my shoe, 
Three, four, knock at the door, 
Five, six, pick up sticks, 
Seven, eight, lay them straight, 
Nine, ten, a big fat hen!

subject: maths
title: Counting with Joy
concept: Understanding numbers through counting objects.
explanation: Let's count some fun objects! How many apples do you see? 🍎🍎🍎 That's right, 3 apples!

practice_questions.q: How many stars do you see? ⭐⭐⭐

subject: maths
title: Adding Fun!
concept: Learning to add numbers using objects.
explanation: If you have 2 apples 🍎🍎 and I give you 1 more 🍎, how many do you have now? Let's add them up! 2 + 1 = 3!

practice_questions.q: What is 1 + 2?

subject: maths
title: Subtracting with Smiles!
concept: Learning to subtract using visual aids.
explanation: If you have 5 balloons 🎈🎈🎈🎈🎈 and 2 fly away 🎈🎈, how many do you have left? Let's subtract! 5 - 2 = 3!

practice_questions.q: What is 4 - 1?

subject: maths
title: Count the Fruits
concept: Count different fruits and write the number.
explanation: Look at the fruits and count them! 🍏🍏🍏 How many green apples do you see?

practice_questions.q: Count the oranges 🍊🍊🍊🍊. How many are there?

subject: maths
title: Getting to Know Numbers
concept: Understanding numbers through play.
explanation: Let's explore numbers by counting fun things around us! 🎉 For example, how many apples 🍎 do you see? Let's count together!

practice_questions.q: How many stars ⭐ do you see?

subject: maths
title: Learning About Shapes
concept: Identifying different shapes.
explanation: Shapes are everywhere! Let's find them! 🔺 Can you spot a circle 🍩 or a square 📦 in your room?

practice_questions.q: What shape is this? 🔺

subject: maths
title: Finding the Furry Cat!
concept: A fun poem to engage with numbers.
explanation: Let's enjoy a poem about a cat! 🐱
content:
Finding the furry cat, oh so sly, 
How many paws does she have? Let's count, oh my! 
One, two, three, four, she runs so fast, 
With her fluffy tail, she’s a joy to have! 🐾

subject: maths
title: Chhuk Chhuk Goes Our Train
concept: A playful rhyme about trains and counting.
explanation: Let's sing a fun train song! 🚂
content:
Chhuk chhuk goes our train, 
Counting all the cars, what a gain! 
One, two, three, four, five, 
All aboard, let’s take a ride! 🚆

subject: maths
title: Playing Math Games
concept: Learning math through games.
explanation: Games make learning fun! 🎲 Let's play a game where we count how many times we can jump! How many jumps can you do?

practice_questions.q: Can you jump 10 times? How many did you do?

subject: maths
title: Count and Color
concept: Counting objects and coloring them.
explanation: Let's count and color! 🎨 How many butterflies 🦋 can you see? Color them in!

subject: maths
title: Understanding Numbers
concept: Numbers help us count and measure things.
explanation: Numbers are like magic keys that help us count things around us! For example, if you have 3 apples 🍎🍎🍎, you can say you have three.

practice_questions.q: How many oranges 🍊 are there if you have 2?

subject: maths
title: Five Little Children
narrative: Once upon a time, there were five little children who loved to play together. They counted their toys, their snacks, and even the stars in the sky. One day, they decided to have a counting game. They counted 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and had so much fun! 🌟

subject: maths
title: Adding Numbers
concept: Addition is putting together two or more numbers.
explanation: When we add, we are putting things together! If you have 2 apples 🍎🍎 and your friend gives you 1 more 🍎, now you have 3 apples! 🍎🍎🍎

practice_questions.q: What is 1 + 1?

subject: maths
title: Counting Fun
text:
One, two, buckle my shoe, 
Three, four, shut the door, 
Five, six, pick up sticks, 
Seven, eight, lay them straight, 
Nine, ten, let's count again! 🎉

subject: maths
title: Taking Away
concept: Subtraction is taking away from a group.
explanation: When we subtract, we take away! If you have 5 candies 🍬🍬🍬🍬🍬 and you eat 2 🍬🍬, you have 3 left! 🍬🍬🍬

practice_questions.q: What is 3 - 1?

subject: maths
title: Finding the Furry Cat!
concept: Understanding basic counting and recognition of numbers.
explanation: Let's find the furry cat by counting! 🐱 How many cats can you see in the picture? Let's count together! 1, 2, 3... 🐾

practice_questions.q: How many cats are there?

subject: maths
title: What is Long? What is Round?
concept: Identifying different shapes and their properties.
explanation: Shapes are all around us! 🌟 A long shape is like a pencil ✏️, and a round shape is like a ball ⚽. Can you find these shapes in your room?

practice_questions.q: What shape is a pizza?

subject: maths
title: Mango Treat
concept: Counting and recognizing numbers from 1 to 9.
explanation: Let's count the mangoes! 🥭 How many mangoes do you see? Let's count together! 1, 2, 3... 🍑

practice_questions.q: How many mangoes are there?

subject: maths
title: Making 10
concept: Understanding how to make the number 10 using addition.
explanation: Let's make 10! 🎉 If you have 5 apples 🍏 and I give you 5 more, how many do you have? 5 + 5 = 10! 🍏🍏🍏🍏🍏 + 🍏🍏🍏🍏🍏 = 10!

practice_questions.q: What is 7 + 3?

subject: maths
topic: Addition and Subtraction of Single Digit Numbers
title: How Many?
concept: Learning basic addition and subtraction with single-digit numbers.
explanation: Let's play with numbers! If you have 3 candies 🍬 and you eat 1, how many are left? 3 - 1 = 2! 🍬🍬

practice_questions.q: What is 4 + 2?

subject: maths
title: Vegetable Farm
concept: Adding and subtracting numbers up to 20.
explanation: Welcome to the vegetable farm! 🥕 If you have 10 carrots and you buy 5 more, how many do you have now? 10 + 5 = 15! 🥕🥕🥕

practice_questions.q: What is 15 - 5?

subject: maths
title: Lina’s Family
concept: Understanding measurement through everyday examples.
explanation: Let's measure Lina's family! 📏 How tall is Lina? If Lina is as tall as 3 books 📚 stacked, how tall is that? Let's measure together!

practice_questions.q: How tall is your favorite toy?

subject: maths
title: Fun with Numbers
concept: Recognizing and counting numbers from 21 to 99.
explanation: Let's have fun with numbers! 🎈 Can you count from 21 to 30? 21, 22, 23... 🎉

practice_questions.q: What comes after 29?

subject: maths
title: Utsav
concept: Identifying and creating patterns.
explanation: Let's create patterns! 🌈 If you have a red ball 🔴, a blue ball 🔵, and a red ball again, what comes next? Yes, a blue ball! 🔵

practice_questions.q: What comes next in the pattern: 🔴🔵🔴?

subject: maths
title: How do I Spend my Day?
concept: Understanding the concept of time and daily activities.
explanation: What time do you wake up? ⏰ Let's talk about our daily activities! I wake up at 7 AM and have breakfast at 8 AM. What about you?

practice_questions.q: What time do you go to bed?

subject: maths
title: How Many Times?
concept: Introduction to the concept of multiplication.
explanation: Let's learn about multiplication! If you have 2 bags with 3 apples each 🍏, how many apples do you have in total? 2 x 3 = 6! 🍏🍏🍏🍏🍏🍏

practice_questions.q: What is 3 x 2?

subject: maths
title: How Much Can We Spend?
concept: Understanding money and basic transactions.
explanation: Let's play shop! 🛒 If you have 10 coins 🪙 and you buy a toy for 5 coins, how many coins do you have left? 10 - 5 = 5! 🪙

practice_questions.q: If you have 20 coins and buy a book for 15 coins, how much do you have left?

subject: maths
title: So Many Toys
concept: Collecting and interpreting data.
explanation: Let's count our toys! 🧸 If you have 5 teddy bears and 3 cars, how many toys do you have in total? 5 + 3 = 8! 🧸🚗

practice_questions.q: How many toys do you have?

subject: maths
title: Puzzles
concept: Fun puzzles to reinforce learning.
explanation: Let's solve some fun puzzles! 🧩 Can you find the missing number? 1, 2, __, 4, 5. What number is missing? It's 3! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What comes next: 5, 6, __?

subject: maths
title: Learning to Count
concept: Counting numbers from 1 to 10.
explanation: Counting is like a fun game! Let's count some apples together! 🍎🍎🍎

practice_questions.q: How many apples are there? 🍎🍎🍎?

subject: maths
title: Adding Numbers
concept: Adding two numbers together.
explanation: When we add, we put things together! If you have 2 🍎 and I give you 3 🍎, how many do you have now?

practice_questions.q: What is 1 🍎 + 1 🍎?

subject: maths
title: Five Little Ducks
concept: Counting down with a fun rhyme.
explanation: Let's sing a fun rhyme while we count! 🎶

subject: maths
title: The Adventure of Numberland
concept: Understanding numbers through a story.
explanation: Join us on an adventure in Numberland where numbers come to life! 🌟

subject: maths
title: Taking Away
concept: Understanding subtraction by taking away.
explanation: Subtraction is like taking away! If you have 5 🍎 and you eat 2 🍎, how many are left?

practice_questions.q: If you have 4 🍎 and give away 1 🍎, how many do you have left?

subject: english
title: Understanding Bicycles
concept: Learning about bicycles and their parts.
explanation: A bicycle is a fun way to move around! It has parts like wheels, pedals, and a bell. 🚲

practice_questions.q: What colour is the seat of the bicycle?
What do you need to keep the bicycle moving?

subject: english
title: Bicycle Rhyme
content:
I have a red bicycle.
I pedal and I ride.
Trin... trin... trin... goes the bell.
Makes my friends move aside.
The seat is blue in colour,
The tyres and pedals are black.
Sometimes fast, sometimes slow,
But my bicycle is always on track.
To keep my bicycle moving,
All I need is some air.
I put on my little helmet,
And go happily everywhere.

subject: english
title: Identify Bicycle Parts
concept: Recognizing different parts of a bicycle.
explanation: Can you name the parts of a bicycle? Let's write them down! 📝

subject: english
title: Imagine Your Magic Bicycle
concept: Creative thinking about a magic bicycle.
explanation: Imagine if you had a magic bicycle! Where would you go? 🌈

subject: english
