title: Fill in the Blanks
concept: Practicing sentence completion.
explanation: Let's complete the sentences about the bicycle! ✏️

practice_questions.q: The child in the poem has a __________ bicycle.
The sound of the _______ makes people move aside.
The __________ and __________ are black.

subject: english
title: Understanding Pictures
concept: Learning to observe and describe pictures.
explanation: Look at the picture and answer questions about it. What do you see? How many children are there? What are they doing? 🤔
examples: What do you see in the picture?
How are the children having fun?

practice_questions.q: How many children are there in the picture?
practice_questions.a: Count the children in the picture.

practice_questions.q: What games do you play?

subject: english
title: OUT! OUT!
concept: Children playing games during a holiday.
explanation: Jeet and Babli are having fun playing games like skipping rope and hide and seek. They even made their own ball! 🎉
narrative: It was a holiday. Jeet and Babli were playing. They played many games. Both of them skipped the rope. They played hide and seek. Babli and Jeet decided to play ‘bat and ball’. Jeet got ready to throw the ball. Jeet threw the ball. Babli hit the ball. The ball went into Mohit’s garden. But his house was locked. They could not find the ball. So the game stopped. This time Babli picked the ball. Jeet picked the bat. Babli threw the ball. Jeet hit the ball hard. The ball opened up and scattered in the air! Babli jumped and caught a piece of rag. She jumped and shouted, “OUT! OUT!” Babli knew how to make a ball. They got a few rags, paper, and wool. Babli also brought a piece of string. She rolled rags, paper, and wool to make a ball. Then she tied the ball with a string. They liked the new ball. The game started again.

subject: english
title: Questions about the Story
concept: Reflecting on the story through questions.
explanation: Let's think about the story and answer some questions together! 🤔

practice_questions.q: What games do you like to play with your friends?
Have you played ‘bat and ball’?

subject: english
title: Sequence the Story
concept: Understanding the order of events.
explanation: Let's put the sentences in the right order to tell the story correctly! 📚

practice_questions.q: Number the following sentences in their correct order.
practice_questions.a: 1. Jeet got ready to throw the ball. 2. Jeet threw the ball. 3. Babli hit the ball. 4. The ball went into Mohit’s garden. 5. They lost the ball.

subject: english
title: Completing the Story
concept: Practicing writing skills.
explanation: Fill in the blanks to complete the story! 📝

practice_questions.q: Write the names of the children in the story.
Write the items that the children used to make a ball.

subject: english
title: Finding Opposites
concept: Learning about antonyms.
explanation: Let's find the opposite words! Can you think of what they are? 🔄

practice_questions.q: Write the opposites of the following words.
practice_questions.a: 1. up - down, 2. in - out, 3. open - closed, 4. fat - thin, 5. hot - cold, 6. small - big

subject: english
title: Gopal Goes to School
concept: Understanding daily activities.
explanation: Gopal is a boy who goes to school every day. Just like Gopal, you also go to school to learn new things! 📚
examples: I go to school every day.

subject: english
title: Liking Fruits
concept: Expressing preferences.
explanation: We all have favorite fruits! For example, I like to eat mangoes and grapes. 🍇🥭 What about you?

subject: english
title: Animals on the Mat
concept: Identifying animals and their actions.
explanation: Cats and dogs are common pets. They can sit on the mat just like we can sit on a chair! 🐱🐶
examples: The cat sat on the mat.
The dog sat on the mat.

subject: english
title: Describing a Ball
concept: Using adjectives to describe objects.
explanation: This ball has yellow and green dots. We can describe objects using colors! ⚽️🌈
examples: This ball has yellow dots on it.
This ball also has green dots on it.

subject: english
title: Classroom Objects
concept: Identifying items in a classroom.
explanation: In our classroom, we have tables and chairs. These are important for learning! 🪑📚
examples: There are tables in my classroom.
There are chairs in my classroom.

subject: english
title: Enact the Story
concept: Engaging in role play to enhance understanding.
explanation: Choose a partner and act out the story of Babli and Jeet! Use your imagination! 🎭

subject: english
title: Learn About Childhood Games
concept: Connecting with family through stories.
explanation: Ask your grandparents or parents about games they played when they were young. Share it in class! 🕹️👵👴

subject: english
title: It’s Fun to Be This
concept: Exploring fun activities through a poem.
explanation: This poem talks about fun things we can do like jumping and climbing! Let's read it together! 🌟
text:
It’s fun to be this,
It’s fun to be that,
To leap like a lamb,
To climb like a cat.
To hop like a frog,
To swim like a fish,
To trot like a horse,
To fly like a bird.
I can act
Just like them all,
It’s fun to be this,
It’s fun to be that.

subject: english
title: Guess the Animal Sounds
concept: Identifying animal sounds.
explanation: Listen to the sounds and guess which animal makes them! 🐸🐔🐦

subject: english
title: If You Were an Animal
concept: Imagining actions of animals.
explanation: If you were a cat, what would you do? Let's share our answers! 🐱💭

practice_questions.q: If you were a cat, what would you do?
practice_questions.a: I would run after a mouse.

practice_questions.q: If you meet an elephant, what would you say?

subject: english
title: What Can You Do Like an Animal?
concept: Expressing abilities related to animals.
explanation: What can you do like any animal? Let's share our talents! 🦁✨

practice_questions.q: What can you do like any animal or bird?
practice_questions.a: I can jump like a frog.

practice_questions.q: Name any two animals or birds that can swim?
Name any two animals that live on trees?

subject: english
title: Make Sentences
concept: Creating sentences using new words.
explanation: Let's make sentences with the words we learned! For example, 'Monkeys climb trees.' 🐒🌳

subject: english
title: Describe the Picture
concept: Writing about visual stimuli.
explanation: Look at the picture and write a few sentences about what you see! 🖼️✍️

subject: english
title: Matching Animals to Actions
concept: Animals have different actions.
explanation: Let's learn what different animals do! 🐴🐑🐍🐿️🐒

practice_questions.q: What does a horse do?
What does a monkey do?

subject: english
title: Song of I
concept: Understanding the letter and word 'I'.
explanation: The letter 'I' is special because it is both a letter and a word! 🎶

subject: english
title: Song of I
concept: Understanding the letter and word 'I'.
explanation: The letter 'I' is special because it is both a letter and a word! 🎶

subject: english
title: Rangoli Art
concept: Creating art through drawing.
explanation: Let's draw a beautiful rangoli! 🎨✨ Follow the steps to make it colorful.

subject: english
title: Onshangla's New Friend
concept: Understanding challenges faced by differently abled individuals.
explanation: Onshangla learns about a new boy who cannot see and plays a fun game with her mother. 🌹🎉

subject: english
title: Finding Out Without Seeing
concept: Children can learn through different senses.
explanation: Even if you can't see, you can learn about things using your other senses! For example, you can smell flowers or hear sounds to understand your surroundings. 🌼👂
examples: Identifying a flower by its smell
Finding out where a sound is coming from

subject: english
title: Close Your Eyes and Identify
concept: Using senses to identify objects.
explanation: Try to identify things without looking! Close your eyes and think about how you would recognize these items.

practice_questions.q: How would you identify water?
practice_questions.a: By feeling its temperature or listening to its sound.

practice_questions.q: How would you identify a flower?

subject: english
title: Let Us Draw
concept: Expressing preferences through art.
explanation: Draw things you love! Sharing your drawings with friends is fun and helps you express yourself. 🎨😊

subject: english
title: Activities with Family
concept: Writing about family activities.
explanation: Think about fun things you do with your family and write them down! It helps you remember special moments. 🏡❤️
examples: I like to walk with my grandfather.

practice_questions.q: What do you like to do with your family?
practice_questions.a: I like to play games with my sister.

subject: english
title: Things I Can Do By Myself
concept: Recognizing personal independence.
explanation: List things you can do all by yourself! It shows how you are growing up. 🌱✨
examples: I can take a bath by myself.

subject: english
title: Taking Care of Our Eyes
concept: Understanding eye health.
explanation: Our eyes are important! Let's learn what is good and not good for them. 👀💖

practice_questions.q: What should you do for your eyes?
What is not good for your eyes?
practice_questions.a: Sitting too close to the TV.

subject: english
title: Eye Movements
concept: Practicing eye movements for health.
explanation: Let's exercise our eyes! Follow the teacher's instructions to keep your eyes healthy. 🏋️‍♂️👁️

subject: english
title: Learning Vowel Sounds
concept: Identifying vowel sounds in words.
explanation: Let's learn about the vowel sound 'o'! Words like 'ox' and 'orange' start with this sound. 🐂🍊

subject: english
title: Going Places
concept: Different modes of transportation.
explanation: Let's have fun with this rhyme about traveling! 🚍✈️
text:
Take a bus 
Or take a train,
Take a boat 
Or take a plane,
Take a taxi, 
Take a car,
Maybe near 
Or maybe far,

subject: english
title: Talking About Travel
concept: Sharing travel experiences.
explanation: Let's talk about our travel experiences! Where have you been? What did you see? 🌍🍕

practice_questions.q: Have you ever traveled by bus or train?
practice_questions.a: Yes, I went to the beach!

subject: english
title: Exploring Vehicles
concept: Understanding different means of transport.
explanation: Vehicles help us travel from one place to another. They can be on land, in the air, or on water. 🚗✈️🚢

subject: english
title: Take a Rocket
concept: A fun rhyme about traveling to the moon.
explanation: This rhyme encourages imagination and fun with travel! 🌙🚀
text:
Take a rocket
To the moon,
But be sure
To come back soon.

subject: english
title: Draw Your Favorite Vehicle
concept: Encouraging creativity through drawing.
explanation: Draw the vehicle that you like the most! It can be real or imaginary. 🎨🚗

subject: english
title: Sort the Vehicles
concept: Classifying vehicles based on their movement.
explanation: Sort the vehicles into land, air, and water categories. 🚗✈️🚢

subject: english
title: Vowel Sound ‘u’
concept: Learning the vowel sound 'u'.
explanation: The vowel sound 'u' is found in words like 'hut' and 'umbrella'. Let's practice! 🏠☂️

subject: english
title: Ravi's Journey
concept: A story about a fun journey to school.
explanation: Ravi and his friend have an exciting adventure on their way to school! 🏫🌳
text: Hello, I am Ravi. I study in Class 2. My friend and I go to school on foot. We pass through the paddy fields, the mango grove and then reach the main road. That is the shortest route we take to school. What fun we have every day!

subject: english
title: Describe Your Journey
concept: Writing about personal experiences.
explanation: Write about the interesting things you see on your way to school! 📝🌼

practice_questions.q: What interesting things do you see on your way to school?
practice_questions.a: I see trees, birds, and sometimes a dog.

practice_questions.q: Ask your family about a long journey they had.
practice_questions.a: They went to the mountains and used a car.

subject: english
title: This is My Town
concept: Understanding the structure of a town and its components.
explanation: In my town, there are many streets, houses, and rooms. Let's explore what we can find in each place! 🏡🚶‍♂️
examples: In my town, there are streets.
In my street, there are houses.

practice_questions.q: What do you see in the picture?
practice_questions.a: Describe the elements in the picture.

practice_questions.q: What is the name of your town/city?
What is the name of your street?

subject: english
title: Rearranging Jumbled Letters
concept: Forming words from jumbled letters.
explanation: Let's have fun rearranging letters to make words! 🅰️🔤

subject: english
title: Make Sentences
concept: Creating sentences from given words.
explanation: Use the words to make your own sentences! 📝✨

practice_questions.q: Make a sentence using the word 'tree'.
Make a sentence using the word 'lizard'.

subject: english
title: Poem about My Town
concept: Understanding the beauty of my town through a poem.
explanation: Let's enjoy this lovely poem about the things in our town! 🌸🏙️
text:
This is my town.
In my town,
There are streets.
In my street,
There are houses.
In my house,
There are rooms.
In my room,
There is a bed.
On the bed,
There is a basket.
In the basket,
There are some flowers.
Flowers in a basket.
Basket on the bed.
Bed in the room.
Room in the house.
House in the street.
Street in the town.

subject: english
title: Write Your Home Address
concept: Learning to write personal information.
explanation: Let's practice writing our home address! 🏠✍️

subject: english
title: Listen and Encircle
concept: Improving listening skills through word recognition.
explanation: Listen carefully to the words and encircle them! 👂🔍

subject: english
title: Spot the Differences
concept: Enhancing observation skills through comparison.
explanation: Look closely at the pictures and find the differences! 🔍👀

subject: english
title: Complete the Picture
concept: Encouraging creativity through drawing.
explanation: Use your imagination to add to the picture! 🎨🖌️

subject: english
title: A Show of Clouds
concept: Observing clouds and imagination.
explanation: When we look up at the sky, we can see different shapes in the clouds. It's fun to imagine what they look like! ☁️✨

practice_questions.q: What do you see in the sky during the day?
practice_questions.a: I see the sun ☀️, birds 🐦, and clouds ☁️.

practice_questions.q: What do you see in the sky at night?
practice_questions.a: I see stars ⭐ and the moon 🌙.

subject: english
title: Cloud Poem
content:
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
I see a big white bear,
Looking down at me.
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
I see a ship sailing,
On a white sea.
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
I see an elephant,
Waving its trunk at me.
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
Oh! It’s fun, it’s fun to me,
To see whatever I want to see.

subject: english
title: Cloud Watching Activity
concept: Engaging with nature.
explanation: Sit outside and watch the clouds. What shapes do you see? Share your observations with your class! 🌤️

practice_questions.q: What did you see in the clouds?
What colors can clouds be?
practice_questions.a: Clouds can be white, grey, or even pink at sunset! 🌅

subject: english
title: Common Sounds
concept: Identifying sounds in words.
explanation: Let's listen to words that have the same sound! Can you hear the 'ck' sound? 🗣️

subject: english
title: A Curious Fly
content: The fly said, “Dear tree, do you know my name?” The tree said, “I don’t know. You may ask the cow sleeping in my shade.”

subject: english
title: Remembering Names
concept: Understanding the importance of names and identity.
explanation: Names are special! They help us know who we are. Just like the little fly who forgot his name and asked others for help! 🐝✨
examples: The fly asked the ant, 'Do you know my name?'
The cow suggested asking the grass.

practice_questions.q: How did the fly find his name?
practice_questions.a: The fly remembered his name when he saw the leaf flying.

practice_questions.q: If you forget your name, whom would you ask?
practice_questions.a: I would ask my friends or family.

subject: english
title: The Fly Who Forgot His Name
concept: A story about a fly who forgets his name and seeks help.
explanation: Once there was a little fly who couldn't remember his name! He asked an ant, a cow, and grass, but they didn't know. Finally, he remembered his name when he saw a leaf flying! 🐞🍃

subject: english
title: Animal Sounds
concept: Learning to recognize and imitate animal sounds.
explanation: Can you make sounds like animals? Let's have fun! 🐶🐱

subject: english
title: What Do You Hear?
concept: Practicing listening skills by identifying sounds.
explanation: Close your eyes and listen carefully. What sounds can you hear? 🌳👂

subject: english
title: Fill in the Blanks
concept: Completing sentences based on the story.
explanation: Let's fill in the blanks using words from the story! 📝

subject: english
title: Complete the Story
concept: Using creativity to finish a story.
explanation: Can you help the cat and cow finish their conversation? 🐱🐄

subject: english
title: What Does Your Name Mean?
concept: Exploring the meanings of names.
explanation: What is the meaning of your name? Let's find out together! 🤔💖

subject: english
title: Using Descriptive Words
concept: Construct sentences using given words.
explanation: Let's create sentences using the words we have! For example, 'Rohit has a blue blanket.' Now you try!

subject: english
title: The Crow's Beauty
concept: Understanding beauty and self-acceptance.
explanation: This poem tells the story of a crow who wants to be beautiful like a peacock. It teaches us that true beauty comes from within! 🌈
text:
There once was a crow.
He was black, as you know.
How to be beautiful,
he wanted to know.
Colourful feathers,
he wanted to grow.
He found one peacock
feather.
He stuck it in his tail.
Then he found another,
He picked up all without fail.
He stuck them
in his wings,
Put one on his head.
I am now beautiful,
He danced and said.
The other crows
laughed at him.
“Oh, what a fool you are!
You are beautiful in black.
We love you for
what you are.”

subject: english
title: Identifying Birds
concept: Recognizing different birds.
explanation: Can you identify the birds in the box? Write their names in the space provided! 🐦

subject: english
title: My Favourite Bird
concept: Expressing creativity through drawing.
explanation: Draw your favorite bird and write its name! Let your imagination fly! 🎨

subject: english
title: Clapping for Syllables
concept: Learning to break words into syllables.
explanation: Close your eyes and listen to the words. Clap for each syllable! It makes learning fun! 👏

subject: english
title: Find the Rhymes
concept: Identifying rhyming words.
explanation: Match the words in Column A with rhyming words in Column B. Let's have some fun with rhymes! 🎶

subject: english
title: Making Sentences
concept: Creating sentences with given words.
explanation: Use one word from each box to make sentences. Let's see what you can come up with! 📝

subject: english
title: Anju and Farida's Market Adventure
concept: A story about Anju and Farida's trip to the market and a lesson on cleanliness.
explanation: Anju and Farida went to the market where they bought some snacks. They learned an important lesson about keeping the environment clean when a monkey showed them how to throw trash in the dustbin. 🌳🐒

subject: english
title: Learning New Words
concept: Understanding new vocabulary related to the story.
explanation: Let's learn some new words from the story! These words will help us understand better. 📚✨

subject: english
title: Discussion Questions
concept: Questions to encourage discussion about the story.
explanation: Let's think about what we learned from the story! 🤔💭

practice_questions.q: Have you seen people throwing packets or bottles in the park or on the road?
After having biscuits, what do you do with the wrapper?
Imagine you are sitting under a tree. What do you see on the tree?
Is there a dustbin in your school? Do you know where it is?

subject: english
title: Syllable Clapping Activity
concept: An activity to practice listening and syllable recognition.
explanation: Close your eyes and listen to your teacher say your name. Clap for each syllable in your name! How many times did you clap? 👏🎉

subject: english
title: Complete the Sentences
concept: Practice writing by completing sentences from the story.
explanation: Fill in the blanks with the right words to complete the sentences! 📝✏️

practice_questions.q: Farida and Anju sat on ________________________ under the tree to rest.
There was a monkey sitting ________________________ of the tree.
The two girls had a ________________________ and a bottle of juice.

subject: english
title: Identify True or False Sentences
concept: Practice identifying correct and incorrect sentences.
explanation: Read the sentences and put a check (✔️) for correct ones and a cross (❌) for incorrect ones! 📖✅❌

practice_questions.q: Anju and Farida went to their school.
Anju bought a bag and Farida bought a water bottle.
There was a bird on the branch of the tree.
The monkey had a banana.
The monkey threw the banana peel in the dustbin.

subject: english
title: Share Your Shopping Experience
concept: Encouraging students to share their shopping experiences.
explanation: Have you ever gone to a nearby shop with your family? Share your experiences with the class! You can also draw or paste pictures of things you liked in the shop. 🛍️🖼️

subject: english
title: Using Blends in Sentences
concept: Creating sentences with given words.
explanation: Let's use words that start with blends! For example, 'There is a clock in the classroom.' Now, you can create your own sentences using the words below.
examples: There is a cloud in the sky.

subject: english
title: Little Drops of Water
concept: Understanding the importance of small things.
explanation: This poem teaches us that little things, like drops of water and acts of kindness, can make a big difference! 🌊❤️

subject: english
title: Importance of Water
concept: Discussing the uses of water.
explanation: Water is essential for life! Let's talk about all the ways we use water every day. 💧

subject: english
title: Color the Birds and Plants
concept: Following directions to color.
explanation: Listen carefully to the instructions and color the birds, leaves, and plants as you like! 🎨

subject: english
title: Make Earth Beautiful
concept: Expressing creativity through drawing.
explanation: Draw something that you think would make our Earth a more beautiful place! 🌍✨

subject: english
title: Match the Words from the Poem
concept: Connecting words to complete phrases.
explanation: Match the words in Column A with Column B to complete the phrases from the poem! 📝

subject: english
title: Complete the Sentences
concept: Using context clues to fill in missing words.
explanation: Fill in the blanks using the words from the box! For example, 'A ____________ of milk.' 🥛

subject: english
title: Rearrange the Words
concept: Forming correct sentences.
explanation: Rearrange the words to make proper sentences. Remember to start with a capital letter and end with a full stop! 🗣️

subject: english
title: Touch and Feel Project
concept: Exploring textures in the kitchen.
explanation: Ask your family to let you touch and feel different things in the kitchen. What do they feel like? 🍚🥔

subject: english
title: We Are All Indians
concept: Understanding diversity in languages and cultures across India.
explanation: In India, we have many languages and cultures! Each person can share where they come from and what they like. 🌏✨
examples: Ramu comes from Gujarat and speaks Gujarati.
Meenakshi comes from Tamil Nadu and speaks Tamil.

practice_questions.q: How old are you?
What is your name?
Where do you study?

subject: english
title: Fill in the Blanks
concept: Practicing personal questions and answers.
explanation: Let's practice asking and answering questions about ourselves! 🤔💬
examples: I am Badal. I come from Bengal. I like painting and music.
I am Neerja. I come from Uttar Pradesh. I have seen the Taj Mahal.

practice_questions.q: What language do you speak?
Which tree do you like?

subject: english
title: New Words
concept: Learning new words related to culture and activities.
explanation: Let's learn some new words that help us talk about our cultures! 📚🌟
examples: Puppet: A toy that can be moved.

subject: english
title: Let Us Speak
concept: Practicing everyday phrases and questions.
explanation: Let's practice what we say in different situations! 🗣️😊

practice_questions.q: What do you say when you want to enter the classroom?
practice_questions.a: Excuse me, may I come in?

practice_questions.q: What do you say when you need a pencil?

subject: english
title: What do you like the most?
concept: Expressing personal preferences through drawing.
explanation: Let's draw something we love! 🎨 What makes you happy at home? Is it your favorite toy, a pet, or something else?
examples: A drawing of my favorite toy car 🚗
A drawing of my pet dog 🐶

subject: english
title: Frame Sentences
concept: Creating sentences using given words.
explanation: Let's make sentences! 🗣️ For example, if we have the words 'bright', 'sunny', and 'day', we can say: 'It was a bright sunny day.' Now it's your turn!
examples: It was a bright sunny day. My brother went to buy bread. He crossed the bridge to reach the shop.

subject: english
title: Discuss with your teacher
concept: Answering questions about India and festivals.
explanation: Let's talk about our country and festivals! 🇮🇳 Can you answer these questions?

practice_questions.q: What do we call the people who live in India?
Name the festival of colours.
Which festival do you like the most? Why?
practice_questions.a: I like Diwali because of the lights and sweets!

subject: english
title: Rearrange the letters
concept: Identifying languages from jumbled letters.
explanation: Can you solve these puzzles? 🧩 Rearrange the letters to find the names of languages!

practice_questions.q: What language is INHDI?
What language is GNIELHS?
What language is RUUD?
What language is ELUTGU?

subject: english
title: Use 'but' to join sentences
concept: Combining sentences using the word 'but'.
explanation: Let's join sentences! ✍️ For example, 'I like to eat mangoes.' and 'I don’t like to eat apples.' can be joined as 'I like to eat mangoes but I don’t like to eat apples.' Now, try these!

practice_questions.q: Join these sentences: I like milk. I don’t like tea.
practice_questions.a: I like milk but I don’t like tea.

practice_questions.q: Join these sentences: My grandfather is old. He is strong.
practice_questions.a: My grandfather is old but he is strong.

practice_questions.q: Join these sentences: I wanted to go out. I was too tired.
practice_questions.a: I wanted to go out but I was too tired.

subject: english
title: The Mystery Object
concept: Exploring curiosity and learning about history.
explanation: In a classroom, students find an unusual object. They wonder what it is and learn about Mahatma Gandhi! 🌟

subject: english
title: Understanding the Charkha
concept: The charkha symbolizes self-reliance and independence.
explanation: Mahatma Gandhi used the charkha to spin thread and promote local crafts. 🌱 This handkerchief is made from the thread spun on the charkha!
examples: A handkerchief made from spun thread.

practice_questions.q: What did Mahatma Gandhi use the charkha for?
What does the charkha symbolize?

subject: english
title: Matching Fruits to Trees
concept: Understanding the connection between fruits and their trees.
explanation: Can you match the fruits to the trees they grow on? 🍌🍏 Let's learn about nature!
examples: Banana grows on a banana tree.
Apple grows on an apple tree.

practice_questions.q: Which tree does the coconut grow on?
Where do grapes grow?

subject: english
title: The Festival of Flowers
concept: Celebrating the arrival of Spring with flowers.
explanation: During Phool Dei, children collect flowers and offer them to elders. 🌸 They receive rice and jaggery in return!

practice_questions.q: What do children collect during Phool Dei?
What do elders give in return?

subject: english
title: Celebrating Sharad Poornima
concept: Understanding the significance of Sharad Poornima.
explanation: Sharad Poornima is celebrated on a full moon night in September-October. 🌕 It marks the end of the monsoon season.

practice_questions.q: When is Sharad Poornima celebrated?
What does it mark the end of?

subject: english
title: The Festival of Sikkim
concept: Celebrating the Pang Lhabsol festival.
explanation: Pang Lhabsol is celebrated in Sikkim to worship Mt Kanchenjunga. 🏔️ People dance and celebrate together!

practice_questions.q: What do people worship during Pang Lhabsol?
When is this festival celebrated?

subject: english
title: The Tamil Monsoon Festival
concept: Understanding the significance of Aadi Perukku.
explanation: Aadi Perukku is a Tamil festival where people worship the river Cauvery. 🌊 Farmers pray for a good harvest!

practice_questions.q: What do people worship during Aadi Perukku?
What do farmers pray for?

subject: english
title: Discovering Folk Songs
concept: Learning about local folk songs from family.
explanation: Ask your family about a folk song they used to sing. 🎶 Learn it in your mother tongue and share it with the class!

subject: english
title: Understanding Holistic Development
concept: Holistic development nurtures all aspects of a child's growth.
explanation: Holistic development means helping children grow in many ways: physically, emotionally, socially, and mentally. 🌱 For example, playing with friends helps them learn to share and be kind!

practice_questions.q: What does holistic development mean?
practice_questions.a: It means growing in many ways like physically and emotionally.

practice_questions.q: Can you name one way to help a child grow?
practice_questions.a: Playing with them helps them learn!

subject: english
title: What is the Foundational Stage?
concept: The Foundational Stage is crucial for early education.
explanation: The Foundational Stage is when children aged 3-8 learn important skills. 📚 This stage helps them get ready for school and life! It's like planting seeds for a big tree! 🌳

practice_questions.q: What age does the Foundational Stage cover?
Why is this stage important?
practice_questions.a: It helps children learn skills for school and life!

subject: english
title: Understanding NEP 2020
concept: NEP 2020 focuses on improving early education.
explanation: The National Education Policy 2020 is a plan to help children learn better in their early years. 🎓 It aims to make education fun and effective! 🌟

practice_questions.q: What does NEP stand for?
What is one goal of NEP 2020?

subject: english
title: Play-Based Learning
concept: Learning through play is essential for children.
explanation: Learning isn't just about books! 📚 It's about playing, exploring, and having fun while learning new things! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is play-based learning?
practice_questions.a: Learning through fun activities and games!

subject: english
title: Five Domains of Learning
concept: There are five important areas of learning.
explanation: We learn about our body, feelings, thinking, language, and culture! 🌍 Each part helps us grow in different ways! 🌱

practice_questions.q: Name one domain of learning.

subject: english
title: Connecting Home and School
concept: What we learn at home is important in school.
explanation: Your experiences at home help you learn better in school! 🏠➡️🏫 Share your stories and ideas! 💬

practice_questions.q: Why is it important to share home experiences?
practice_questions.a: It helps us learn from each other!

subject: english
title: Making Learning Fun for Everyone
concept: Learning should be for everyone, no matter where they come from.
explanation: Everyone has different stories and backgrounds! 🌈 Let's celebrate them in our learning! 🎊

practice_questions.q: How can we make learning inclusive?
practice_questions.a: By sharing and respecting everyone's stories!

subject: english
title: Thinking Questions
concept: Asking questions helps us think better.
explanation: Let's use our brains! 🧠 Asking questions makes us smarter! 🤔
examples: What happens if we don't water plants? 🌱❓
Why do we need to share? 🤝❓

practice_questions.q: Why is asking questions important?
practice_questions.a: It helps us learn and understand more!

subject: english
title: Importance of Language
concept: Language is a key medium for communication and development.
explanation: Language helps us express our thoughts and feelings. It is important for learning and understanding the world around us! 🌍✨

practice_questions.q: Why is language important?
practice_questions.a: It helps us communicate and learn!

subject: english
title: Learning in Our Own Language
concept: Learning is easier in our home language.
explanation: When we learn in a language we know well, like our mother tongue, we understand things better! 🏠💬

practice_questions.q: What is your home language?

subject: english
title: Developing Oral Skills
concept: Building strong speaking skills is essential.
explanation: Talking clearly and using good words helps us share our ideas! 🗣️💡
examples: Sharing a story about your day

practice_questions.q: How can we improve our speaking skills?

subject: english
title: Introducing Writing
concept: Writing helps us express our thoughts on paper.
explanation: Writing is like telling a story, but we do it with letters! ✍️📖
examples: Writing a letter to a friend

practice_questions.q: What do you like to write about?
practice_questions.a: I like to write about my adventures!

subject: english
title: Learning is Fun
full_text:
Learning is fun, oh yes indeed! 🌟
With stories and games, we plant the seed.
We talk and we write, we sing and we play,
In our world of language, we learn every day! 🎉

subject: english
title: Learning Through Play
concept: Learning is more fun with activities!
explanation: We learn best when we play and explore! 🎈🤹‍♂️

practice_questions.q: What games do you like to play while learning?

subject: english
title: Holistic Language Learning
concept: Language learning involves thinking, listening, observing, speaking, reading, imagining, and writing.
explanation: Learning a language is like going on an adventure! You get to think, listen, and even draw! 🎨 You can talk about pictures and read stories. It's all about having fun while learning! 🌟

subject: english
title: Understanding Curricular Goals
concept: Curricular goals help guide language learning activities.
explanation: Curricular goals are like treasure maps that show us how to learn better! 🗺️ They help us listen to songs, understand rhymes, and use new words every day! 🎶

subject: english
title: Let Us Speak
concept: Encouraging conversation builds confidence in speaking English.
explanation: Talking is super fun! 🗣️ When we practice speaking, we become better at sharing our thoughts and ideas. Let's chat and have fun! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite color?
Can you name three animals?
practice_questions.a: Sure! I can name a cat, dog, and elephant!

subject: english
title: Colour the Hut
concept: Following instructions helps in understanding tasks.
explanation: Instructions are like clues! 🕵️‍♂️ When we follow them, we can complete fun tasks like coloring! 🎨 Let's see how well we can listen!
examples: Draw a sun above the hut

subject: english
title: My Bicycle
concept: Enjoying time with friends and having fun.
explanation: This story is about a child who loves riding their bicycle and playing games with friends! 🚴‍♂️ It's all about joy and friendship! 🤗
narrative: Once upon a time, there was a child who loved riding their bicycle. Every day after school, they would ride around the park, feeling the wind on their face. They would also invite friends to play games like bat-ball and gilli-danda. Together, they laughed and created wonderful memories!

subject: english
title: Little Drops of Water
concept: A poem reflecting Indian values of cooperation.
explanation: This poem teaches us about the importance of working together and living in harmony. 🌊🤝
text:
Little drops of water,
Little grains of sand,
Make the mighty ocean,
And the pleasant land.
Thus the little minutes,
Humble though they be,
Make the mighty ages
Of eternity.

subject: english
title: Belonging to Home and Beyond
concept: Understanding the idea of belonging.
explanation: This lesson helps us feel connected to our home and community. 🏡🌍
examples: My town has parks and schools.
I feel safe in my neighborhood.

practice_questions.q: What makes you feel at home?
practice_questions.a: My family and friends make me feel at home.

subject: english
title: Travel and Modes of Transport
concept: Exploring different ways to travel.
explanation: This lesson talks about how we go to school and other places. 🚍🚴‍♂️
examples: My mom drives me in the car.

practice_questions.q: How do you go to school?

subject: english
title: Poem about Observation
concept: Learning to observe and imagine.
explanation: This poem encourages us to look at the clouds and use our imagination. ☁️✨
text:
A Show of Clouds
Look at the clouds in the sky,
They float and dance, oh so high!
Some are fluffy, some are gray,
They change their shapes throughout the day.

subject: english
topic: The Fly that Forgot its Name
title: Story of a Forgetful Fly
concept: Understanding identity and memory.
explanation: This story is about a fly that forgets its name and goes on an adventure to find it. 🐝✨
text: Once upon a time, there was a little fly who forgot its name. It buzzed around asking everyone, 'What is my name?' It met a bee, a butterfly, and even a spider. Each told it a different name. Finally, the fly realized it was special just the way it was!

subject: english
title: Poem about Self-Esteem
concept: Understanding self-esteem and self-identity.
explanation: This poem teaches us to appreciate ourselves and be proud of who we are. 🐦💖
text:
The Crow
A crow sat on a tree,
Feeling proud and free.
With feathers black and bright,
It sang with all its might.

subject: english
title: Story about Hygiene
concept: Importance of hygiene and cleanliness.
explanation: This story shows how a smart monkey learns to keep itself and its surroundings clean. 🐒🧼
text: Once there was a smart monkey who loved to play. One day, it noticed that its home was messy. The monkey decided to clean up and taught its friends the importance of being tidy and taking care of their surroundings.

subject: english
title: Regional and National Identity
concept: Understanding patriotism and togetherness.
explanation: This lesson teaches us about being proud of our country and respecting our differences. 🇮🇳🤝
examples: People from various cultures live in harmony.

practice_questions.q: What does unity in diversity mean?
practice_questions.a: It means people from different backgrounds live together peacefully.

subject: english
title: Play about Mahatma Gandhi
concept: Learning about historical figures and their contributions.
explanation: This short play introduces us to Mahatma Gandhi and the significance of the Charkha in India's history. 🎭🧵
text: In this play, children act out the story of Mahatma Gandhi and how he used the Charkha to promote self-reliance and unity among people.

subject: english
title: My Bicycle
concept: Understanding the joy of riding a bicycle.
explanation: Riding a bicycle is fun and helps us stay active! 🚴‍♂️ Imagine feeling the wind on your face as you pedal fast down the street.
examples: Riding with friends in the park
Learning to balance on two wheels

practice_questions.q: What do you like about riding a bicycle?
practice_questions.a: I love feeling free and going fast!

subject: english
title: Picture Reading
concept: Interpreting images to tell a story.
explanation: Pictures can tell us stories without words! 🖼️ Look at a picture and think about what is happening.

practice_questions.q: What story does this picture tell?
practice_questions.a: It shows friends having fun at a picnic!

subject: english
title: It is Fun
concept: Exploring what makes our world enjoyable.
explanation: There are so many fun things in our world! 🌍 From playing games to exploring nature, every day can be an adventure.

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite fun activity?
practice_questions.a: I love going to the amusement park!

subject: english
title: Seeing without Seeing
concept: Understanding perception and imagination.
explanation: Sometimes we can imagine things even if we can't see them! 🌈 Use your imagination to picture your favorite place.
examples: Imagining a beach on a rainy day
Thinking of a favorite book character

practice_questions.q: What can you imagine right now?
practice_questions.a: I can imagine a sunny day at the beach!

subject: english
title: A Show of Clouds
concept: Observing and describing clouds.
explanation: Clouds can look like many things! ☁️ Sometimes they look like animals or shapes. What do you see in the clouds?
examples: A cloud that looks like a bunny
A cloud that looks like a car

practice_questions.q: What shape do you see in the clouds today?

subject: english
title: My Name
concept: Understanding the significance of names.
explanation: Our names are special! They tell people who we are. 🌟 What does your name mean?
examples: My name is 'Joy' because I bring happiness!
My name is 'Sam' because it means 'listener'

practice_questions.q: What does your name mean?

subject: english
title: The Crow
concept: Learning about the cleverness of crows.
explanation: Crows are very smart birds! 🐦 They can solve problems and find food in clever ways.
narrative: Once upon a time, a hungry crow found a pitcher with some water at the bottom. The water was too low for him to reach. So, he dropped pebbles into the pitcher one by one until the water rose high enough for him to drink. What a clever crow!

subject: english
title: The Smart Monkey
concept: Understanding the importance of cleverness.
explanation: Monkeys are playful and smart! 🐒 They often use their brains to solve problems.
narrative: In a jungle, a smart monkey wanted to eat bananas from a tall tree. Instead of climbing, he noticed a stick nearby. He used the stick to knock the bananas down. The other animals were amazed by his cleverness!

subject: english
title: Little Drops of Water
concept: Appreciating nature and water.
explanation: Water is essential for life! 💧 Let's enjoy this rhyme about little drops of water.
content:
Little drops of water, little grains of sand,  
Make the mighty ocean and the pleasant land.  
Thus the little minutes, humble though they be,  
Make the mighty ages of eternity.

subject: english
title: We are all Indians
concept: Understanding unity in diversity.
explanation: We all belong to one country, India! 🇮🇳 Let's celebrate our differences and similarities.

practice_questions.q: What makes India special?

subject: hindi
title: परिवार के सदस्यों के बारे में
concept: परिवार के सदस्यों की पहचान और उनके साथ समय बिताना।
explanation: हमारे परिवार में कई सदस्य होते हैं जैसे दादी, माँ, पिता आदि। हमें उनके साथ समय बिताना चाहिए और उनकी बातें सुननी चाहिए। 🏡❤️

practice_questions.q: आपके परिवार में कौन-कौन हैं?
practice_questions.a: मेरे परिवार में माँ, पिता, और एक भाई हैं।

practice_questions.q: आप अपने दादी के साथ क्या करते हैं?
practice_questions.a: मैं दादी के साथ कहानियाँ सुनता हूँ।

subject: hindi
title: नीमा और दादी
concept: समय का महत्व और परिवार के साथ खेलना।
explanation: नीमा अपनी दादी के साथ खेलना चाहती है। दादी उसे कहती हैं कि थोड़ा देर बैठो। यह कहानी हमें सिखाती है कि खेलना कितना मजेदार होता है! 🎉
narrative: नीमा दोपहर में दो बजे स्कूल से लौटती है। इस समय घर पर सिर्फ दादी होती हैं। दादी नीमा का इंतज़ार कर रही होती हैं। नीमा दादी से कहती है, 'दादी, आप भी मेरे साथ खेलने चलिए!' दादी हंसते हुए कहती हैं कि खेलना समय बिताने का अच्छा तरीका है।

practice_questions.q: नीमा की दादी घर पर क्यों रहती हैं?
practice_questions.a: दादी घर पर रहती हैं क्योंकि वह नीमा का इंतज़ार कर रही होती हैं।

practice_questions.q: नीमा स्कूल से घर आकर क्या करती है?
practice_questions.a: नीमा दादी से बातें करती है और फिर खेलने जाती है।

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों का खेल
concept: शब्दों को जोड़कर नए शब्द बनाना।
explanation: शब्दों को जोड़कर नए शब्द बनाना बहुत मजेदार है! चलो, कुछ शब्दों को जोड़ते हैं। ✏️

practice_questions.q: इन शब्दों को जोड़कर नए शब्द बनाइए: 'नी' और 'मा'
इन शब्दों को जोड़कर नए शब्द बनाइए: 'द' और 'ादी'

subject: hindi
title: प्यारा घर
concept: घर की सुंदरता और परिवार का प्यार।
explanation: हमारा घर हमारे लिए बहुत खास होता है। यह हमें प्यार और सुरक्षा देता है। 🏠💖
text:
पापा, कयों अचछा लगता है
अपना पयारा-पयारा घर?
घूम-घाम लें, खेल-खाल लें
नहीं भकूलता लेस्न घर।
नहीं आप्ो लगता पापा
है माँ की गोदी-सा घर।
पयारी-पयारी ममतावाला
सुंदर-सुंदर नयारा घर।
थ्‍र जब वापस आते हैं
सबछ-सबछ जाता घर।

subject: hindi
title: घर की विशेषताएँ
concept: घर हमारे लिए एक सुरक्षित स्थान है।
explanation: घर वह जगह है जहाँ हम अपने परिवार के साथ रहते हैं। यह हमें सुरक्षा और प्यार देता है। 🏡❤️
examples: घर में माँ, पिता और भाई-बहन रहते हैं।
घर में हमें आराम मिलता है।

practice_questions.q: आपको अपने घर में सबसे अच्छी चीज़ क्या लगती है और क्यों?
practice_questions.a: मुझे अपने घर का बगीचा बहुत पसंद है क्योंकि वहाँ खेलना मजेदार है।

practice_questions.q: आपको अपने घर की याद क्यों आती है?
practice_questions.a: क्योंकि घर में परिवार के साथ बिताए हुए पल बहुत खास होते हैं।

subject: hindi
title: माला की पायल
concept: माला की पायल उसे सबको डराने में मदद करती थी।
explanation: माला की माँ ने उसे एक प्यारी सी चाँदी की पायल दी। जब माला पायल पहनती थी, तो उसकी आवाज़ से सब उसे पहचान लेते थे। लेकिन एक दिन माला ने पायल उतार दी। 😮
narrative: माला सबको डराती। माला की चाँदी की पायल चिमकती थी। वह अपनी नानी और छोटे भाई को डराती थी। लेकिन जब सब डरने लगे, तो माला ने पायल उतार दी।

practice_questions.q: माला सभी को क्यों डराती होगी?
माला ने पायल क्यों उतारी?
practice_questions.a: क्योंकि सब उसे पहचानने लगे थे।

subject: hindi
title: माँ की महिमा
concept: माँ हमारे जीवन की सबसे महत्वपूर्ण व्यक्ति हैं।
explanation: माँ हमें प्यार और साहस देती हैं। 🌼❤️
text:
माँ तुम स्तनी भोली-भाली
स्तनी पयारी-पयारी हो
सदल से सचची समसरी जैसी
सारे जग से नयारी हो।

मेरे मन में जोश तुमहीं से
तुमसे ही प्र्ाश
मुझमें साहस तुमसे आता
तुमसे ही सवशवास।

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों का खेल
concept: शब्दों को जोड़कर नए शब्द बनाना।
explanation: जब हम किसी शब्द के ऊपर चंद्रबिंदु लगाते हैं, तो वह नया शब्द बन जाता है। जैसे 'मा' पर चंद्रबिंदु लगाकर 'माँ' बन जाता है।

subject: hindi
title: मैं अपने दादाजी के साथ
concept: दादाजी के साथ बिताए गए समय की खुशी।
explanation: यह कहानी एक बच्ची की है जो अपने दादाजी के साथ समय बिताती है। वे साथ में खेलते हैं और मजेदार बातें करते हैं।
narrative: मैं अपने दादाजी के साथ रहती हूँ। उन्हें मैं 'थाथा' कहती हूँ। जब उन पर बहुत प्यार आता है, तो मैं उन्हें 'थाथकू' बुलाती हूँ। हम समुद्र तट पर जाते हैं, वहाँ लहरें उठती हैं और हम दौड़ लगाते हैं। जब अंधेरा हो जाता है, तो मैं उनके हाथ पकड़कर घर आती हूँ। मुझे अपने थाथा से बेहद प्यार है।

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों का खेल
concept: शब्दों को सही क्रम में लगाना।
explanation: नीचे दिए गए शब्दों को सही क्रम में लगाकर वाक्य बनाएं।

practice_questions.q: सही जगह पर चंद्रबिंदु लगाकर अपनी कॉपी में लिखिए।
practice_questions.a: ऊँची-नीची लहरें मेरे चारों तरफ उठती-सागरती हैं।

practice_questions.q: नीचे दिए गए शब्द उलट-पलट हो गए हैं। सही क्रम में लगाकर वाक्य लिखिए।
practice_questions.a: साथ में रहती हूँ मैं दादाजी।

subject: hindi
title: चींटा-चींटा
concept: चींटी के बारे में एक मजेदार कविता।
explanation: यह कविता चींटी के कामों और उसकी खुशियों के बारे में है।
full_text:
चींटा-चींटा दकूला, दकूला दही जमा,
बसढ़या-बसढ़या दही जमा,
खट्ा-मीठा दही जमा।
चींटा-चींटा गनना ला,
गनना ला शक्र बना,
चींटी भकूखी आएगी,
झटपट वो खा जाएगी।
चींटा-चींटा शक्र ला,
शक्र ला शरबत बना,
चींटी पयासी आएगी,
झटपट वो पी जाएगी।
चींटा-चींटा घर बना,
चींटी कूप से आएगी,
देख के खुश हो जाएगी,
झट-से वो रुच जाएगी।
चींटा-चींटा बाजार जा,
शक्र ला, चावल ला,
रोटी ला, पानी ला,
झटपट जा, झटपट आ।
चींटी जब घर आएगी,
देख के खुश हो जाएगी,
तेरे ही गुण गाएगी,
तेरे ही गुण गाएगी।

subject: hindi
title: परिवार के सदस्यों के लिए धन्यवाद कार्ड बनाना
concept: परिवार के सदस्यों के प्रति आभार व्यक्त करना
explanation: हम अपने परिवार के सदस्यों का धन्यवाद करते हैं क्योंकि वे हमारे जीवन में बहुत महत्वपूर्ण होते हैं। 😊

subject: hindi
title: चींटियों के कार्य
concept: चींटियाँ कैसे काम करती हैं
explanation: चींटियाँ बहुत मेहनती होती हैं और वे अपने काम में बहुत व्यस्त रहती हैं। 🐜

subject: hindi
title: स्कूल का पहला दिन
concept: स्कूल जाने का आनंद
explanation: स्कूल का पहला दिन हमेशा खास होता है। 🎒
text:
सटलल कू जी स्कूल गए,
बसता घर पर भ कू ल गए।
रसते भर थे डरे-डरे,
पहुँचे गेट पर अरे अरे।
खेि गीत छु ट्ी ्ा नोसटस सचप्ा,
देख खु शी से कू ल गए।
दोनों बाँहें माँ े,
डाल गले में झ कू ल गए।
— नरेश सकसेना

subject: hindi
title: नटखट लड़के की कहानी
concept: सीखने की लगन
explanation: एक नटखट लड़का था जो हमेशा सीखने के लिए उत्सुक रहता था। 📚
text: नटखट ददवाकि सदवा्र पाँच वर्फ़ ्ा नटखट लड़्ा है। सारे सदन सठठोली ्रता है और सब्ो हँसाता है। उसे गसणत सीखने ्ी बहु त लगन है। उस्ी बड़ी बसहन मीना उसे संखयाओं ्ो जोड़ना ससखाती है और सदवा्र धयान से सीखता है। सदवा्र ्हता है, “ए् और दो, तीन!” मीना ्हती है, “सपेरा बजाता बीन!” सदवा्र ह्ता है, “तीन और चार, सात!” मीना ्हती है, “लाओ ्लम दवात!”

subject: hindi

subject: hindi
title: बीज की कहानी
concept: बीज कैसे उगता है
explanation: एक बीज को गमले में डालने पर वह धीरे-धीरे पौधा बनता है। यह प्रकृति का एक अद्भुत चक्र है। 🌱
narrative: मुझे एक बीज दमला। मैंने उसको गमले में डाला। मैंने उसे पानी दिया और बहुत सारी धूप। क्या यह पेड़ है? क्या यह झाड़ी है? क्या इसमें फूल होंगे? क्या यह लंबा ऊँचा होगा? क्या यह छोटा ही रहेगा? मुझे नहीं पता। कोई चिंता नहीं पड़ता!

practice_questions.q: बीज कहाँ दमला होगा?

practice_questions.a: हाँ, पेड़ बड़े होते हैं और झाड़ियाँ छोटी।

subject: hindi
title: किसान की मेहनत

full_text:
मुझे एक बीज दमला।
मैंने उसको गमले में डाला।
मैंने उसे पानी दिया और बहुत सारी धूप।
क्या यह पेड़ है?
क्या यह झाड़ी है?
क्या इसमें फूल होंगे?
क्या इसमें फल होंगे?
क्या यह लंबा ऊँचा होगा?
क्या यह छोटा ही रहेगा?
मुझे नहीं पता।
कोई चिंता नहीं पड़ता!

subject: hindi
title: नानाजी और मूली
concept: सहयोग और मेहनत का महत्व
explanation: यह कहानी हमें सिखाती है कि जब हम सब मिलकर काम करते हैं, तो हम कठिनाईयों को पार कर सकते हैं। 🌱👨‍🌾

practice_questions.q: नानाजी ने इतनी बड़ी मूली के साथ क्या किया?
practice_questions.a: उन्होंने मूली को बाहर निकालने के लिए सबको बुलाया।

practice_questions.q: मूली इतनी बड़ी कैसे हुई?
practice_questions.a: मूली को अच्छी देखभाल और पानी मिला।

full_text:
नानाजी ने बगीचे में मूली बोयी।
मूली से नानाजी बोले, "उगो-उगो मूली। मज़बूत बनो और लंबी हो।"
चिड़िया पंखे तो दिन नानी को आवाज़ लगायी।
नानी ने थामा नाना को, नाना ने थामा मूली को।
दोनों ने पूरा जोर लगाया। मगर मूली को दोनों निकाल न पाए।
उग आयी मोटी और लंबी मूली। नानाजी गए उसे निकालने।
खींचते रहे, अपना पूरा जोर लगाया, मगर मूली को बाहर न ला पाए।
तभी नानी ने नादतन को बुलाया। नादतन ने थामा नानी को, नानी ने थामा नाना को, नाना ने थामा मूली को, सबने दमलकर खींचा।
दमलकर पूरा जोर लगाया। मगर मूली को निकाल न पाए।
तब नादतन ने अपने कुत्ते को बुलाया। कुत्ते ने नादतन को थामा, नादतन ने नानी को थामा, नानी ने नाना को थामा, नाना ने मूली को थामा, सभी ने दमलकर जोर लगाया। आखिर दमलकर सबने मूली को बाहर निकाल ही डाला!

practice_questions.q: शेर के दाँत भी तो पैने होते हैं, तो उसने खुद ही जाल क्यों नहीं कुतर डाला?
practice_questions.a: शेर जाल में फँस गया था, इसलिए उसे मदद की जरूरत थी।

title: कहानी से सीखें

practice_questions.q: इस कहानी से हमने क्या सीखा?

practice_questions.q: आपके सबसे अच्छे मित्र का नाम क्या है? आप उसके साथ क्या-क्या करते हैं?
practice_questions.a: मेरा सबसे अच्छा मित्र ... है। हम खेलते हैं।

subject: hindi

subject: hindi
title: Friendship Rhyme
concept: Expressing friendship through fun rhymes.

full_text:
एक थाल मोती से भरा,

narrative: राजू और मीना दोनों दोस्त हैं और साथ में स्कूल जाते हैं। एक दिन, स्कूल जाते समय एक बिल्ली उनके सामने आ गई। वे डर गए और रोने लगे। लेकिन उन्होंने एक-दूसरे का हाथ पकड़ा और समझा कि डरना ठीक नहीं है।

subject: hindi

subject: hindi
title: जीत और ििली का खेल
concept: जीत और ििली ने खेल खेला।
explanation: जीत और ििली ने मिलकर गेंद से खेला। खेल में मजा आया जब उन्होंने गेंद को फेंका और पकड़ा। 🎉⚽

practice_questions.q: जीत और ििली ने कौन-कौन से खेल खेले?
practice_questions.a: जीत और ििली ने गेंद का खेल खेला।

practice_questions.q: आप कौन-कौन से खेल खेलते हैं?
practice_questions.a: मैं क्रिकेट और फुटबॉल खेलता हूँ।

practice_questions.q: ििली ने गेंद िनाने में कौन-कौन सी वस्तुओं का उपयोग किया?
practice_questions.a: ििली ने गेंद और सु्तली का उपयोग किया।

practice_questions.q: अगर आप अंपायर होते तो क्या जीत को आउट देते?
practice_questions.a: मैं खेल के नियमों के अनुसार निर्णय लेता।

subject: hindi
title: खेलों के बारे में जानकारी इकट्ठा करें
concept: खेलों के नाम और उनके बारे में जानकारी।
explanation: नीचे दिए गए खेलों के बारे में जानकारी इकट्ठा करें और लिखें। 📝🏀

subject: hindi
title: खेल गीत
concept: खेलों पर एक मजेदार गीत।
explanation: यह गीत बच्चों को खेलों के बारे में बताता है और उन्हें खेलने के लिए प्रेरित करता है। 🎶🏃‍♂️
text:
प़ोषम पा भई प़ोषम पा,
सा रे गा मा सा रे गा
आओ डमलकर खेलें खेल
छुपन-छुपाई छुक-छुक रेल
हँसना है मुस्काना है
फूलों-सा डखल जाना है

subject: hindi
title: छुपन-छुपाई का खेल
concept: जीत ने छुपन-छुपाई खेला।
explanation: एक दिन जीत ने छुपन-छुपाई खेला। वह सौ तक गिनकर दोस्तों को ढूंढने लगा। 🕵️‍♂️🤫
text: एक दिन जीत छुपन-छुपाई खेल रहे थे। उस दिन जीत की बारी थी। जीत सौ तक गिनकर सिक़ो ढूंढने निकला।

subject: hindi
title: जीत और नाड जया की कहानी
concept: दोस्तों के साथ खेलना और छुपना
explanation: जीत और नाड जया अपने दोस्तों के साथ छुपन-छुपाई खेल रहे थे। जीत ने नाड जया को कई जगहों पर ढूंढा। यह खेल हमें सिखाता है कि हमें अपने दोस्तों के साथ मिलकर खेलना चाहिए। 🎉
examples: जीत ने डमल गई जगहों पर नाड जया को ढूंढा।
नाड जया ने जीत को धपका दिया।

practice_questions.q: जीत ने कितनी जगहों पर नाड जया को ढूंढा?
practice_questions.a: जीत ने कई जगहों पर नाड जया को ढूंढा।

practice_questions.q: छुपन-छुपाई में धपका किसे कहते हैं?
practice_questions.a: धपका उस समय होता है जब कोई छुपा हुआ व्यक्ति अचानक बाहर आता है।

subject: hindi
title: सवाल-जवाब
concept: खेल के दौरान चोट लगने पर क्या करना चाहिए
explanation: अगर खेलते हुए आपको चोट लग जाए, तो आपको आराम करना चाहिए और किसी बड़े से मदद मांगनी चाहिए। 🤕
examples: अगर आप खेल में हार जाते हैं, तो आपको क्या करना चाहिए?

practice_questions.q: आप अपने घर में कहाँ-कहाँ छिप सकते हैं?
practice_questions.a: आप अलमारी, दरवाजे के पीछे, या पलंग के नीचे छिप सकते हैं।

practice_questions.q: अगर खेलते हुए आपको चोट लग जाए, तो आप क्या करेंगे?

subject: hindi
title: हाथी साइडकल

explanation: यह कविता हाथी और चींटी के बीच की मजेदार बातचीत को दर्शाती है। यह हमें सिखाती है कि हर कोई खास होता है। 🐘🐜

subject: hindi
title: अपनी कहानी लिखें
concept: अपनी कहानी को लिखने का अभ्यास

examples: मैं अपने दोस्तों के साथ खेलता हूँ।

subject: hindi
title: हाथी और चींटी की कहानी
concept: एक हाथी और चींटी की दोस्ती की कहानी।
explanation: यह कहानी एक हाथी और एक चींटी की है। हाथी साइडकल चला रहा था और चींटी उसकी मदद करने आई। यह हमें सिखाती है कि दोस्ती में मदद करना बहुत जरूरी है। 🐘🐜

subject: hindi
title: अपनी कहानी लिखें
concept: कहानी लिखने का अभ्यास।
explanation: बच्चों को कहानी लिखने के लिए प्रेरित करें। उन्हें अपनी कल्पना का उपयोग करके एक नई कहानी लिखने के लिए कहें। ✍️📖

subject: hindi
title: नरेंद्र की कहानी
concept: नरेंद्र और दादाजी की बातचीत।
explanation: यह कहानी नरेंद्र की है जो अपने दादाजी से एक पेड़ के बारे में सुनते हैं। दादाजी उन्हें बताते हैं कि उस पेड़ पर एक दैत्य रहता है। यह हमें सिखाता है कि हमें डरने की जरूरत नहीं है। 🌳👴

subject: hindi
title: चार दिशाएँ
concept: दिशाओं के बारे में कविता।
explanation: यह कविता हमें चार दिशाओं के बारे में बताती है। यह बच्चों को दिशा पहचानने में मदद करती है। 🌅🌍
text:
उगता सूरज जिधर सामने
उधर खडे हो मुँह करके तुम
ठीक सामने पूरब होता
और पीठ पीछे है पश्चिम
चमबायीं ओर दक्षिण
और बायीं ओर तुम्हारे है उत्तर
चार दिशाएँ होती हैं यों
पूरब पश्चिम दक्षिण उत्तर।

subject: hindi
title: आकाश का अन्वेषण
concept: आकाश और उसके तत्वों का ज्ञान
explanation: आकाश वह जगह है जहाँ सूरज, चाँद और तारे होते हैं। हम आकाश में उड़ते हुए बादलों और रंग-बिरंगे पक्षियों को देख सकते हैं। 🌤️🕊️
examples: चाँद रात में हमें रोशनी देता है।

practice_questions.q: आप जिशा का अनुमान कैसे लगाते हैं?
practice_questions.a: हम आकाश में बादलों और रंगों को देखकर अनुमान लगाते हैं।

practice_questions.q: नीचे कुछ शब्द दिए गए हैं। इन शब्दों से अपनी-अपनी कहानी बनाइए और कक्षा में सुनाइए।
practice_questions.a: बातचीत के लिए शब्द: आकाश, ओले, बाल, जंगल, मिलकर, जानवर, शेर, हाथी, लोमड़ी, डर, सुबह, खुशी-खुशी, नाचना, पहाड़।

subject: hindi
title: चिंता मामा की कहानी
concept: चिंता मामा का आकाश में प्रकाश
explanation: चिंता मामा रात में आकाश में चमकते हैं और हमें रोशनी देते हैं। 🌕✨
examples: चिंता मामा ने कहा, 'मैं तो चाँद से गपशप कर रहा था।'

practice_questions.q: चिंता मामा किससे गपशप कर रहे थे?
क्या चिंता मामा ने बाघ जी को बुलाया?
practice_questions.a: नहीं, उन्होंने कहा कि बाघ जी नहीं आएंगे।

subject: hindi
title: चिंता मामा की कविता
concept: चिंता मामा की अद्भुतता
explanation: यह कविता हमें बताती है कि कैसे सबने चिंता मामा को देखा। 🌌
text:
जगरे ताल में चंिा मामा,
सबने िेखा सबने िेखा,
ँसे जाल में चंिा मामा,
सबने िेखा सबने िेखा।
सबने िेखा एक अचंभा,
मछुारे ने जाल समेटा,
कहीं नहीं थे चंिा मामा,
कहाँ गए जी चंिा मामा।

subject: hindi
title: शब्दों का खेल
concept: शब्दों को क्रम में रखना
explanation: आइए, हम शब्दों को सही क्रम में रखें। यह खेल हमें शब्दों को समझने में मदद करता है। 🧩

subject: hindi
title: चाँद का आकार बनाना
concept: चाँद के आकार का अवलोकन
explanation: रोज़ रात में चाँद को देखिए और उसके आकार को बनाइए। यह हमें चाँद के घटने-बढ़ने के बारे में सिखाता है। 🌙✏️

subject: hindi
title: बारिश का आनंद
concept: बारिश का अनुभव
explanation: बारिश में हमें बहुत मज़ा आता है। हम छत पर और आँगन में बारिश का आनंद लेते हैं। ☔🌧️

subject: hindi
title: बारिश में छाता
concept: बारिश में छाता हमें कैसे बचाता है।
explanation: जब बारिश होती है, तो हम छाता लेकर चलते हैं ताकि हम गीले न हों। छाता हमें सूखा और गर्म रखता है। ☔️
examples: जब मैं और सलीम छाता के नीचे थे, हम गीले नहीं हुए।
जितने बच्चे छाते के नीचे आए, सब सूखे रहे।

practice_questions.q: बारिश से जुड़े अपने अनुभव बताइए।
practice_questions.a: मैंने बारिश में छाता लेकर खेला।

practice_questions.q: आप बड़े होकर क्या बनाना चाहेंगे?
क्या सचमुच कोई इतना बड़ा छाता बना सकता है?
practice_questions.a: नहीं, ऐसा छाता बनाना संभव नहीं है।

practice_questions.q: छाते में ऐसा क्या होता है कि हम बारिश में भी गीले नहीं होते?
practice_questions.a: छाता बारिश से हमें बचाता है।

subject: hindi
title: बड़े छाते की कहानी
narrative: एक दिन, दयाल चाचू ने कहा कि हमें एक बहुत बड़ा छाता चाहिए। सब बच्चे छाते के नीचे आ गए। लेकिन हर बार छाता छोटा पड़ जाता था। अंत में, दयाल चाचू ने कहा कि वह आसमान जितना बड़ा छाता बनाएंगे। सब बच्चे खुश हुए और बारिश में सूखे रहे। ☔️🌧️

subject: hindi
title: तोते का प्यारा खाना
content:
पहेिी तोते का प्यारा खाना है, 
लेजकन मेरी सी-सी है, 
इसका उत्तर दे सकते हो, 
यह तो बात ज़रा-सी है।

title: शब्दों का खेल

practice_questions.a: हाँ, कभी-कभी बारिश से बाढ़ आ सकती है।

explanation: Look at the clouds in the sky and draw their shapes in your notebook. ☁️✏️

subject: hindi

subject: hindi
title: Understanding Water Cycle
concept: The process of water changing forms.
explanation: पानी गरमी से भाप बनता है और फिर आसमान में जाकर बारिश बनता है। 🌧️
examples: पानी गरमी से भाप बनता है।
भाप ऊपर जाकर बारिश बनाती है।

practice_questions.q: क्या गरमी पानी को भाप बनाती है?
practice_questions.a: हाँ, गरमी पानी को भाप बनाती है।

practice_questions.q: भाप कहाँ जाती है?

subject: hindi
title: बरखा
concept: A rhyme about rain.
explanation: यह कविता बारिश के बारे में है। 🌧️
text:
काली भूरी नीली है, 
लाल गुलाबी पीली है, 
बरखा से यही बचाती है, 
धूप नहीं आ पाती है। 
रहती है जसर के ऊपर, 
पीली रहती है तन कर, 
सुंदर सी है, जानो तुम, 
अपने जसर पर तानो तुम।

subject: hindi
title: कहानी सुनाने वाला
concept: A fun story about a storyteller.
explanation: यह कहानी एक कहानी सुनाने वाले की है जो बच्चों को कहानी सुनाता है। 📖
text: कहानी सुनाने वाला कहाजनयाँ सुनाकर थक गया, पर सुनने वाले नहीं थके। उसे घेरकर बैठे बच्चों और बूढ़ों ने उससे और कहानी सुनाने का आग्रह किया। तो उसने कहानी सुनाना शुरू किया। वह बोला, “एक पेड पर बहुत-सी जचजडयाँ रहती थीं।” इतना कहकर वह रुक गया। सुनें कहानी! “जिर?” “जिर एक जचजडया पेड से उडी – झुर्र!” “जिर?” “जिर एक जचजडया पेड से उडी – झुर्र!” “जिर?” “जिर एक जचजडया पेड से उडी – झुर्र!” “जिर?” “झुर्र!” बहुत देर तक यही चलता रहा। “जिर?” “झुर्र!” “जिर?” “झुर्र!” से लोग थक गए। अंततः एक बच्चे ने पूछा, “यह कब तक चलेगा?” कहानी सुनाने वाले ने कहा, “जब तक सारी जचजडयाँ उड नहीं जाती!”

subject: hindi
title: Fill in the Blanks with Words
concept: Practice filling in the blanks.
explanation: दिए गए शब्दों से रिक्त स्थान भरें। ✏️

subject: hindi
title: बच्‍चों के विकास का महत्व

practice_questions.q: राष्ट्रीय शिक्षा नीति 2020 का उद्देश्य क्या है?
practice_questions.a: शिक्षा की गुणवत्ता में सुधार करना

subject: hindi
title: सीखने के पांच आयाम
concept: सीखने के विभिन्न आयामों का महत्व
explanation: सीखने के पांच आयाम हैं: शारीरिक, मानसिक, सामाजिक, भाषाई और सांस्कृतिक। ये सभी मिलकर बच्चों के विकास में मदद करते हैं। 🌈

subject: hindi

explanation: कोश हमारे शरीर के विभिन्न स्तरों का प्रतिनिधित्व करते हैं। जैसे अन्नमय कोश, प्राणमय कोश, मनोमय कोश, चवज्ानमय कोश और आनदमय कोश। ये सभी कोश मिलकर हमारे अनुभव और ज्ञान को बनाते हैं। 🌟
examples: अन्नमय कोश: यह हमारे शरीर के भौतिक स्तर को दर्शाता है।
प्राणमय कोश: यह हमारे जीवन शक्ति को दर्शाता है।

practice_questions.q: कोश कितने प्रकार के होते हैं?

subject: hindi
title: शिक्षण सामग्री का महत्व

subject: hindi
title: Importance of Language in Life
concept: Language is essential for communication and understanding.
explanation: Language helps us express our thoughts and feelings. It's like a bridge that connects us with others! 🌉 For example, when you say 'hello' to a friend, you are using language to communicate.
examples: Saying 'thank you' when someone helps you.

practice_questions.q: Why is language important?
practice_questions.a: It helps us communicate and understand each other.

subject: hindi
title: Starting to Read and Write
concept: Reading and writing are important skills for learning.
explanation: When you read, you discover new stories and ideas! 📚 Writing helps you share your own stories. For example, writing a letter to your friend is a fun way to communicate.
examples: Writing a diary entry about your day.

practice_questions.q: What do you like to read?

subject: hindi
title: Understanding Our Culture
concept: Culture is a part of our identity.
explanation: Culture includes our traditions, festivals, and values. 🎉 For example, celebrating Diwali with family is a cultural practice that brings joy and togetherness.

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite festival?

subject: hindi
title: Practicing Language Skills
concept: Engaging in activities enhances language skills.
explanation: Let's play a game! 🎲 Try to find five new words in your favorite storybook and use them in sentences.
examples: Find the word 'happy' and say, 'I feel happy when I play.'

subject: hindi
title: Understanding Words and Sentences
concept: Identifying and constructing words and sentences.
explanation: बच्चों को शब्दों को पहचानना और उन्हें सही तरीके से लिखना सिखाना है। यह उनके मौखिक भाषा विकास में मदद करता है। 🌟

practice_questions.q: कचवता का मतलब क्या है?
practice_questions.a: यह एक गतिविधि है जिसमें बच्चे अपने विचार साझा करते हैं।

practice_questions.q: सुनें कहान्री में बच्चों को क्या करना चाहिए?
practice_questions.a: कहानी सुनकर उसके बारे में बात करनी चाहिए।

explanation: कहानियों को पढ़ने से बच्चे न केवल पढ़ाई में सुधार करते हैं, बल्कि अपने विचारों को भी साझा करते हैं। 📖✨

subject: hindi
title: Beginning to Write
concept: Starting to write words and sentences.
explanation: बच्चों को चित्रों के माध्यम से अपने विचारों को व्यक्त करने के लिए प्रेरित किया जाता है। ✍️🌈

practice_questions.q: बच्चे कैसे लिखना शुरू करते हैं?
practice_questions.a: चित्रों से शुरू करके और फिर शब्दों को जोड़कर।

practice_questions.a: हाँ, यह बच्चों के लिए आसान और मजेदार होता है।

explanation: बच्चों को शब्दों के साथ खेलना सिखाना बहुत मजेदार होता है। जैसे, 'रंर-ह्री-रंर' जैसे शब्दों का उपयोग करके हम बातचीत कर सकते हैं। 🤗

explanation: कहानियाँ सुनाने से बच्चे नई चीजें सीखते हैं और उनकी कल्पना शक्ति बढ़ती है। 📖✨
examples: बच्चों को 'चाँद और तारे' की कहानी सुनाना।
कहानी सुनाते समय बच्चों से सवाल पूछना।

subject: hindi

examples: कचवता से हम अपने विचारों को व्यक्त कर सकते हैं।
कचवता से हम नई चीजें सीखते हैं।

practice_questions.q: कचवता का क्या महत्व है?
practice_questions.a: कचवता से हम अपने विचारों को व्यक्त कर सकते हैं और नई चीजें सीख सकते हैं।

explanation: बच्चों, अब आप अपनी कचवता लिखें। आप अपने विचारों को कागज पर उतार सकते हैं। 📝😊
examples: मैंने आज एक सुंदर फूल देखा।

subject: hindi
title: भावनाओं को चित्रों से व्यक्त करना

explanation: बच्चों, चित्रों के माध्यम से हम अपनी भावनाओं को व्यक्त कर सकते हैं। 🎨❤️
examples: जब मैं खुश होता हूँ, तो मैं हंसता हूँ।
जब मैं दुखी होता हूँ, तो मैं रोता हूँ।

practice_questions.q: आपकी खुशी का चित्र कैसे होगा?

subject: hindi
title: हमारा समुदाय

subject: hindi
title: खेल-खेल में गतिविधियाँ

title: नीमा की दादी

title: शेर और गिलहरी की दोस्ती
concept: दोस्ती का महत्व
explanation: दोस्ती में एक-दूसरे की मदद करना और साथ रहना बहुत जरूरी है। 🦁🐿️

subject: hindi
title: सबसे बड़ा छाता
concept: सामानों का उपयोग
explanation: छाता बारिश से बचाता है। ☔️
examples: जब बारिश होती है, तो हम छाता लेते हैं।

practice_questions.q: आपको छाता क्यों पसंद है?
practice_questions.a: मुझे छाता पसंद है क्योंकि यह ...

practice_questions.q: आप बारिश में क्या करते हैं?

subject: maths
title: Counting at the Beach
concept: Counting objects in groups.
explanation: Let's count the objects we see! You can count them one by one or in groups. 🌊🐚

practice_questions.q: How did you count them?
practice_questions.a: I counted them one by one.

practice_questions.q: Did you see any animals at the seashore?

subject: maths
title: Fill in the Blanks
concept: Understanding quantities through fill-in-the-blank exercises.
explanation: Look at the picture and fill in the blanks with the correct numbers! ✏️

practice_questions.q: There are ___________ necklaces of shells.
There are ___________ groups of balloons.

subject: maths
title: Surbhi's Seashell Adventure
concept: Using seashells to make jewelry.
explanation: Surbhi collected seashells to make beautiful bracelets and necklaces! 🌊💍

subject: maths
title: Help Manoj with Chikoos
concept: Counting items in trays.
explanation: Manoj is selling chikoos. Let's help him count how many trays he needs! 🍐

practice_questions.q: How many trays for 43 chikoos?
How many trays for 35 chikoos?

subject: maths
title: Counting with Ten Strips
concept: Understanding units in ten strips.
explanation: A ten strip has 10 units. Let's count how many units are in different strips! 📏

practice_questions.q: How many units in a ten strip?

subject: maths
title: Seashells Tongue Twister
concept: Fun with language and sounds.
explanation: Try saying this tongue twister! It's fun and tricky! 🗣️
text: She sells seashells at the seashore.

subject: maths
title: Let Us Make 100!
concept: Combining ten strips to make 100.
explanation: Let's see how we can combine different strips to make 100 units! 🎉

practice_questions.q: How many yellow and red units make 100?

subject: maths
title: Rolling Two Dice
concept: Understanding addition through dice rolling.
explanation: When you roll two dice, you add the numbers together to take that many units from the bank. If you get 10 or more, you can exchange them for a ten strip! 🎲💰
examples: If you roll a 4 and a 5, you take 9 units.
If you roll a 6 and a 5, you take 11 units and exchange for a ten strip.

subject: maths
title: Counting through Cards
concept: Using cards to represent groups of ten and one.
explanation: You can use cards to help count! Each card represents a group of ten shells and one card represents one shell. 📦🐚

subject: maths
title: Complete the Following
concept: Understanding tens and ones.
explanation: Fill in the blanks with the correct number of tens and ones! 📝

practice_questions.q: So, there are total of 31 shells. Fill in the blanks: ___ tens ___ ones
What is 390 in tens and ones?

subject: maths
title: Make Numbers with Cards
concept: Forming numbers using tens and ones cards.
explanation: You can make numbers by combining tens and ones cards! For example, to make 52, use a 50 card and a 2 card. 🎴✨
examples: To make 27, use a 20 card and a 7 card.

subject: maths
title: Which is More?
concept: Comparing two quantities.
explanation: Let's compare two groups of oranges! Which group has more? 🍊🍊

practice_questions.q: Which is more in number: 43 oranges or 34 oranges?
practice_questions.a: 43 oranges are more because they have 4 tens.

practice_questions.q: Which is less: 27 oranges or 72 oranges?

subject: maths
title: More or Less
concept: Understanding greater and lesser values.
explanation: Fill in the blanks with 'more' or 'less' to compare the numbers! 🆚

practice_questions.q: 67 chikoos are _______ than 76 chikoos.
53 shells are _______ than 35 shells.

subject: maths
title: Make Two-Digit Numbers
concept: Creating two-digit numbers with flash cards.
explanation: Use flash cards to create two-digit numbers! Can you make a number greater than 50? 🎴🔢

practice_questions.q: Which is the smallest two-digit number you can make?
Which is the largest two-digit number you can make?

subject: maths
title: Finding the Largest Number
concept: Identifying the largest number using the same digits.
explanation: Let's play with numbers! You can rearrange the digits to find the largest number possible. For example, with the digits 3, 5, and 7, the largest number you can make is 753! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is the largest number you can make with the digits 6, 2, and 1?
What is the largest number you can make with the digits 8, 0, and 3?

subject: maths
title: Make Your Own Questions
concept: Creating questions about two-digit numbers.
explanation: Now it's your turn! Can you think of your own questions about two-digit numbers? Try to describe them like in the examples! 🤔

subject: maths
title: Decreasing Order Challenge
concept: Arranging numbers in decreasing order.
explanation: Can you start from the largest number and go to the smallest? Make sure to pass through each number at least once! 🚀

subject: maths
title: The Snake Boat Race
concept: Understanding positions in a race.
explanation: In Kerala, there's a fun race called Vallam Kali! Let's see which boat is in the lead and where others are positioned. 🐍🚣‍♂️

practice_questions.q: Which colour boat came first in the race?
Which colour boat is likely to come third?
At which positions are blue and green boats?
Write the position of pink and orange boats.

subject: maths
title: Reading the Calendar
concept: Understanding dates and events on a calendar.
explanation: Let's look at the calendar for August! Can you find out when Onam and Independence Day are celebrated? 📅🎉

practice_questions.q: Onam will be celebrated on which day of the month?
When is Independence Day celebrated?
When is Raksha Bandhan in August?

subject: maths
title: Identifying Shapes of Musical Instruments
concept: Recognizing shapes of different musical instruments.
explanation: Look at the musical instruments! Can you find which ones are shaped like a drum or a matka? Let's explore shapes together! 🎶🔍

practice_questions.q: Circle the musical instruments that are similar in shape to a drum.
Tick the musical instruments that are similar in shape to a matka.
How is the shape of harmonium different from the shape of a drum?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Faces, Edges, and Corners
concept: Identifying faces, edges, and corners of solid objects.
explanation: Let's explore solid shapes! A cube has 6 faces, 12 edges, and 8 corners. Can you find objects around you that have these features? 🧊📦

practice_questions.q: How many stars do we need for a cube?
How many edges does a cuboid have?

subject: maths
title: How Many Items Do We Need?
concept: Calculating the number of decorative items for solid shapes.
explanation: Let's decorate shapes! If a cube has 6 faces, how many stars do we need? Let's count together! 🌟✨
examples: Cube: 6 stars, 12 ribbon stripes, 8 pompom balls

practice_questions.q: For a cuboid, how many stars do we need?
For a cone, how many edges does it have?

subject: maths
title: Guess the Object
concept: Identifying objects based on their features.
explanation: Blindfold your friend and let them guess the object you describe! Can they find something with 3 corners? 🤔👀

subject: maths
title: Fun with Ginladi
concept: Counting beads and recognizing numbers.
explanation: Let's count the beads on the ginladi! How many beads do you see? Let's write the numbers together! 🎉🔢

practice_questions.q: How many beads are there in ginladi?
What number comes after 44?

subject: maths
title: Number Guessing Game
concept: Playing a guessing game with numbers.
explanation: Think of a number and let your friends guess it! They can ask yes/no questions. Can they guess your number in 10 questions? 🤔🎲

subject: maths
title: Ants on a Number Line
concept: Identifying positions on a number line.
explanation: Draw ants on the number line! Where do they sit? Let's find out together! 🐜📏

subject: maths
title: The Jumping Frog
concept: Observing patterns in jumping.
explanation: Look at how Jumpy the frog jumps! Can you see the pattern? 🐸✨

subject: maths
title: Creating a Number Line
concept: Understanding numbers through a number line.
explanation: Let's make a number line on the ground! 🌍 You can jump on it and explore numbers. If you jump in steps of two, which numbers will you land on? 🤔

subject: maths
title: Completing Patterns
concept: Recognizing and completing number patterns.
explanation: Can you fill in the missing numbers in these patterns? Let's see how good you are at spotting patterns! 🕵️‍♂️

subject: maths
title: Skip Counting Practice
concept: Learning skip counting by twos and fives.
explanation: Let's skip count! 🎉 Draw on the number chart while skip counting by twos and fives. Can you find the common numbers? 🤩

subject: maths
title: Jump and Find the Answers
concept: Using jumping to solve counting questions.
explanation: Jump on your number line and answer these questions! 🤸‍♂️ Will you land on the numbers mentioned? Let's find out!
examples: If you start from 10 and jump in tens, will you land on 100?

practice_questions.q: If you start from 5 and count in fives, will you jump on number 40?
If you start from 4 and count in twos, will you jump on number 17?

subject: maths
title: Let Us Jump Backward
concept: Understanding backward counting.
explanation: Let's count backward! 🔙 What comes just before these numbers? Can you fill in the blanks? 📝

subject: maths
title: Discuss Counting Questions
concept: Explaining counting scenarios.
explanation: Let's discuss these counting scenarios! 🤔 Why will Rizwan not say 19 if he starts from 20? Let's think together! 💭

practice_questions.q: Will Rizwan say 19 if he starts counting from 20?
Will Chavi say 43 if she counts in twos from 10?
practice_questions.a: No, because 43 is not in her count.

subject: maths
title: Exploring Number Patterns
concept: Identifying patterns in numbers.
explanation: Let's look at the number chart! Can you find what comes just before 10, 20, 30, and 40? 🌟 Numbers follow patterns, and we can discover them together!

practice_questions.q: What comes just before 10?
What comes just before 20?
What comes just before 30?
What comes just before 40?

subject: maths
title: Fill in the Missing Numbers
concept: Completing number sequences.
explanation: Look at the green numbers in the number chart: 1, 12, 23, ____, ____, ____, ____, ____, ____, ____. Can you fill in the blanks? 📝

subject: maths
title: Number Window Activity
concept: Finding numbers around a central number.
explanation: If the center of your number window is on 28, can you tell me which numbers are above, below, to the right, and to the left? 🔍

practice_questions.q: What number is above 28?
What number is below 28?
What number is on the right of 28?
What number is on the left of 28?

subject: maths
title: Let us Play — Bingo!
concept: Playing Bingo to reinforce number recognition.
explanation: Let's play Bingo! 🎉 Fill a 3 × 4 grid with numbers from 2 to 12. When a child rolls two dice, they will call out the sum, and you cross it off your grid. The first to cross all wins!

subject: maths
title: Nakul and the Shadow Play
concept: Understanding shadows through a story.
explanation: Nakul and his friends saw a shadow play called Togalu Gombeyaata at a village fair. They were amazed and wanted to create their own shadows! 🌟 Can you make shadows with your fingers too?

subject: maths
title: Fun with Shadows
concept: Observing shadows and their changes.
explanation: Put a torchlight on objects around you and see how their shadows change! 🌞 When is your shadow longest? When is it shortest? Let's find out!

practice_questions.q: Will the size of shadows change if you move the torch?
When is your shadow longest?
When does a shadow vanish?

subject: maths
title: Let us Trace
concept: Tracing and drawing shapes of objects.
explanation: Trace the shapes of objects around you like an eraser or a leaf. ✏️ Then, draw the shapes you see! What fun shapes can you create?

subject: maths
title: Hide and Seek with Shapes
concept: Understanding shapes through a fun game.
explanation: Circle and Rectangle are playing hide and seek! Can you help them find good hiding spots? 🌟
examples: Circle can hide behind a clock 🕰️
Rectangle can hide in a shoe box 📦

practice_questions.q: Where can Circle hide?
practice_questions.a: Circle can hide in the clock, ball, or behind a book.

practice_questions.q: Where can Rectangle hide?
practice_questions.a: Rectangle can hide in the shoe box, photo frame, or under the table.

practice_questions.q: Where can Triangle and Square hide?
practice_questions.a: Triangle can hide in a corner, and Square can hide behind a chair.

subject: maths
title: Shapes Join the Game
concept: Shapes interacting and playing together.
explanation: Triangle and Square join Circle and Rectangle in a fun game of hide and seek! Can you guess where they hide? 🎉

subject: maths
title: Colour the Carpet
concept: Identifying shapes and coloring them.
explanation: Let's color the shapes! 🎨 Can you color the triangles red, circles green, rectangles yellow, and squares blue?

subject: maths
title: Catch the Corner Game
concept: Understanding corners and edges through a game.
explanation: In this game, you need to stand near the corners! Can you name the children near the corners? 🏃‍♂️🏃‍♀️

practice_questions.q: Name the children standing near the corners of the table.
practice_questions.a: Udit, Paramjeet, Wasim, Naysa, Seema, Naveen.

practice_questions.q: Can this game be played around a round table? Why or why not?
practice_questions.a: No, because round tables don't have corners!

subject: maths
title: Explore Edges and Corners
concept: Identifying edges and corners in everyday objects.
explanation: Look around you! Can you find objects with edges and corners? 🏠📏

subject: maths
title: Make a Paper Dog
concept: Creating shapes through origami.
explanation: Let's fold a paper to make a cute dog! 🐶 How many corners does it have?

practice_questions.q: How many corners do objects with curved edges have?
Can you fold the square sheet to get a shape with 4 sides?
practice_questions.a: Yes, it will still have 4 corners!

subject: maths
title: Create Your Own Patterns
concept: Making patterns with different shapes.
explanation: Use bottle caps, erasers, and other objects to create fun patterns! 🌈

subject: maths
title: Make Leaf Patterns
