concept: Counting and sharing sea shells.
explanation: Dhruv collected 112 sea shells and wants to make necklaces for his friends. Let's see if he has enough! 🐚✨
examples: If Dhruv takes 28 shells for one necklace, how many shells will he have left after making three necklaces?

practice_questions.q: How many necklaces can Dhruv make from 112 shells?
How many shells are left after making 3 necklaces of 28 shells each?

subject: maths
title: Making Necklaces with Sea Shells
concept: Division and sharing in real-life scenarios.
explanation: Let's help Dhruv figure out how many necklaces he can make and how many shells he has left! 🐚💍
examples: If Dhruv uses 28 shells for each necklace, he can make 4 necklaces from 112 shells.

practice_questions.q: Kannu makes a necklace of 17 sea-shells. How many such necklaces can be made using 100 sea-shells?
Dhruv finds 127 shiny pebbles and distributes them equally to 3 friends. How many will each get?

subject: maths
title: Sharing Equally
concept: Understanding how to share objects equally.
explanation: Shabnam and Mukta are sharing a paratha and chocolates. Let's learn how to share things equally! 🍫🍽️
examples: If a paratha is shared between 2 people, each gets half!

practice_questions.q: How do you share objects equally?
practice_questions.a: By dividing them into equal parts.

practice_questions.q: How many halves make one whole?

subject: maths
title: Activities for Fair Sharing
concept: Practical exercises on sharing equally.
explanation: Let's practice sharing with fun activities! 🎉✂️
examples: Draw lines to show different ways of making a half.

practice_questions.q: Tick the paratha that has been cut equally.
practice_questions.a: Choose the one that looks equal.

practice_questions.q: Draw a line to show one-half of the whole.
practice_questions.a: Draw a line down the middle.

subject: maths
title: Understanding Halves and Quarters
concept: Learning how to share equally using fractions.
explanation: When we share things equally, we can use fractions! For example, if you have 8 marbles and you share them with a friend, each of you gets 4 marbles. That's half! 🍬
examples: If you have 6 apples and share with 2 friends, each gets 2 apples (half). 🍏
When sharing a pizza with 4 friends, each person gets 1/4 of the pizza. 🍕

practice_questions.q: Is this a fair way of sharing? Is there another way to share it fairly?
What part of the chikki did each get?
practice_questions.a: Each person gets a quarter of the chikki.

subject: maths
title: Fill in the Blanks
concept: Understanding the relationship between halves and doubles.
explanation: Let's see how many marbles we can double or halve! If I have 4 marbles, how many is that of 8? 🤔

practice_questions.q: Fill in the blanks: 4 marbles are ______ of 8 marbles.
How far is 5 from half of 14?

subject: maths
title: Fraction Riddles
concept: Using clues to identify fractions.
explanation: Can you guess the fraction based on the clues? 🕵️‍♂️
examples: I have less than double of 3 marbles. I have more than half of 8 marbles.

practice_questions.q: I have less than double of 4 marbles. I have more than half of 10 marbles. What fraction am I?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Quarters
concept: Learning about quarters and how to visualize them.
explanation: When we divide something into 4 equal parts, each part is called a quarter! 🍰
examples: When a whole pizza is shared among 4 people, each gets 1/4 of the pizza.

practice_questions.q: How many quarters in a whole?
practice_questions.a: There are 4 quarters in a whole.

practice_questions.q: Tick the objects that show quarters.
practice_questions.a: Look for shapes divided into 4 equal parts!

subject: maths
title: Choose the Right Fraction
concept: Identifying whether to use half or quarter.
explanation: Let's decide if we should say half or a quarter! 🤔
examples: Shabnam has coloured half of the birds. Mukta has coloured a quarter.

practice_questions.q: Shabnam has coloured half/a quarter of the birds.
Lakshanya tied half/a quarter of her flowers.

subject: maths
title: Counting Crows with Akbar and Birbal
concept: Estimating quantities through counting.
explanation: Akbar wanted to know how many crows were in the city. Birbal estimated there were 963 crows! 🐦 How do you think he figured that out? Sometimes, we can guess how many things there are by thinking about how they might behave.
examples: If I see 5 apples 🍏 on a table, I can guess there might be more in the basket! 🍏🍏🍏🍏🍏

practice_questions.q: How many crows did Birbal say there were?
What did Akbar offer for the correct answer?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Numbers with Tiles
concept: Representing numbers using tiles.
explanation: You can draw tiles to represent numbers! For example, to show the number 832, you can draw 8 hundreds, 3 tens, and 2 ones. 🎨 Let's practice this with different numbers!

practice_questions.q: How would you represent the number 726?
What does the number 504 represent?

subject: maths
title: Locating Numbers on the Number Line
concept: Finding numbers on a number line.
explanation: Let's locate numbers on a number line! 📏 It's like a treasure map where each number has its special place. Can you find where 530 and 628 are?

practice_questions.q: Where is 590 located on the number line?
Locate 759 on the number line.

subject: maths
title: Playing Tambola with Numbers
concept: Using clues to identify numbers.
explanation: Let's play a fun game of Tambola! 🎉 You will fill a grid with numbers and strike out the ones that match the clues. The one with the most strikes wins! 🏆
examples: If the clue is 'A number with 4', you can strike out 574! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What number is between 595 and 605?
What is two more than 610?

subject: maths
title: Helping the Crane Reach Its Food
concept: Understanding number patterns and jumps.
explanation: Teji and Jojo are helping a crane reach its food! 🦢 Let's help them figure out how many steps the crane needs to take. Can you find the right jumps?
examples: To reach the worm, the crane needs to jump from 667 to 700!

practice_questions.q: How many steps does the crane need to reach 650 from 667?
What is the jump from 750 to 800?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Number Sentences
concept: Creating number sentences with given numbers.
explanation: Let's write number sentences using the numbers in the center! For example, if we have 290 and 110, we can write 290 + 110 = 400! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is 290 + 110?
What is 750 + 25?

subject: maths
title: Matching Quantities
concept: Matching numbers with quantities.
explanation: Can you match the number of children in your school with the right number range? Let's see how many books are in your classroom! 📚

practice_questions.q: Match the number of stars in the sky with a range.
How many pages are in your mathematics book?

subject: maths
title: Exploring Special Numbers
concept: Identifying special numbers and patterns.
explanation: Let's have some fun with numbers! Can you find all the hundreds? 🎈
content:
Let us have some fun, with numbers and patterns, everyone!
Look at the hundreds – 100, 200, 300
Can you find all the hundreds?
Some numbers are special, let's explore,
789, 345, and 123, what more?
Jojo wonders why they're so neat,
Teji says 876 and 321 too have the same beat!
Now, numbers that repeat, just the same,
11, 22, 33, have twin digits.
111, 222, 333 are triplet digits.
Can you find more such numbers that follow the pattern?
Here are more numbers that look the same,
from left to right, and right to left:
353, 868.
Finding them is a fun game. Write other such numbers.

subject: maths
title: Creating Numbers with Slips
concept: Forming numbers using paper slips.
explanation: You have 6 slips where you can write 100, 10, or 1. What numbers can you make? Let's find out! ✏️

practice_questions.q: What will you write on these slips for making 231?
practice_questions.a: 2 slips of 100, 3 slips of 10, 1 slip of 1

practice_questions.q: What will you write on these slips for making 123?
practice_questions.a: 1 slip of 100, 2 slips of 10, 3 slips of 1

subject: maths
title: Making Numbers with Digits
concept: Creating numbers using specific digits.
explanation: Using the digits 3 and 8, let's make as many 2 or 3 digit numbers as we can! 🌟

practice_questions.q: What is the smallest number you can make?
What is the largest number you can make?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Word Numerals
concept: Learning to express numbers using words.
explanation: Bhutasankhya means using words to say numbers! For example, instead of saying '1', we can say 'one'. Let's practice! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is the word for the number 5?
How do you say 7 in words?

subject: maths
title: Measuring Length
concept: Understanding different ways to measure length.
explanation: We can measure things using our hands, feet, or even strings! 🖐️👣 Let's explore how we can measure lengths in fun ways!

practice_questions.q: How do Leena and Adi measure the table?
What can you use to measure the height of a door?
practice_questions.a: You can use a string or your hand!

subject: maths
title: Decorate the Board
concept: Using colorful strings to decorate and measure.
explanation: Let's have fun decorating! 🎨 Use strings of different colors to make your board look beautiful! How many strings do you need?

practice_questions.q: How many more colorful paper strings will be needed?
How many strings are needed to decorate the entire border?

subject: maths
title: Shelly and Adi's Challenge
concept: Finding ways to measure and fit objects.
explanation: Shelly and Adi have a heavy table, and they need to figure out if it can go through the door without lifting it! Let's help them! 🏠

practice_questions.q: How can they check if the table fits through the door?
practice_questions.a: By measuring the table with a string!

practice_questions.q: What if the table is too big?
practice_questions.a: They can try turning it or measuring differently!

subject: maths
title: Understanding Measurement
concept: Measuring objects using different tools.
explanation: We can measure things using different tools like pencils, paper strips, and ropes. 📏 Let's see how we can measure the length of a saree using a paper strip! If a pencil is too short, we can use a longer paper strip! 📜
examples: Measuring a saree with a paper strip
Using a metre rod to measure a rope

practice_questions.q: Is measuring with a pencil faster than with a paper strip?
practice_questions.a: No, a paper strip can be longer and faster!

practice_questions.q: Can we measure the length of our height using a rope?
practice_questions.a: Yes, we can use a rope to measure our height!

subject: maths
title: Measure Your Height
concept: Comparing heights of friends.
explanation: Let's measure our heights! 📏 Mark one metre on the wall and see who is taller. Write down the names of your friends who are taller or shorter than one metre! 🌟

subject: maths
title: Finding Object Lengths
concept: Identifying lengths of various objects.
explanation: Look around you! Can you find objects that are one metre long, more than one metre, or less than one metre? 🧸📏 Let's write them down!
examples: A door is more than one metre
A pencil is less than one metre

subject: maths
title: Jumping Measurement
concept: Measuring how far we can jump.
explanation: Let's see how far we can jump! 🏃‍♂️ Mark a start line and jump! Measure how far you jumped using a rope. Was it more than a quarter metre? Let's find out! 🎉

subject: maths
title: Estimating Lengths
concept: Estimating and verifying lengths.
explanation: Can you estimate how long one metre is? 🤔 Cut a piece of wool or thread and see if it's really one metre! Let's check with our friends! 📏

subject: maths
title: Understanding Metres
concept: Measuring length using a metre rope.
explanation: A metre rope helps us measure things! We can use it to see how long something is, like a wall. 🏠
examples: A rope that is 1 metre long can be used to measure the length of your classroom wall.

practice_questions.q: How many cuts did you make if you cut a one-metre long rope into 4 equal pieces?
How many footsteps fit into a metre rope?
practice_questions.a: Depends on the length of your foot!

subject: maths
title: Filling and Lifting
concept: Understanding the capacity of different vessels.
explanation: Different containers hold different amounts of liquid. Let's find out which one holds more! 🥛
examples: A glass holds less than a jug, but more than a small bowl.

practice_questions.q: Who drank the most milk?
practice_questions.a: You need to compare the glasses!

practice_questions.q: Fill in the blanks with ‘more’ or ‘less’: Nita’s glass holds ............. milk than Monu’s glass.

subject: maths
title: Guess and Check
concept: Estimating and measuring capacity.
explanation: Let's guess how many small bowls will fill a glass and then check if we are right! 🎉

practice_questions.q: How many ladles fill the bowl?
How many glasses can be filled by the jug?

subject: maths
title: Whose Glass Holds More?
concept: Comparing the capacity of different glasses.
explanation: Let's find out whose glass holds the most milk! 🥛
examples: Ritu’s glass, Monu’s glass, and Nita’s glass.

practice_questions.q: Who drank the least milk?
practice_questions.a: Check the size of the glasses!

subject: maths
title: Nita and the Milkman
concept: Understanding why measuring cups are used.
explanation: Nita sees the milkman pouring milk using a measuring cup every day. Why do you think he does that? 🤔

subject: maths
title: Understanding 1 Litre
concept: Identifying vessels that hold 1 litre or more.
explanation: Let's explore different vessels to see if they can hold 1 litre or not! 🥛 Use a 1 litre bottle to check if your jug, glass, bowl, or ladle can hold the same amount.
examples: A jug holds more than 1 litre.
A glass holds less than 1 litre.

practice_questions.q: Does the mug hold a litre or half litre of water?
Does the glass hold a litre, half litre, or quarter litre of water?

subject: maths
title: Heavy or Light?
concept: Understanding the concept of weight through comparison.
explanation: Let's compare different objects to see which one is heavier or lighter! 🏋️‍♂️ Use your lunch box and pencil box to find out!
examples: Lunch box is heavier than pencil box.
A balloon filled with air is lighter than a basketball.

practice_questions.q: Which is heavier: your school bag or lunch bag?
Is an apple heavier or lighter than a watermelon?

subject: maths
title: Guess the Weight
concept: Estimating weights using matchboxes.
explanation: Fill a matchbox with sand and use it to guess the weight of different objects! 🏋️‍♀️
examples: Guess how many matchboxes it takes to balance a pencil box.

practice_questions.q: How many matchboxes do you think it will take to balance a spoon?
How many matchboxes are needed to balance 4 marbles?

subject: maths
title: Find Your Bag's Weight
concept: Comparing weights of bags in groups.
explanation: In groups, find a bag that is heavier and one that is lighter than yours! 👜 Discuss why they are different.
examples: Count the number of books in each bag.

practice_questions.q: Why is your bag heavier or lighter?
practice_questions.a: Because of the number of books.

practice_questions.q: Can you make the two bags of about the same weight by moving a book?

subject: maths
title: What is 1 Kilogram?
concept: Identifying objects that weigh 1 kilogram.
explanation: With your parents, find objects at home that weigh 1 kilogram! 🏠 Feel them and guess what else might weigh the same.
examples: A bag of sugar weighs 1 kilogram.

practice_questions.q: What other objects do you think weigh 1 kilogram?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Kilograms
concept: Identifying objects that weigh 1 kilogram.
explanation: Let's learn about weight! We can use a 1 kg salt packet to help us measure. Can you think of objects that might weigh 1 kg? 🤔
examples: A packet of sugar weighs 1 kg 🍬
A small bag of rice weighs 1 kg 🍚

practice_questions.q: Write the names of the objects that are 1 kilogram.
practice_questions.a: Examples include a packet of salt or sugar.

subject: maths
title: Guessing Weights
concept: Comparing weights of objects.
explanation: Can you guess which objects are heavier or lighter than 1 kg? Use a tick mark to show your guesses! ✔️

subject: maths
title: Using a Pan-Balance
concept: Identifying heavier and lighter balls using a pan-balance.
explanation: Montu has a puzzle with 3 balls! One is heavier, and two are the same weight. Can you find the heavy ball using the pan-balance? ⚖️
examples: Red ball, Orange ball, Green ball

practice_questions.q: How many times will you have to weigh?
practice_questions.a: You can find the heavy ball in just one weighing!

subject: maths
title: Kishan and His Saplings
concept: Adding and subtracting saplings.
explanation: Kishan has 364 saplings and he gets 52 more. How many saplings does he have now? Let's add them together! 🌱

practice_questions.q: How many saplings does Kishan have now?

subject: maths
title: How Many More Saplings?
concept: Subtracting saplings to find out how many more are needed.
explanation: Kishan needs to deliver 230 saplings but has packed 75. How many more does he need to pack? Let's subtract! 📦

practice_questions.q: How many more saplings does Kishan need to pack?
practice_questions.a: He needs to pack 155 more saplings!

subject: maths
title: Blocks of 10s
concept: Understanding hundreds, tens, and ones.
explanation: Numbers can be broken down into hundreds, tens, and ones. For example, the number 225 has 2 hundreds, 2 tens, and 5 ones. Let's visualize it! 🎉

subject: maths
title: Kishan's Saplings
concept: Solving word problems using subtraction and addition.
explanation: Let's help Kishan figure out how many saplings he has left after giving some away! 🌱

practice_questions.q: Kishan has 456 saplings and gives away 63. How many does he have left?
Kishan has 309 saplings and gets 80 more. How many does he have now?
Kishan has 270 saplings and his friend has 36. How many more does Kishan have?

subject: maths
title: Using the Grid
concept: Solving addition problems using a grid.
explanation: Use the grid to find answers to the addition problems. Color your answers for fun! 🎨

subject: maths
title: The Village of Jadupur
concept: Understanding barter system and exchange.
explanation: In the village of Jadupur, people exchanged goods based on their needs. Let's see how they did it! 🏡
narrative: Many years ago, in the Village ‘Jadupur’, people exchanged things based on their need. Shaamu Kaka gave 5 sacks of rice to Dariya Didi. She in return gave 10 sacks of vegetables. Dariya Didi got 2 sarees from Bablu Dada by giving 5 sacks of onions. Like this, people in the village exchanged their things. Discuss in class why people in this village had to give different quantities while exchanging things.

subject: maths
title: Using Notes and Coins
concept: Understanding how to use money for purchases.
explanation: Let's learn how to use different notes and coins to buy things! 💰

practice_questions.q: How many ₹100 notes are equal to a ₹500 note?
What things can you buy with ₹100?
practice_questions.a: Two bottles of milk or a basket of pomegranates.

subject: maths
title: Understanding Earnings
concept: Calculating earnings from money transactions.
explanation: When you have some money and earn more, you can find out how much you have earned by subtracting the original amount from the new amount. For example, if you start with ₹465 and end with ₹756, you earned ₹291! 🎉
examples: If you have ₹100 and earn ₹50, you now have ₹150!

practice_questions.q: In the morning, Peter uncle has ₹465. By afternoon, he has ₹756. How much has he earned since morning?

subject: maths
title: Buying and Returning Money
concept: Calculating change after a purchase.
explanation: When you buy something and give more money than the cost, you get change back! If Reena buys groceries for ₹209 and pays with a ₹500 note, she should get back ₹291! 💵
examples: If you buy a toy for ₹150 and pay with ₹200, your change is ₹50!

practice_questions.q: Reena bought groceries for ₹209. She gave a ₹500 note to Peter uncle. How much money should Peter uncle return to Reena?

subject: maths
title: Saving Over Time
concept: Calculating total savings over several weeks.
explanation: If you save some money every week, you can find out how much you have saved in total by adding up your savings. For example, if Shireen saves ₹100 every week for 4 weeks, she will have ₹400! 💰
examples: If you save ₹20 each week for 5 weeks, you will have ₹100!

practice_questions.q: Shireen has ₹150 in her piggy bank. She puts ₹100 every week in her piggy bank. How much money does she have at the end of four weeks?

subject: maths
title: Calculating Total Savings
concept: Finding total savings over multiple months.
explanation: To find out how much money you have saved over several months, just add up all the amounts saved each month! If Peter uncle saved ₹250 in the first month, ₹125 in the second month, and ₹350 in the third month, he saved a total of ₹725! 📊
examples: If you save ₹100 in January, ₹150 in February, and ₹200 in March, your total savings is ₹450!

practice_questions.q: Peter uncle saved ₹250 in the first month, ₹125 in the second month, and ₹350 in the third month. How much has he saved in these three months?

subject: maths
title: Estimating Numbers
concept: Rounding numbers to the nearest hundred.
explanation: When you want to estimate a number, you can round it to the nearest hundred. For example, 156 rounds to 200 and 34 rounds to 0. This makes it easier to add or subtract! 🔢

practice_questions.q: Estimate the nearest hundred for 156 + 34
Estimate the nearest hundred for 125 - 15
Estimate the nearest hundred for 105 + 195

subject: maths
title: Time Goes On
concept: Understanding the calendar months.
explanation: This fun rhyme helps us remember the months of the year and how they can sometimes get mixed up! 🗓️
text:
Appa bought a new calendar,
Amma points a big blunder.
Missing was the month of July,
I wonder, where did it fly?
Don’t worry, don’t worry,
I have a solution to your query.
July and January looks the same,
Let us play the calendar game.

subject: maths
title: Finding the School Reopening Date
concept: Calculating the date when school reopens after closure.
explanation: If the school closes on July 7 for 15 days, we can find out when it will reopen by adding 15 days to July 7. Let's count together! 📅

practice_questions.q: What date will the school reopen?

subject: maths
title: Making a Calendar for July 2024
concept: Creating and understanding a monthly calendar.
explanation: Let's make a fun calendar for July 2024! 🌞 We will write down the days and dates. Can you help fill it in?
examples: July 1 is a Monday, July 2 is a Tuesday, and so on.

practice_questions.q: What day is July 4, 2024?

subject: maths
title: Calendar Observations
concept: Identifying similarities and differences in calendars.
explanation: Look at the calendars from the last two years. What do you see that is the same? What has changed? Let's tick the right answers! ✔️
examples: Months names are the same, but the days can change.

practice_questions.q: Do the names of the months change from year to year?
How many Sundays are there in a year?

subject: maths
title: Guessing Ages
concept: Understanding age relationships.
explanation: Hetal is twice as old as her brother and 10 years older. Can you guess their ages? Let's think together! 🤔
examples: If Hetal is 10, her brother is 5.

practice_questions.q: If Hetal is 12, how old is her brother?

subject: maths
title: Age Fun Activity
concept: Calculating ages based on family relationships.
explanation: Let's find out how old everyone is! Fill in the blanks with your family's ages. 🧑‍👩‍👧
examples: My age today: ___, Age of my mother’s mother when my mother was born: ___.

practice_questions.q: How old is your mother today?
How old was your mother when you were born?

subject: maths
title: Reading Birth Certificates
concept: Understanding birth certificates and calculating age.
explanation: Look at Bincy's birth certificate and answer the questions! 📜

practice_questions.q: What date was Bincy born?
How old will Bincy be on 2 May 2025?

subject: maths
title: Make Your Own Birth Certificate
concept: Creating a personal birth certificate.
explanation: Let's create your own birth certificate! Fill in the details. 🖊️

practice_questions.q: What is your name?
What is your date of birth?

subject: maths
title: Observing Clocks
concept: Understanding how to read and observe clocks.
explanation: Get a working clock and observe how the hands move. Can you tell the time? ⏰
examples: The short hand shows the hour, the long hand shows the minutes.

practice_questions.q: What does the short hand on the clock show?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Time
concept: Learning to read time on a clock.
explanation: A clock has two hands: the hour hand and the minute hand. The hour hand shows the hour, and the minute hand shows the minutes. 🕒 Let's learn how to tell time!

practice_questions.q: How many minutes did Hetal take to eat her breakfast?

subject: maths
title: Draw the Clock Hands
concept: Practicing drawing hour and minute hands.
explanation: Let's draw the hour and minute hands on a clock for different times! 🕑

subject: maths
title: Match Activities with Time
concept: Connecting daily activities with the time they take.
explanation: Match the activity with the time shown on the clock! 🕘

subject: maths
title: Fill the Time Table
concept: Understanding different durations of time.
explanation: Fill in the table with activities that take different amounts of time! ⏳

subject: maths
title: Calculate Time Taken
concept: Calculating time taken for activities.
explanation: Look at the clock and calculate how much time has passed! ⏰

subject: maths
title: Analog vs Digital Clocks
concept: Understanding the difference between analog and digital clocks.
explanation: An analog clock shows time with hands, while a digital clock shows time with numbers. 🕰️

practice_questions.q: What is the difference between an analog clock and a digital clock?
practice_questions.a: Analog clocks have hands, digital clocks show numbers.

subject: maths
title: Make Your Own Sand Clock
concept: Creating a timer using sand.
explanation: Let's make a sand clock to measure time! ⏳ Follow the steps carefully.

subject: maths
topic: A Day in the Life of Hetal
title: Hetal's Daily Routine
concept: Understanding daily activities and their timings.
explanation: Join Hetal as she goes through her day, learning about time! 🌞

subject: maths
title: Understanding Symmetry
concept: Identifying symmetrical and non-symmetrical objects.
explanation: Symmetry means that one half of an object is a mirror image of the other half. For example, if you fold a butterfly in half, both sides look the same! 🦋

practice_questions.q: How many malas can be made?
practice_questions.a: You can make different malas by using various colors and arrangements.

practice_questions.q: Tick the malas that are symmetrical.
practice_questions.a: The malas with two identical halves are symmetrical.

subject: maths
title: Create Your Own Mala
concept: Using beads to create symmetrical malas.
explanation: Let's make malas with beads! Use 4 beads of one color and 4 of another to create a symmetrical pattern. 🌈
examples: 4 red beads and 4 blue beads make a symmetrical mala.

subject: maths
title: Exploring Rangolis
concept: Understanding symmetrical rangolis.
explanation: Rangolis are beautiful patterns made on the ground. Some are symmetrical, meaning they look the same on both sides! 🎨
examples: A rangoli with 6 dots on each side is symmetrical.

practice_questions.q: Are all rangolis symmetrical?
practice_questions.a: No, some rangolis can be asymmetrical.

practice_questions.q: Draw and complete the symmetrical rangolis given below.
practice_questions.a: Use your creativity to finish the patterns!

subject: maths
title: Create Your Own Rangoli
concept: Drawing symmetrical rangolis.
explanation: Draw some more rangolis in your notebook that are symmetrical! Use dots to help you. 🌼

subject: maths
title: Creating Symmetrical Masks
concept: Understanding how to make symmetrical masks.
explanation: Let's make a mask! Fold a paper, draw on one side, and cut it out. When you open it, you’ll have a symmetrical mask! 🎭
examples: A cat mask drawn and cut out symmetrically.

subject: maths
title: Understanding Mirror Images
concept: Mirror images are reflections of objects.
explanation: When you look at something in a mirror, you see its reflection. Just like how Soni and Avi played with counters, the counters on one side are the mirror image of the other side! 🪞✨
examples: 🟡🟡🟡🟡 on one side and 🟡🟡🟡🟡 on the other side

practice_questions.q: What is the trick the painter is playing?
practice_questions.a: The painter is drawing only half of the picture.

practice_questions.q: Can you place counters to create a mirror image?
practice_questions.a: Yes, by placing them opposite to each other.

subject: maths
title: Playing the Mirror Game
concept: Using mirrors to understand symmetry.
explanation: In the mirror game, you can see how objects reflect and create symmetry. Let's play with counters! 🎲
examples: Place 4 counters on one side and see the mirror image on the other side.

practice_questions.q: Has Avi placed the counters at the right places?
Can you rearrange Micy's side to match Catty's?

subject: maths
title: Creating Tiled Paths
concept: Using shapes to fill spaces without gaps.
explanation: When we create paths with tiles, we need to make sure there are no gaps or overlaps. Let's use rangometry shapes! 🟩🟦
examples: Fill a square with triangle tiles without leaving any gaps.

practice_questions.q: How can you fill shapes with no gaps?
What happens if there are gaps between tiles?
practice_questions.a: It won't look nice and won't be complete!

subject: maths
title: Finding Dada and Dadi
concept: Using directions to find someone.
explanation: Soni and Avi are looking for their Dada and Dadi at the fair. They need to follow directions to find them! 🧭
examples: Take 2 steps to your right, then 1 step forward.

practice_questions.q: Where is the chaupal?
practice_questions.a: It's where Dada and Dadi are waiting.

practice_questions.q: Can you help find the chaupal?

subject: maths
title: Understanding the Map of the Fair
concept: Learning to read and interpret a map.
explanation: Maps help us find our way! Just like a treasure map, it shows us where to go! 🗺️✨
examples: The hut shows the handicraft stalls. 🏠
The play area is marked with a fun picture! 🎡

practice_questions.q: Which place does the sign show?
practice_questions.a: Look at the map to find the answer!

practice_questions.q: Circle the picture in the map that shows the play area?
practice_questions.a: Find the picture of the play area and circle it!

practice_questions.q: How many exit routes are there in the fair?
practice_questions.a: Count the exit signs on the map!

subject: maths
title: Navigating the Fair
concept: Learning to follow directions step by step.
explanation: Following directions is like a fun game! 🎮 Let's go on an adventure! 🏃‍♂️🏃‍♀️
examples: Turn right on the green lane. ➕

practice_questions.q: What do you see when you turn right?
Where do you meet Dada Dadi?

subject: maths
title: Finding the Way Out
concept: Exploring paths and sharing experiences.
explanation: Let's find our way out of the maze together! 🌀 What did you see on the way? 🌟

practice_questions.q: Share the way you went through the maze. What did you find?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Numbers
concept: Learning to recognize and use numbers.
explanation: Numbers are everywhere! Let's learn to read them! 🔢✨

practice_questions.q: What number comes after 5?
Can you count to 10?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Fractions
concept: Learning about whole and parts.
explanation: Fractions show us parts of a whole! 🍰 Let's share a cake! 🎂
examples: 1/2 of a cake, 1/4 of a pizza

practice_questions.q: What is half of a whole?
How many quarters make a whole?

subject: maths
title: Creating Shapes
concept: Learning about shapes and their nets.
explanation: Shapes are fun to create! Let's make cubes and cones! 🧊🎈

practice_questions.q: What shape can you make with this net?
Can you fold this net into a cone?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Numbers
concept: Recognizing and writing numbers up to 100.
explanation: Numbers are like magic symbols that help us count and measure things! Let's learn how to write and recognize numbers up to 100! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What comes after 25?
Write the number that comes before 10.

subject: maths
title: Adding Numbers
concept: Combining two or more numbers to find the total.
explanation: Addition is like putting together! If you have 🍎 2 apples and 🍎 3 apples, how many apples do you have in total? Let's find out! 🍏

practice_questions.q: What is 4 + 5?
If you have 3 candies and get 2 more, how many do you have?

subject: maths
title: Subtracting Numbers
concept: Taking away one number from another.
explanation: Subtraction is like taking away! If you have 🍎 5 apples and you give 🍎 2 apples to a friend, how many apples do you have left? 🍏

practice_questions.q: What is 7 - 3?
If you have 10 balloons and 4 fly away, how many do you have?

subject: maths
title: Maths Practice
concept: Exercises to reinforce learning.
explanation: Let's practice what we've learned! Solve these fun problems! 📝

practice_questions.q: What is 3 + 2?
What is 6 - 1?
If you have 🍎 4 apples and you buy 🍎 3 more, how many do you have?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Whole Numbers
concept: Whole numbers are numbers without fractions or decimals.
explanation: Whole numbers start from 0 and go up like 0, 1, 2, 3... 🌟 Let's count some apples! 🍎🍎🍎 means we have 3 whole apples!

practice_questions.q: What is the smallest whole number?
Count the number of stars: ⭐⭐⭐⭐

subject: maths
title: Introduction to Fractions
concept: Fractions represent a part of a whole.
explanation: If you have a pizza 🍕 and you cut it into 4 equal pieces, each piece is a fraction of the whole pizza. If you take 1 piece, you have 1/4 of the pizza!

practice_questions.q: What fraction is 1 piece of a pizza cut into 4?
If you have 2 out of 5 candies, what fraction do you have?

subject: maths
title: Exploring Shapes
concept: Shapes are the forms of objects around us.
explanation: Look around! 🏠 A house is a square, a ball is a circle ⚽, and a book is a rectangle 📚. Shapes are everywhere!

practice_questions.q: What shape is a clock?
How many sides does a triangle have?

subject: maths
title: Understanding Measurement
concept: Measurement helps us find out how long, heavy, or much something is.
explanation: We can measure length with a ruler 📏, weight with a scale ⚖️, and capacity with a jug 🥤. Let's measure how tall you are!
examples: Length: 5 cm, Weight: 2 kg, Capacity: 1 liter

practice_questions.q: What tool do we use to measure length?
How do we measure weight?

subject: maths
title: Let Us Play
concept: Games make learning math fun!
explanation: Let's play a game where we count how many fruits we have! 🍌🍌🍌🍌 How many bananas are there? Let's find out together!

subject: maths
title: Why Mathematics is Fun
concept: Mathematics helps us understand the world around us.
explanation: Mathematics is like a magical tool that helps us solve problems and discover new things! 🌍✨
examples: Measuring the length of a pencil ✏️

practice_questions.q: What do you think mathematics helps us with?

subject: maths
title: Let Us Do Activities
concept: Engaging activities make learning fun.
explanation: Activities like games and puzzles help us learn math in a fun way! 🎲🧩

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite math game?

subject: maths
title: Let Us Think
concept: Thinking critically helps us understand math better.
explanation: When we think about math problems, we can find different ways to solve them! 🤔💡
examples: Finding two ways to add numbers

practice_questions.q: How can you solve this problem in two different ways?
practice_questions.a: I can add or use a number line!

subject: maths
title: Let Us Explore
concept: Exploration leads to discovery in math.
explanation: Exploring different math concepts helps us learn and discover new ideas! 🌟🔍

practice_questions.q: What did you discover while exploring math?
practice_questions.a: I found out how to measure things!

subject: maths
title: Let Us Discuss
concept: Talking about math helps us understand it better.
explanation: When we discuss math with others, we can share ideas and learn from each other! 🗣️🤝
examples: Explaining a concept to a family member

practice_questions.q: Why is it important to talk about math?
practice_questions.a: It helps us learn from each other!

subject: maths
title: Math Practice
concept: Practice makes perfect!
explanation: Let's solve some fun math problems to practice what we've learned! 📚✏️
examples: 2. How many sides does a triangle have? 🔺

practice_questions.q: What is 4 + 1?
How many corners does a square have?

subject: maths
title: Importance of Sharing Ideas
concept: Discussing ideas enhances learning.
explanation: When children talk about their ideas, they learn from each other! 💬 It's like sharing secrets that help everyone grow smarter! 🌱
examples: Child A shares how they solved a math problem, and Child B learns a new method.

practice_questions.q: Why is it important to share ideas?
practice_questions.a: It helps everyone learn and find new solutions!

subject: maths
title: Different Ways to Assess Learning
concept: Assessment can be fun and varied!
explanation: We can assess learning through games, pictures, and even by creating things! 🎨 This makes learning exciting and helps teachers understand how well we are doing! 📊
examples: Using blocks to show how many apples 🍎 are in a basket.

practice_questions.q: What are some fun ways to show what you learned?
practice_questions.a: By drawing, playing games, or making models!

subject: maths
title: Practice Makes Perfect!
concept: Completing tasks helps improve skills.
explanation: Doing tasks on paper helps you practice what you've learned! ✏️ It's like training for a race! 🏃‍♂️
examples: Solve these word problems: If you have 3 apples 🍏 and get 2 more, how many do you have?

practice_questions.q: How can you practice math at home?
practice_questions.a: By doing exercises and solving problems!

subject: maths
title: Understanding Numbers
concept: Numbers are used to count and measure.
explanation: Numbers help us count things like 🍎 apples or 🐶 dogs! Let's learn how to use them in everyday life.

practice_questions.q: How many apples do you see? 🍎🍎

subject: maths
title: Adding Numbers
concept: Addition means putting things together.
explanation: When we add, we combine two groups. For example, if you have 🍎🍎 and you get 🍎, how many do you have now? Let's find out!

practice_questions.q: What is 1 + 2?

subject: maths
title: Taking Away Numbers
concept: Subtraction means taking away from a group.
explanation: When we subtract, we remove some items. If you have 🍎🍎🍎 and you eat 🍎, how many are left? Let's see!

practice_questions.q: What is 3 - 1?

subject: maths
title: Counting Fun
concept: A fun way to learn counting.
explanation: Let's sing a rhyme to count together! 🎶
text:
One, two, buckle my shoe, 
Three, four, open the door, 
Five, six, pick up sticks, 
Seven, eight, lay them straight, 
Nine, ten, a big fat hen!

subject: maths
title: Math Exercises
concept: Practice makes perfect!
explanation: Let's solve some problems to get better at math! 🧠

practice_questions.q: What is 2 + 3?
What is 5 - 2?

subject: maths
title: Jaya He
concept: Celebration of India's unity and diversity
explanation: This poem is our national anthem, celebrating the beauty and strength of India. It reminds us of our rich culture and heritage. 🇮🇳

subject: maths
title: Understanding Names
concept: The importance of names and their meanings
explanation: Names are special! They tell us about our identity and culture. Just like how every person has a unique name, every place does too! 🌍
examples: My name is Riya, and it means 'singer'! 🎤

practice_questions.q: What does your name mean?
practice_questions.a: It means something special about you!

subject: maths
title: Fun with Toys
concept: Exploring different types of toys and their uses
explanation: Toys are not just fun, they help us learn! From building blocks to dolls, each toy has a purpose. 🧸🛴
examples: Blocks can be used to build tall towers! 🏗️
Dolls can help us learn about caring for others! 👶

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite toy and why?
practice_questions.a: My favorite toy is a car because I love to race!

subject: maths
title: A Special Visit
concept: The joy of spending time with family
explanation: This story is about a child who visits their grandmother and learns valuable lessons about love and family. ❤️

subject: maths
title: Learning Shapes
concept: Identifying and understanding different shapes
explanation: Shapes are all around us! From circles to squares, they help us understand the world better. 🔵🔲
examples: A window is usually a rectangle! 🪟

practice_questions.q: Can you name a shape that is round?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Introduction to Motor Skills
concept: Understanding gross and fine motor skills.
explanation: We use our big muscles for activities like jumping and throwing (gross motor skills) and our small muscles for tasks like writing and drawing (fine motor skills). 🎉

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Throw a Ball
concept: Steps to perform a proper throw.
explanation: To throw a ball, hold it with your fingers spread, twist your body, and swing your arm forward. Remember to balance your body! ⚾️

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Catch a Ball
concept: Steps to catch a ball effectively.
explanation: To catch a ball, stand with your knees slightly bent, keep your eyes on the ball, and form a cup with your hands. Catch it softly! 👐
examples: Catching a ball at eye level

subject: physicaleducation
title: Catch and Throw Game
concept: Practicing throwing and catching with a partner.
explanation: Pair up with a friend and practice throwing and catching the ball without dropping it! Have fun! 🎉

subject: physicaleducation
title: Pick Up and Throw Variations
concept: Different ways to practice throwing.
explanation: Try switching your throwing arm after each round and alternate between different throwing styles! It makes practice exciting! 🏃‍♂️

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Derarm Throwing
concept: Developing throwing skills and motor abilities.
explanation: In this game, we form two teams and throw paper balls to the other side of the court. The team with fewer balls on their side wins! 🎉
examples: Throwing paper balls across the court.

practice_questions.q: What do you need to form teams?
practice_questions.a: You need to divide players into two teams.

practice_questions.q: How do you win the game?
practice_questions.a: By having fewer balls on your side.

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Hit the Target
concept: Improving accuracy and physical fitness.
explanation: In this game, you throw a soft ball at a circle on the wall. Try to hit the circle without crossing the line! 🎯
examples: Throwing a ball at a circle on the wall.
Competing to see who can hit the target the most.

practice_questions.q: What do you need to throw the ball?
practice_questions.a: You need a soft sponge ball or socks.

practice_questions.q: How do you win the game?
practice_questions.a: By hitting the circle the most times.

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Knock the Cones
concept: Enhancing focus and social interaction.
explanation: Teams try to knock down cones with soft balls. The team that knocks down the most cones wins! 🥳

practice_questions.q: What do you need to play this game?
practice_questions.a: You need soft balls and cones.

practice_questions.q: How do you win?
practice_questions.a: By knocking down more cones than the other team.

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Relay Catching
concept: Building coordination and readiness.
explanation: In this game, team leaders throw balls to their teammates, who catch and return them. The team with the most catches wins! 🏆
examples: Catching balls in a relay race.
Running to the end of the line after catching.

practice_questions.q: What do you need to play?
practice_questions.a: You need a soft ball or tennis ball.

practice_questions.q: How do you win the game?

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Ball on the Wall
concept: Developing catching skills and confidence.
explanation: Throw the ball against the wall and catch it when it bounces back. The team with the most catches wins! 🥅
examples: Catching a ball after it hits the wall.
Playing with friends to see who can catch the most.

practice_questions.q: What do you need for this game?
practice_questions.a: You need a handball or tennis ball.

practice_questions.q: How do you win?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Introduction to Kicking and Receiving
concept: Understanding the basics of kicking and receiving a ball.
explanation: In football, we kick the ball to our partner and receive it back. Kicking helps us move the ball in the right direction! ⚽
examples: Kicking a ball to a friend.
Receiving a pass in a game.

practice_questions.q: What do you do when you kick the ball?
practice_questions.a: You send it to your partner.

practice_questions.q: What happens when you receive the ball?

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Kick a Ball
concept: Learn the proper technique for kicking a ball.
explanation: To kick a ball, stand behind it and focus your eyes on the ball. Move towards it, keeping your non-kicking leg to the side. Swing your kicking leg to kick the ball and follow through to maintain balance. 🦵⚽

practice_questions.q: What should you focus on when kicking the ball?
practice_questions.a: You should focus on the ball.

practice_questions.q: What do you do with your non-kicking leg?
practice_questions.a: Keep it to the side of the ball.

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Receive a Ball
concept: Learn the proper technique for receiving a ball.
explanation: To receive a ball, stand with your knees bent and face the direction of the ball. Raise one leg to receive it while keeping the other leg firm on the ground. Balance your body as the ball touches the ground. 🤸‍♂️⚽

practice_questions.q: What position should your knees be in when receiving a ball?
What do you do with your non-receiving foot?
practice_questions.a: Keep it firm on the ground.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Ball Passing Exercise
concept: Practice passing a ball between your feet.
explanation: Stand with a soft ball between your feet. Pass the ball from one leg to another while balancing your body. You can walk, jog, or run while doing this! 🏃‍♂️⚽

practice_questions.q: What should you do with your body while passing the ball?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Shadow Ball Exercise
concept: Develop coordination by following a partner.
explanation: Pair up with a classmate. One person dribbles the ball while the other follows. When the whistle blows, face each other and kick the ball back and forth! 🤝⚽
examples: Kicking the ball to a friend

practice_questions.q: What do you do when the whistle blows?
practice_questions.a: Face each other and kick the ball.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Flicking the Ball Exercise
concept: Practice flicking the ball with your foot.
explanation: Stand facing your partner and flick the ball using the top of your foot. Alternate legs while flicking and catch the ball when your partner flicks it back! 🎾👟
examples: Flicking a ball back and forth

practice_questions.q: What part of your foot do you use to flick the ball?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Flicking the Ball into a Box
concept: Aim to flick the ball into a box.
explanation: Place a box on the ground and stand at a distance. Take turns flicking the ball into the box. The team that gets the most balls in wins! 🏆📦
examples: Flicking balls into a target box

practice_questions.q: What do you aim for when flicking the ball?
practice_questions.a: Aim for the box on the ground.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Monkey in the Middle
concept: Teamwork and communication through play.
explanation: In this game, one player is in the middle trying to catch the ball while others pass it around. It's all about teamwork! 🤝
examples: Passing the ball without getting caught!
Using different body parts to pass the ball.

practice_questions.q: What happens if the ball is caught?
practice_questions.a: The player who passed the ball goes to the middle.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Sit and Rise
concept: Cognitive development through anticipation.
explanation: In this game, players must react quickly when they receive the ball. If they don't, they switch places with the player in the center! 🏃‍♂️
examples: Receiving the ball with your foot!
Sitting down quickly when the ball is passed.

practice_questions.q: What should you do if you don't catch the ball?
practice_questions.a: You switch places with the player in the center.

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Strike the Ball
concept: Proper technique for striking a ball.
explanation: To hit the ball, stand sideways, bend your knees, and swing the bat while keeping your eyes on the ball. It's like a dance move! 💃
examples: Swinging the bat to hit a cricket ball.
Following through after hitting the ball.

practice_questions.q: What should you do with your eyes while hitting?
practice_questions.a: Always keep your eyes on the ball!

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Receive the Ball with a Stick
concept: Techniques for receiving a ball.
explanation: When receiving a ball with a stick, stand with your knees bent and predict where the ball will go. It's like being a superhero! 🦸‍♂️
examples: Using a hockey stick to receive a ball.
Keeping your body low to catch the ball.

practice_questions.q: How should you hold the stick?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Strike the Balloon
concept: Improving hand-eye coordination and timing.
explanation: In this game, you throw a balloon in the air and try to hit it with your fingertips. The goal is to keep it from touching the ground! 🎈
examples: Throw the balloon and tap it with your fingers!
Play in teams to see who can keep the most balloons in the air.

practice_questions.q: What do you need to do when the balloon is in the air?
practice_questions.a: You need to strike it with your fingertips!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Body and Balloon
concept: Development of focus and concentration.
explanation: In this activity, you throw a balloon and hit it with different parts of your body like your head or shoulder. Keep it from touching the ground! 🥳
examples: Hit the balloon with your palm!
Try using your shoulder to keep it up!

practice_questions.q: Which body parts can you use to hit the balloon?
practice_questions.a: You can use your head, shoulder, chest, hand, or thigh!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Soft Ball Control
concept: Development of strength and control.
explanation: Using a stick, you will push a soft ball through cones. This helps you learn how to control the ball better! ⚽️
examples: Push the ball through the cones!

practice_questions.q: What do you use to push the ball?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Playing Pass
concept: Engagement of body parts in coordination.
explanation: Find a partner and gently pass a ball back and forth using a stick. This helps you learn how to work together! 🤝
examples: Pass the ball gently to your partner!
Increase the speed as you get better!

practice_questions.q: What do you do with your partner?
practice_questions.a: You pass the ball back and forth!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Scoop in the Square
concept: Building confidence through success.
explanation: In this game, you scoop a soft ball into squares. The more you succeed, the more confident you become! 🏆
examples: Scoop the ball into the first square!
Try to get it into the next square!

practice_questions.q: What happens if you scoop the ball into the square?
practice_questions.a: You get to try again for the next square!

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Receiving with Stick
concept: Developing receiving skills with a stick.
explanation: In this game, you and a partner will throw and catch a soft ball using a stick. You need to run to catch the ball while it's thrown away from you! 🏃‍♂️🏃‍♀️
examples: Throw the ball to your partner while they run!
Try to catch the ball while moving.

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Balancing the Stick
concept: Improving balance by balancing a stick.
explanation: Try to balance a stick on your palm without letting it fall! The longer you can balance, the better you get! 🏆
examples: Challenge your friends to see who can balance the longest.

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Keep on Moving
concept: Enhancing coordination and teamwork.
explanation: In this game, you and your friends will hold hands and try to move from one line to another without touching the person in the middle! 🤝🏃‍♂️
examples: Form a line and move together!
Try to reach the finish line first!

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Hold your Ground
concept: Improving stability and exercise skills.
explanation: Jump, run, and walk around boxes, and when the music stops, do the exercise in the box you land on! 🎶🏃‍♀️
examples: Jump into a box and do 10 jumping jacks!
Run to a box and hold a plank.

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Do Planks
concept: Strengthening core muscles.
explanation: Lie down and lift your body with your elbows and toes. Try to hold this position for as long as you can! 💪⏳
examples: Start with 2 seconds and try to go up to 10 seconds!
Feel the strength in your tummy!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Find the House
concept: Running and jumping in a circle.
explanation: In this game, you will run around a big circle and jump into hoops! 🏃‍♂️🌀

practice_questions.q: What do you do when the second whistle blows?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Chor-Police
concept: A game of stealth and freezing.
explanation: In this game, one person is the police and the others are thieves trying to steal an object! 🤫🚓

practice_questions.q: What happens when the guard turns around?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Shuttle Run
concept: Team relay with skipping.
explanation: In this fun race, you will skip to pick up balls and bring them back to your team! 🏃‍♀️🏐

practice_questions.q: What do you do after picking up the ball?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Dodging Move to Safety
concept: Running and dodging to avoid being caught.
explanation: In this game, you will run and dodge to reach the finish line without getting tagged! 🏃‍♂️💨

practice_questions.q: What do you need to do to score points?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Disease and Vaccine Game
concept: Understanding the importance of vaccines in preventing diseases.
explanation: In this fun game, we learn about diseases and their vaccines! Players are divided into two groups: one group represents diseases and the other represents vaccines. The vaccine group tries to catch the disease players to show how vaccines work to protect us. 🦠💉

practice_questions.q: What do vaccines do?
practice_questions.a: Vaccines help protect us from diseases!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Seven Stones Game
concept: Playing traditional games fosters teamwork and physical skills.
explanation: The Seven Stones game is a fun activity where players work in teams to knock down a tower of stones and dodge balls! This game helps us learn to cooperate and have fun together. 🏐🪨
examples: Throwers try to knock down the stones
Defenders try to catch the ball

practice_questions.q: What do you shout when you win?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Learning Fruits and Vegetables
concept: Using games to learn about fruits and vegetables.
explanation: In Golaap Togor, players learn about different fruits and vegetables while having fun! One player is blindfolded and must guess the fruit or vegetable called by their leader. 🍎🥕

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite fruit?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Guess Who Tapped You
concept: Identifying players through touch.
explanation: In this game, one player is blindfolded and has to guess who tapped them on the back. It helps to build teamwork and empathy! 🤝

practice_questions.q: What do you feel when you guess correctly?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Chasing Game
concept: Chasing and tagging in a defined area.
explanation: In Pakdam-Pakdai, one player is the 'den' who chases others. If they touch someone, that player becomes the new den! 🏃‍♂️💨
examples: Runner B becomes the new den

practice_questions.q: How do you feel when you are the den?
practice_questions.a: I feel excited and ready to chase!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Forming a Chain
concept: Working together as a team.
explanation: In this game, when the den touches a runner, they join hands to form a chain and chase together! 🌟
examples: Den touches Runner A, forming a chain

practice_questions.q: What is important when forming a chain?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Blindfolded Tag
concept: Using sound to locate players.
explanation: In Aankh Micholi, one player is blindfolded and tries to tag others based on the sounds they make. It’s a fun way to use your ears! 👂🎶

practice_questions.q: How do you find players when you can't see?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Jumping Over
concept: Jumping over obstacles.
explanation: In Bitta Kud, players jump over a stick or cloth held by a pair sitting on the ground. It’s all about coordination and fun! 🏃‍♀️🪢
examples: Player A jumps over the stick

practice_questions.q: What do you need to do to jump successfully?
practice_questions.a: I need to focus and jump high!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Discussing Fruits and Vegetables
concept: Understanding the benefits of healthy food.
explanation: Let’s talk about why fruits and vegetables are good for us! They help us grow strong and stay healthy! 🍎🥦

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite fruit and why?
practice_questions.a: I love bananas because they are sweet!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Jumping Over the Stick
concept: Jumping over obstacles to build strength and courage.
explanation: In this game, players jump over a stick. If they fail, they become the new den. The stick's height increases if everyone jumps successfully! 🎉
examples: Jumping over a stick with friends
Using a cloth or wooden stick as an obstacle

practice_questions.q: What happens if you fail to jump over the stick?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Playing Hopscotch
concept: A fun game that improves balance and coordination.
explanation: In hopscotch, you throw a marker onto squares and hop to retrieve it. You must hop on one foot or both feet depending on the squares! 🦶🏽

practice_questions.q: What do you do if your marker doesn't land correctly?
practice_questions.a: Your turn ends and you start from where you stopped.

subject: physicaleducation
title: One Leg Skipping
concept: A game that enhances balance and coordination.
explanation: In this game, players hop on one leg while trying to push others out of a circle. The last one standing wins! 🏆
examples: Hopping on one leg inside a circle
Trying to push friends out of the circle

practice_questions.q: How do you win this game?
practice_questions.a: By being the last person to remain on one leg!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Crocodile Game
concept: A game that promotes alertness and quick reactions.
explanation: In Kumir Denga, one player is the crocodile and the others are runners. Runners must dodge the crocodile while moving between land areas! 🐊
examples: Teasing the crocodile while staying safe

practice_questions.q: What happens if the crocodile catches a runner?
practice_questions.a: The caught runner becomes the new crocodile!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Hum Phoolon Ki Sadak Par
concept: Walking on the road of flowers.
explanation: In this game, we walk towards each other while saying fun slogans! 🌼
examples: We walk on the road of flowers 🌸
We walk in the cool breeze 🌬️

subject: physicaleducation
title: Team Challenge Game
concept: Engaging in a fun challenge with teams.
explanation: Two teams take turns to challenge each other by picking players! 🤝
examples: First team asks, 'Who do you want to take?'

practice_questions.q: Why is it important to choose players of equal strength?
practice_questions.a: To make the game fair and fun!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Playing Dodge Ball
concept: Dodging balls while staying safe.
explanation: One player tries to hit others with a soft ball while they dodge! 🏐
examples: The den stands in the center.
Dodgers try to avoid getting hit.

practice_questions.q: What movements help in dodging the ball?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Spoon Race Challenge
concept: Balancing a spoon with a lemon or marble.
explanation: Players walk to the finish line without dropping their lemon! 🍋
examples: Walking with a spoon in your mouth.

practice_questions.q: How does concentration help in this game?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Playing Gadda Maar
concept: Hitting players with a soft ball.
explanation: One player tries to hit others with a soft ball while they run! ⚽
examples: The player asks, 'Are you all ready?'
Players run around to avoid getting hit.

practice_questions.q: What injuries can happen during the game?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Hum Phoolon Ki Sadak Par
concept: A fun rhyme about walking.
explanation: This rhyme makes our game more exciting! 🎶

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Tug of War
concept: Teamwork and strategy in a fun game.
explanation: In Tug of War, two teams pull on a rope to try to get the other team across a line. It's all about teamwork! 💪
examples: Two teams pulling a rope at a picnic.
Kids playing Tug of War during a sports day.

practice_questions.q: What do you need to play Tug of War?

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Three-legged Race
concept: Coordination and teamwork in a race.
explanation: In the Three-legged Race, two players tie their legs together and race to the finish line. It's super fun and helps you work together! 🏃‍♂️🏃‍♀️
examples: Kids racing with their legs tied at a school event.
Friends laughing while trying to run together.

practice_questions.q: What happens if you fall during the race?
practice_questions.a: You get back up and keep trying!

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play In and Out
concept: Following instructions and quick reactions.
explanation: In the In and Out game, you jump in and out of a ring based on the teacher's commands. It's a great way to practice listening! 🎉
examples: Jumping in and out of a circle during gym class.
Friends playing the game during recess.

practice_questions.q: What do you do when the teacher says 'Kullam'?

subject: physicaleducation
title: How to Play Light and Shade
concept: Agility and teamwork in a fun chase.
explanation: In Light and Shade, one team hides in the shadows while the other team is in the light. If you're touched, you switch teams! 🌞🌑
examples: Playing in the park with friends, dodging in and out of shadows.
Kids laughing while trying to tag each other.

practice_questions.q: What happens when a Shade player is touched by a Light player?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Introduction to Yoga
concept: Yoga for a healthy body and mind.
explanation: Yoga helps us stay healthy and strong! It teaches us to be kind and think positive. Let's stretch and have fun! 🧘‍♀️🧘‍♂️
examples: Doing yoga poses in a class.

practice_questions.q: Why is yoga important?
practice_questions.a: It helps us stay healthy and happy!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Activities that Make Us Happy
concept: Engaging in joyful activities enhances happiness.
explanation: We do many fun activities every day that make us smile! 😊 What activities do you enjoy? Let's explore them together!

practice_questions.q: Do you like any other activities? If yes, write them below.
Share your experiences of happy moments with your friends in the class.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Activities for Strength and Health
concept: Certain activities help us stay strong and healthy.
explanation: Eating nutritious food and exercising daily keeps our bodies fit! 💪 What activities do you think help us?

practice_questions.q: Discuss in pairs about the importance of each activity given above.
How do you feel after exercising?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Acts of Kindness
concept: Being kind to others spreads happiness.
explanation: When we are kind, we make others happy! 😊 Let's see how we can show kindness every day.

practice_questions.q: How do you feel when you fight with your friends?
What do you do if your friend falls down and gets hurt?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Introduction to Yoga
concept: Yoga helps us become happy and healthy.
explanation: Yoga has eight aspects that guide us to live joyfully! 🧘‍♂️ Let's start our Yoga journey with Yama and Niyama.

practice_questions.q: What is Yama?
practice_questions.a: Friendly rules like sharing and kindness.

practice_questions.q: What does Ahimsa mean?
practice_questions.a: Not to hurt others in thoughts, words, or actions.

subject: physicaleducation
title: The Kind Prince
concept: Kindness can change the world around us.
explanation: Once upon a time, in the kingdom of Kosala, there were two princes. One was kind and gentle, while the other was harsh. This story teaches us the importance of kindness.
narrative: Once upon a time in the kingdom of Kosala Desha, there were two princes. Prince Siddhartha was known for his gentle and kind nature, while his brother, Prince Devadatta, was harsh and rude. One day, while the princes were playing with their bows and arrows, Devadatta took aim at a graceful swan flying above them. Before Siddhartha could react, the arrow struck the swan, causing it great pain. Acting quickly, Siddhartha...

subject: physicaleducation
title: The Story of Siddhartha and the Swan
concept: Kindness and compassion towards all living beings.
explanation: Siddhartha helped a wounded swan and took care of it until it was healthy again. This teaches us to be kind and compassionate to others, including animals. 🦢❤️

practice_questions.q: Which character did you like in the story—Prince Siddhartha or Prince Devadatta?
If you were Siddhartha, what would you have done?
Share a few occasions when you helped others.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Understanding Shaucha
concept: The importance of cleanliness.
explanation: Shaucha means keeping our body, mind, and surroundings clean. We should wash our hands, brush our teeth, and be polite to everyone. 🧼✨

practice_questions.q: What are some ways you can keep your body clean?
How can we keep our mind clean?
practice_questions.a: By speaking kind words and being polite.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Laddoos in the Fair
concept: The consequences of not practicing cleanliness.
explanation: Asha loved sweets and ate a laddoo without washing her hands, which made her sick. This story teaches us the importance of washing hands before eating. 🍬🤒

practice_questions.q: When do you wash your hands?
practice_questions.a: Before and after eating, after playing, etc.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Hand Washing Activity
concept: Practicing good hygiene.
explanation: Tick the activities that you do every day to keep clean. 🧼✋

subject: physicaleducation
title: Clean Hands are Safe Hands
concept: Encouraging cleanliness.
explanation: A fun rhyme to remember the importance of clean hands!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Practicing Good Habits
concept: Understanding good habits for health and well-being.
explanation: Good habits help us stay healthy and happy! 🌟 For example, washing hands before meals keeps germs away. 🧼

practice_questions.q: Why is it important to wash hands?
practice_questions.a: To keep germs away and stay healthy!

practice_questions.q: What should you do before sleeping?
practice_questions.a: Read a good story and sleep early!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Introduction to Yoga
concept: Learning about Asanas in Yoga.
explanation: Yoga helps us be strong and healthy! 💪 Let's mimic animals and nature with fun poses! 🐶🌳
examples: Flap your legs like a butterfly 🦋

practice_questions.q: Which yoga pose did you enjoy the most?
practice_questions.a: I liked pretending to be a butterfly!

practice_questions.q: What does yoga help us do?
practice_questions.a: It helps us energize our bodies!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Types of Asanas
concept: Understanding different types of yoga postures.
explanation: There are four types of Asanas: Standing, Sitting, Prone, and Supine. Each has a starting and relaxing position! 🧘‍♂️

practice_questions.q: What is the starting position of Tadasana?
What is the relaxing position of Dandasana?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Performing Asanas
concept: Practicing specific yoga poses.
explanation: Let's practice some fun yoga poses together! 🧘‍♀️ Each pose helps us feel relaxed and strong!

practice_questions.q: How do you perform Vrikshasana?
practice_questions.a: Stand on one leg and raise your arms!

practice_questions.q: What should you do in Sukhasana?
practice_questions.a: Sit comfortably with your back straight!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Vajrasana
concept: A seated yoga pose for relaxation.
explanation: In Vajrasana, you sit on your legs with your heels under your buttocks. It's great for calming the mind and improving digestion! 🌼
examples: Sit with your legs folded, heels under your buttocks.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Bhujangasana
concept: The Cobra Pose, which strengthens the back.
explanation: In Bhujangasana, you lie on your stomach and lift your chest up like a snake! 🐍 This helps to stretch your spine and open your chest.
examples: Keep your hands by your chest and lift your head up.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Pavanamuktasana
concept: The Wind-Relieving Pose, good for digestion.
explanation: In Pavanamuktasana, you hug your knees to your chest. This pose helps release gas and makes your tummy feel better! 🌬️
examples: Bend your knees and pull them towards your chin.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Breath Awareness
concept: Understanding how to be aware of your breath.
explanation: Breath awareness helps you notice how you breathe in and out. It's the first step to learning Pranayama! 🌬️
examples: Feel your breath with your finger below your nose.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Om Chanting
concept: Chanting 'Om' to calm the mind.
explanation: Chanting 'Om' helps you feel calm and activates your body. It's like a magic sound! ✨
examples: Inhale and chant 'A', then 'U', and finally 'M'.

practice_questions.q: Did you experience the vibrations in your body?
practice_questions.a: Yes, I felt them in different areas!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Keeping Eyes Healthy
concept: Exercises to relax and strengthen your eyes.
explanation: Doing eye exercises helps keep your eyes fresh and healthy! 👀 Let's take care of our eyes with some fun movements.
examples: Palming, moving eyeballs side to side, and rotating.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Movement of Eyeballs Up and Down
concept: Practicing eye movement to relax and strengthen eye muscles.
explanation: Let's move our eyeballs up and down! 😄 Close your eyes for a few seconds, then open them slowly. Move your eyes down and then up. Repeat this five times! After that, do some simple palming to relax your eyes. 👐

subject: physicaleducation
title: Diagonal Movement of Eyeballs
concept: Practicing diagonal eye movements for flexibility.
explanation: Now, let's move our eyes diagonally! 😃 Close your eyes, then open them. Move your eyes to the right corner up and left corner down. Do this three times. Then, move your eyes left up and right down, also three times. Finish with some palming! 👐

subject: physicaleducation
title: Rotation of Eyeballs
concept: Rotating eyeballs to improve eye coordination.
explanation: Let's make circles with our eyes! 🌟 Close your eyes, open them, and move your eyes to the right, down, left, and up, making a circle. Do this five times, then switch directions! Remember to relax with palming afterward. 👐

subject: physicaleducation
title: Memory Game
concept: Improving memory and awareness through a fun game.
explanation: Let's play a fun name game! 🎉 Sit in a circle and say your name along with the names of everyone before you. The first person starts, and each person adds their name. Can you remember all the names? 🤔

subject: physicaleducation
title: Counting Game
concept: Enhancing concentration through counting.
explanation: Let's count together! 🧮 Sit in a circle and count numbers. But when you reach a multiple of five, say 'Bharat' instead of the number! If you forget, you're out! Keep counting until everyone has had fun! 🎊

subject: physicaleducation
title: Follow the Teacher Game
concept: Improving coordination and listening skills.
explanation: Let's play Japa-Tapa! 🙌 Sit in a circle. When I say 'Japa', raise your hands, and when I say 'Tapa', put them down. If you don't follow, you're out! Can you keep up with me? 😄

subject: physicaleducation
title: Breath Control Game
concept: Enhancing breath control and voice culture.
explanation: Let's use our breath! 🌬️ Sit in a circle. One person takes a deep breath and runs around chanting 'Om'. When they finish, the next person takes their turn. Let's see how long we can keep it going! 🎶

subject: physicaleducation
title: Patanjali Says Game
concept: Following instructions in a fun game.
explanation: In this game, you listen carefully to the teacher. If the teacher says 'Patanjali says' before the instruction, you must follow it! If not, you must stay still. 🧘‍♂️

practice_questions.q: What do you do if the teacher doesn't say 'Patanjali says'?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Shabda Grahi Game
concept: Guessing who clapped in a circle.
explanation: In this game, one student stands in the middle with their eyes covered. When someone claps, they have to guess who it was! 🎉
examples: If you hear a clap, try to guess who it is!

practice_questions.q: How many chances does each student get to guess?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Sundar-Uttam Game
concept: Responding to the teacher's call.
explanation: In this fun game, the teacher says 'Sundar Sundar Sundar Hai' and you respond with 'Uttam Uttam Uttam Hai'. You have to match the number and tone! 🎶
examples: If the teacher says 'Sundar' three times, you say 'Uttam' three times!

practice_questions.q: What happens when the tone becomes very low?
practice_questions.a: The game ends with a loud clap!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Session Structure and Annual Plan
concept: Planning physical education sessions.
explanation: Physical education is important! You can have 90 hours of fun activities throughout the year. Plan your sessions to include games, yoga, and discussions! 📅
examples: You can have 5 periods of 40 minutes each week.

practice_questions.q: How many periods are suggested for physical education?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Importance of Warm-up and Cool-down
concept: Warm-up and cool-down are essential for safe physical activities.
explanation: Warm-up helps prepare our bodies for exercise by increasing heart rate and loosening joints. Cool-down helps lower heart rate and prevent muscle stiffness. 🏃‍♂️💪

practice_questions.q: Why is warm-up important?
practice_questions.a: It prepares our body for activity and reduces injury risk.

practice_questions.q: What does cool-down help with?
practice_questions.a: It helps relax the body and prevent muscle stiffness.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Structure of a Yoga Session
concept: A yoga session includes prayers, practices, and exercises.
explanation: Each yoga session starts with a prayer to bring peace and focus. Then, we practice different yoga poses and end with another prayer to reflect on our learning. 🧘‍♀️🙏

practice_questions.q: What do we do at the beginning of a yoga session?
Why do we end with a prayer?
practice_questions.a: To reflect on our day and reinforce positivity.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Opening Prayer
concept: A prayer for protection and nourishment.
explanation: This prayer helps us connect with our teacher and seek wisdom together. 🌟
text:
ॐ सह नााववतु
सह नाौ भुनाक्तु
सह वीर्यंं करवावहै

subject: physicaleducation
title: Closing Prayer
concept: A prayer for happiness and peace.
explanation: This prayer wishes for happiness and health for everyone. 🌈

subject: physicaleducation
title: Common Yoga Exercises
concept: Different exercises to include in yoga sessions.
explanation: These exercises help improve flexibility and strength. They can be customized based on age and fitness levels. 🧘‍♂️💪

practice_questions.q: What is one exercise you can do for warm-up?
Why should exercises be age-appropriate?

subject: physicaleducation
title: Introduction to Yoga
concept: Yoga helps us feel happy and healthy.
explanation: Yoga is a fun way to relax and feel good! 🌈 Let's learn how to breathe deeply and chant 'Om' to calm our minds.
examples: Chanting 'Om' while breathing in and out.

practice_questions.q: What does 'Om' mean?
practice_questions.a: 'Om' is a sound that helps us feel peaceful.

practice_questions.q: How does yoga make you feel?
practice_questions.a: Yoga makes me feel happy and relaxed!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Ahimsa Story and Activity
concept: Ahimsa means non-violence and kindness.
explanation: Ahimsa teaches us to be kind to everyone, including ourselves! 💖 Let's hear a story about being kind and do a fun activity.
examples: Helping a friend when they fall.

practice_questions.q: What is Ahimsa?
practice_questions.a: Ahimsa means being kind and not hurting others.

practice_questions.q: Can you give an example of being kind?
practice_questions.a: I can share my toys with my friends!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Good Eating Habits
concept: Eating healthy foods helps us grow strong.
explanation: Eating fruits and vegetables makes our bodies happy and healthy! 🍏🥦 Let's learn about good eating habits.
examples: Eating an apple instead of candy.

practice_questions.q: What is a healthy snack?
practice_questions.a: A healthy snack is something like fruit or yogurt.

practice_questions.q: Why is it important to eat vegetables?
practice_questions.a: Vegetables help us stay strong and healthy!

subject: physicaleducation
title: Eye Exercises for Relaxation
concept: Eye exercises help keep our eyes healthy.
explanation: Just like our bodies need exercise, our eyes do too! 👀 Let's practice some fun eye exercises to relax our eyes.
examples: Looking at something far away and then close up.

practice_questions.q: Why do we do eye exercises?
practice_questions.a: We do eye exercises to keep our eyes strong and healthy.

practice_questions.q: What is one eye exercise you can do?
practice_questions.a: I can look at a distant tree and then at my book.

subject: physicaleducation
title: Introduction to Asanas
concept: Asanas are yoga poses that help our bodies.
explanation: Asanas are fun yoga poses that help us stretch and stay fit! 🧘‍♀️ Let's learn some new poses together.
